poem1_content:
Part 1

I Recite This Tale,
To Assure You, The One So Sublime.
By The Way Of The Quill And The Ink, It's Time. 
That I Tell You, Why I Call You Mine.

In The Middle Of Somewhere,
It Was Twenty-sixteen. 
The Air Was Festive, The Grass Was Green,
And There I Was, A Distracted Teen.

Trying To Have A Bash, 
We Were Running Around.
With A Dozen Issues In Our Homes,
Waiting To Be Found.

And How I Felt That Day, 
Only I'd Be Able To Tell. 
Only I'd Be Able To Comprehend, 
How Hard For You I Fell.

I Was A Dreamy Guy, 
Seeing Nothing Else To Do. 
I Stood There, Dumbfounded, 
With My Eyes Following You.

My Sight Fixated, My Gaze So Sure, 
Ever Since, It Was All I Craved To See. 
Ever Since, I Couldn't Imagine Myself Alone,
And Only Yours, I Wanted To Be.

In Not The Slightest Way, I Assure You, 
Am I Exaggerating. 
Sounding So Grand, Is Still A Small Expression, 
Of What My Heart Is Stating.

Part 2

As Time Flew By,
My Feelings Became Stronger
All I Wished Was 
For You To Stay A Bit Longer

Oh. I Still Remember The Time, When I Returned Home At Nine.
I Was Getting Scolded, But It All Seemed So Still. 
How Could I Explain, the Endless Attraction, 
That Holds Me Vigorously, Against My Will.

As Situations Separated Me From You,
I Moved To Another Place.
Losing Touch, I thought I'd Never See You Again. 
I Still Hoped On My Emotions, Expecting A Familiar Face.

Six Years Later. When I Least Thought.
I Saw You Again.
"oh, She's Still The Same",
I Calmly Told My Friend.

Of The Remaining Days I Had To See You,
Before I'd Go For A Long Time.
Searching Anxiously, I Wanted One More Look. 
From Above, I Wished One Sure Sign.

Part 3

On The Last Day. I Finally Saw,
How Sudden, It Brings Me Awe,
For The Next 2 Hours, We Walked "aimlessly",
And In You, I Still Couldn't Find A Flaw.

It Pains Me To See What You Went Through,
But Treat You Better, I Must. 
Why Do You Doubt Yourself Like So? 
When I So Blindly Trust?

And Even If, You Break My Heart,
Break It A Million Fold. 
It Won't Change Who I Want, 
It's You I Want To Hold.

Like A Hopeful One, I Will,
Pick Up All Those Pieces, And Brew. 
Brew It Into A Beautiful Heart Again, 
And Give It To You.

poem1_theme: Ache of love and loss experienced in absence, and the inevitability of letting go.
poem1_content:
A rose quivers in a room that forgot its doors.
Dandelion seeds drift through the hush,
small white ghosts of things that never stayed.
Somewhere, a melody burns without a singer—
a hush that hums, a flame that does not flare,
only smoulders in the marrow of memory.
Petals loosen.
Not falling—
withdrawing.
Even the soil seems to listen
as crimson sighs into its waiting dark.
There is a serenity in this ruin,
an opulent ache in being unkept.
Nothing here is held.
Nothing survives being named.
Love moves like smoke through vacant rooms,
leaving no shape, only scent—
a faint, unbroken music
that settles in the chest
and refuses to go.
From such quiet wreckage
a softer grief will bloom,
unclaimed, untethered,
tender enough to wound.

I look at you like you look at your guitar,
With quiet love, both near and far,
And just for a heartbeat, I swear I knew,
You looked at me the way I look at the stars too.
You shook my hand like we’re just old friends,
But held it longer than the moment intends,
A second too long, a hush in the air,
Like you hoped I'd notice that you care.
You say we're friends, then hug me goodbye,
And my voice trembles with a silent why.
You whisper,"Maybe this isn’t the end",
And I almost forget you’re not more than a friend.
So maybe forever was never “till death,”
Maybe forever was just a borrowed breath,
Just one soft morning, one final sigh,
A lingering, lovely, maybe goodbye.

I still remember 
My reason to write

It was you
You!
Who filled me with joy
You!
Who in my eyes
Shined so very bright 
Like a star in the sky
The one I yearned for
In my darkest hours

I miss you
You!
Whom I could never call mine
But
Whom I think of
From time to time
Every once in a while

I loved you
You!
For whom,
I threw away my dignity 
It wasn’t important 
If it meant I could see you
Even for a moment distantly.

I dream of you
No sooner you become a distant memory 
Reigniting my feelings for you
And there I am again
Crumpling 
For you

I lament you
For I lost you
The moment you knew
What I felt for you
I couldn’t hide it
And the cost..?
Our Friendship..

I don’t regret you
Just sorrow
That I never knew you
I loved you
But what if…
For the image I had of you…?

For you
I wrote through 
This belongs to you
 I am now letting go
 I  do hope you know
I once really did cherish thou

poem1_theme: Existence was your crime, and life was your punishment.
poem1_content:
So, the court was all set. Mr. Copper was on the balustrade, his attorney, Jonathon Hale, standing next to him. 
Sitting in the crowd was Mrs. Parker. His attorney, Marcus Whitman, was standing there. Her eyes were filled with sorrow, as if she had lost a diamond of her own, which apparently was the case. 
The judge, Marie Brooks, was in her seat, waiting to hear the case and declare a judgment, which was a death sentence by all the means in this court.
The court started, “Now, let me hear the case, Mr. Whitman, you can explain to the whole court and people sitting here, what horrible act this person has done.” The judge declared.
Whit came forward, giving Mr. Copper a look as if he were a school bully. He set his suit, smirked at him again, and then started, “Good morning to all the people present in this honorable court, today. I’m here to get this demon, a death sentence for the crime he committed. He killed a boy, a child 7 year old, Noah Parker. There sits her mother, in sorrow, sobbing, dying from the inside. He took her only precious thing she had, her son, a piece of her heart; he poisoned his food. He was the chef in the restaurant; he was the one who made the dish, which had poison in it, which killed him slowly. He tried that once before, where that was decided that if Mr. Copper did such a thing again, he would be hanged! I demand nothing but Justice for that weeping mother!”
“Objection, you honor!” 
“Objection overruled!”
Marcus clenched his jaw. “This demon doesn’t even deserve a trial; he should be hanged at first!” The judge cried.
“I want to call a witness, Noah’s mother herself, Mrs. Parker.”
“Okay!”
Her mother started sobbing as she was standing. “Please, control yourself.” Whitman whispered to her. “Okay, as a mother, I can understand.” the judge said, “The court will take a ten-minute recess to allow the witness to prepare.”
—————————————————————————————————————————
In the support group, a person entered the room. He was Steven, his eyes were dark, he looked soulless, like the only thing he could’ve done right now is die, like the group is just his last chance.
 “Hi, sit!” The old-looking person said, Steven sat in front of him, looking like he had nothing but grief, endless sorrow.
“Introduce yourself!” the old man said, with a hopeful smile on his face.
“I’m Steven, 20 years old.”
“That’s great, son! What happened? Why are you sad?” he asked 
Steven took a look at them. 5 middle-aged people were sitting there.
“Is it a girl?” One asked.
“Yeah!” Steven replied, looking awkward.
“You can share everything, boy! We are not gonna judge you!” One said, other nodded along.
“So, so it’s a long story, yes, a long, terrible story.
 When I was a child, there was a boy. A bully. Always there, always there, shadowing me, laughing at me, breaking me
 Because of him, I  never had a life. A social life. Not until I was fifteen. Fifteen years old, and I was alone.
My mother died four years ago. Four years and I was left with nothing.
 I worked, yes, I worked in a small, cheap, low-wage, long hours
 And somehow my life began, maybe, maybe just a little better.
School, I hated school. I was afraid of everyone, afraid of the teachers
 Particularly, Mrs. Brooks’ history, her voice sharp and piercing
 She was, she was his mother. The bully’s mother. Can you imagine?
 Every lesson, every look a reminder, a way, a knife.
Then seventeen years old, and I don’t know why I started going regularly.
 Perhaps hope? Foolish hope or just stubbornness.
 Things they changed, yes. The bully is finally gone.
 But the teacher still poked and prodded
 And yet I had friends. A girl talked to me. I thought she liked me
But there was another bully
 He liked her. And he hated me for it.
 I told, I believed in a friend, and he betrayed me.
 Spilled it all, every thought, every hope
 The words the poison got to her first.
She never spoke to me again.
 Not a word, not a glance, nothing.
 And the bully threatened everyone, anyone who might speak to me.
 I was completely alone.
I cried, oh, I wept
 Over a stupid crush.
 Destroyed everything I had
 For nothing at all.
And I had nobody.
 No one to hear, no one to care, nobody.”“Son, Things like this happen, but you stood hard and here for 3 years, now it’s history, forget it.” The old man said.
“That’s the biggest mistake I’ve ever made; it will cost me my life!”
—————————————————————————————————————————
The court was all set again, everyone in their position, “How’s your girlfriend?” Haye asked, “You know my mother was the same as her. I wasn’t meant to do it, but it just happened.” Mr. Copper replied. 
“But it was a crime, you already harmed that boy once, and now.”
“I, I said already, it’s a human emotion, I know I’m not getting anywhere with this, until a miracle pounds the door.” Mr. Copper said, looking directly towards the ground, shaking his head in denial.
“You know that, I’m just a formality, it’s a death sentence, you will not even have a call for it, no one does here, they’re gonna hang u the next minute, the statements end.” Marcus cried. 
“Yeah!” Mr. Copper replied, now looking towards the courtroom door, “Where’s the judge?”
“Running late, I guess.” Marcus shrugged.

—————————————————————————————————————————
“Cost your life? What do you mean?” The old man asked.
“Nothing, you know, after that, I was in my home, stuck there, I wasn’t able to go out, work, and I sold my belongings to live, but I liked it again, I fucked up again, there was a girl, I wanted her, I want her so much, again, again, so much.

 The day I was about to express my feelings for her, 
The same day, I texted her, Hi, hi, just a hi, 
She didn’t reply to me for a bit, then she texted me, 
said she liked someone else, she told me she didn’t, but she lied. 

She texted me that the guy asked her from the front.
I, I cried, I didn’t reply to her. I was alone, again and again.
I wept again, then I remembered the judgment of The Court.
It was a death sentence; I killed myself, just for a crush,
to be honest, I like her even now, and that became my biggest sin.
I committed a crime, and I got the punishment.
You know, existence is my crime and life is my punishment.”

—————————————————————————————————————————Mrs. Parker was on the balustrade, “Are you alright now?” The judge asked. “Yes! My honor.” 
“You should start, so we can sentence this demon to death.”
“My honor! This, here is the person who… who killed my son, Poisoned him.
My beloved son was killed by this person here!” She cried, “He nearly killed my son 3 years ago, and this time he put poison in my Noah, to kill, yeah, kill!”
“The disaster doesn’t discriminate!” Mr. Copper screams in the middle.
“Oh, so you accept that was a disaster?” Whitman asked.
“You didn’t change a bit, motherfucker!” Mr. Copper said with an odd disgust as if he really was his bully.
We see Mrs. Books, her eyes were full of red wrath. “Order, order, order!”

—————————————————————————————————————————
“Oh, son, a girl is not everything!” The old man said.
“They will not understand!” Steven cried.
“You know, the same things happened with me, happy days will come.” One middle-aged person spoke.
“You know, I heard this Punjabi saying of a saint, that I don’t even want happiness because of sorrow, I wish for happiness, but sorrow comes with that too, but pain comes with it too, if you ask for a coin from anyone, and tell them to give you one side, and keep the other side with them? you must keep both the sides with must keep both the sides in your pocket. How can it be only one side? Both must be kept. 
                                                                                   -Sant Maskin Singh,” said the old man.
“But I never get the happiness, only sorrow comes!” 

—————————————————————————————————————————

“Don’t do it, Son, you know you are not getting away with this.” Mrs. Parker said, with a low, defeated voice.
Mr. Copper flinched.
“Order! Order! Maintain the order of the honorable court!” The judge screamed.

—————————————————————————————————————————

“I never even feel this feeling called happiness! My father left me when I was small! 
My mother hated him. She even changed her surname to Parker!” Steven said, crying now, “She used to call me little Noah.”

—————————————————————————————————————————
“This court had decided the fate of this demon!” The judge declared, Mr. Steven Copper is sentenced to death by hanging!” She signed the paper and broke the tip of the fountain pen. 
They were taking Copper to the back room, in the middle, he stops there, looks into Mrs. Parker’s eyes, and said, “I’m sorry, mother.” In a defeated voice.
—————————————————————————————————————————
“My mother gave me the name Noah, but my surname Cooper was given by my fucker father! And that little Noah or big Steven is going to die because of my silly second crush!”
Steven cried.

—————————————————————————————————————————
He was standing on the platform, the noose was in front of him, “Do you have any last words?” The father asked. “I killed him!” Mr. Copper said in a defeated voice.
“Killed who?” The father asked.
“I killed Noah!” Mr. Copper finally screamed.

Then his eye opened, and he was standing on a stool, the nose was around his neck, the window below him showed the park, and it was dark.
Someone was pounding on the door, and it looked like he was doing it for too long now.
Noah got off the stool, tried to hide everything, thinking, “Who is it at this hour?”
“I’m Ryan. They told me this was my dorm, and you are my partner, I guess.” The person standing out said, as if he had read Steven’s mind. 
Steven opened the door, there he was, a man dressed in a whole black coat-pant, looking like a mafia, his face wasn’t very visible, he was white, the same height as Steven, “Hi! I’m -”
“I know!” Steven said, thinking Why is he here? 
“I’m your roommate!”
“Let me prepare some space for you, bring your stuff into the room,” said Steven, and went into the room again. We see a glimpse of Ryan’s face, who was identical to Steven with a smug smile on his face. When Steven came back, he saw there was no one, no stuff, no Ryan. He got shocked, he closed his door, shivering. 
     
                                                       ...

Usse kya pta ki koi usko itni shiddat se chahta hai,
Usse kya pta ki koi usko apni palkon pr iss qadar sajata hai.
Usse kya pta ki koi usko apni har shayari ka lafz banata hai,
Usse kya pta ki koi usko apni mohobbat ki vajah btata hai.
Usse kya pta ki koi usko apne dil mein sajata hai,
Usse kya pta ki koi usko khuda ka darza de kar namaz padhne lag jaata hai,
Ki kahin taqdeer mein kucch reh gya ho,
Toh uske aane se pura ho jaaye,
Iss dil mein uski mohabbat ka zehar fell gya ho,
Toh ye jism bhi fakir ho jaaye.
Taqdeer mein na sahi mere dil ke andar ki tasveer ho jaye,
Iss jahan mein nhi toh dusre jahan mein kahin wo mera ho jaye.
Mera rab Mera peer sab wo ho jaye,
Mera zarf khoye aur iss jism ki aakhri saans bhi uske naam ho jaaye.
Usse kya pta ki koi uska intezaar karte karte apna dum kho betha tha,
Usse kya pta ki koi usse ishq ka naam de kar asal mein usse ibaadat kar betha tha...

Today your remembrance affected me,  
Many years ago, 
My pages were wet with my ink,  
And by your tears, 
Had left a mark of your love. 

When I curved those pages, 
Your love of tears and ink of my words, 
Filled those pages, 
I had waited for all my loved ones, 
To find you I had looked in all directions. 
  
Time was out of my hands,
Your hands were handcuffed,
With an unknown man, 
I was helpless, 
I lost strength. 
 
My love can't be judged,
With no tears I congratulated you,  
After our last meet, 
I had a minor ache in my heart, 
Couldn’t turn back to save my heart. 
 
Your love made me so grave in life, 
To see  and feel you from faraway, 
I left you for whole life, 
Many things are still incomplete in life, 
We shall meet again. 
 
My life's last pages are empty and dry, 
The things which are needed, 
My ink and your love of tears, 
To complete those pages, 
To end our book of life.

I was drenched in something .
I was loving the feeling.
I was getting a similar gaze right above me .
The cupid’s telling me it’s from my eyes
The way I saw it,
The way I imagine it,
I was loving it a lot 
I can’t bear losing it one more time .
Seeing that same face lost again ,
Made me uncomfortable ,
Feel like I’m about to go blind.
I was drenched in the thought of thinking
Is this the last phase of my life ?
The final phase.

poem1_title: Too hard to Love or to easy to Leave
poem1_content:
Was I too hard to love?
Or was I too easy to leave?
I don't know which one hurts more,
But honestly I knew that happiness wasn't actually for free...

Was I too strong?
Too strong that I crushed your dreams?
Or was I too weak?
I bet that's why I couldn't even save my dreams...

Was I too loud?
Too loud to hear your cries?
Or was I too quiet?
I bet that's why I didn't even get a goodbye...

Was I too rough?
Too rough to take care of your delicate heart?
Or was I too gentle?
I bet that's why it was so easy for you to break my heart...

Was I too hard to love?
Or was I too easy to leave?
I don't know which one hurts more,
But honestly I knew that happiness wasn't actually for free...

Was I too Kind?
Was I too Inconsiderate?
Was I too Naive?
Was I too Jaded?
Was I too Absent?
Or was I too Lifeless?

Just tell me what was my fault,
I promise you I tried my best...
Was I too hard to love?
Or was I too easy to leave?

मैं आज़ाद हूँ, मुझे आज़ादी है               
सबसे पहले जग कर, सीधे चौके में आने की,
सुबह की पहली चाय के साथ सबकी आँखें खुले
इसकी व्यवस्था बनाने की,
सबको नींद से जगा कर
समय पर स्कूल, दफ़्तर, दुकान पहुँचाने की।

मैं आजाद हूँ, मुझे आज़ादी है
परदे में रह कर भी, नंगी आँखों से घूरे जाने की,
बसों और रेलों में
वक्ष और पुट्ठे पर हाथ खाने की,
सड़क पर चलते समय
मर्दों के भद्दे तानों की।

मैं आज़ाद हूँ, मुझे आज़ादी है
माँ के गर्भ में ही मार दिए जाने की,
दहेज की चिता में
जिंदा जलाए जाने की,
खुद में कोई कमी नहीं
फिर भी बाँझ कहलाने की।

मैं आज़ाद हूँ !
यह कथन नहीं, प्रश्न होना चाहिए
क्या मैं आज़ाद हूँ ?

मैं कौन हूँ ?
मैं नारी हूँ
ब्रह्मा की कृति न्यारी हूँ
पिता की दुलारी हूँ, पति की प्यारी हूँ
युग प्रवर्तक अवतारी हूँ
फिर भी आज़ादी की मारी हूँ।
poem2_content:
नव युग का सूर्य उदय हुआ 
नारी अब सौगात नहीं।
सूद समेत है वापस करती 
सहती कोई आघात नहीं।

नारी अगर आ जाए अपने पर 
रोक सके उसको, ऐसी कोई झंझावात नहीं 
वो सह लेती है, तो शोषित है 
वरना उसका शोषण किसी की औकात नहीं।

फूलों सी वो लगती कोमल 
पर दुर्बल उसकी गात नहीं 
पाषाणों सा दृढ़ उसका संकल्प 
दे सकता उसको कोई मात नहीं।

कोमल हृदय, ममता की मूर्ति 
पर शत्रु से कोई जज़्बात नहीं 
जल, थल, नभ हो या ब्रह्माण्ड
क्या कोई जगह जहाँ उसकी बिसात नहीं। 

नारी शक्ति की सूत्रधार है 
बिन उसके सृष्टि की होगी शुरुआत नहीं 
देवों ने जिसको अपने अस्त्र दिए 
उसको अबला कहने की बात नहीं।

poem1_content:
White Flowers 🤍

I do not know how many years must pass, 
before I lose the memory of your face—
each minute spent with you exceeds the grace 
of any verse I'll ever write.

I set these words upon this quiet page, 
yet ink cannot contain what you possess:
a beauty deeper than my lines will ever confess, 
more radiant than all my borrowed language.

I could have fallen blind and still have known—
the shape of you,
the way your laughter sounds,
the gentle pull that draws me to your soul.

As my eyes sink deeper and deeper,
Into the abyss of your soul
I hope that you accept,  
as I give You, Your white flowers, whole.

What bloomed between us,
ripened into need 
not hunger of the flesh, 
though flesh is true, 
but I desire something vaster, 
YOU-

Your voice, your presence, each unspoken creed.
I crave my hours simply at your side, 
to hear the symphony your being makes, 
to witness joy for its own gentle sake, 
to dwell within the warmth you cannot hide.

But seasons turn, and even fire must wane; 
what once blazed bright, now only flickers. 
Still, you remain a home within my chest, 
I hope I cross your heart through joy or pain, 
for you have never left the halls of mine, 
as I lay down these white flowers to rest.

One day, perhaps, you'll understand what waited for you
But I fear when that day will arrive,
this heart will burn and all that's left behind
is the radiance of your being.

One day, with your permission—

I will kiss your gentle hands, caress each line 
that grace has drawn upon your rosy face, 
adorn your golden locks, and find my place 
beside the soul I long to truly call mine. 
I will learn the shade of your lips,
the curve those hips form, 
delve deep into your fragrance and your truth, 
measure my spirit's fabric against yours.
The greatest gift that I can bestow you with, 
Is this love I hold, unapologetic, pure, 
I offer You, Your white flowers, at your door.

Until that day, if ever it should come,
I honour both your distance and your choice.
The most I give is what I cannot keep: 
my love, which asks for nothing but your voice to say, 
when you are ready, if you are ready—
I may bring You, Your white flowers.

poem1_title: My Thoughts Finally Took a Break
poem1_content:
It's been a while, I was sitting under a tree, at fall. 
Mostly brown leaves laying on the ground and few attached to the thin stems and branches. 
The lake beside me was still and I could see myself, clear and clean. 

And as I admired the reflection of the drowning sun, a little drop escaped my eyes. 
"Have I not loved enough?" I said, calling my memories back and forth, I flipped through the pages of my past. 
Even though I could not remember anything as such, I frowned when I saw his name. 

A misfortune, caused a while ago, now I'm sitting beside a lake under a tree, a place unknown. 
How did I get here, where am I from? It's an unsolved mystery, I've yet to unfold. 
The book in my hand gave me all, information and directions, till my fall. 

All I remember was his name, and his eyes, the way he talked and passed me by. 
The book told me a story of how you met the man and you loved him, again and again. 
And now with my memories lost, the only souvenir is this book, it holds the moments that my brain could not hold anymore. 

I feel the love, the love and the love, growing within me, unstopped, into a beautiful woman. 
Known for her grace and elegance, her eyes filled with evidence.
But as the book says it all, it must have been a fall. 

So after I read this book, I will close it with tears.
And return it back to its shelf, where it belongs.
Suddenly, a little drop formed ripples along the lake. 

My thoughts finally took a break.
poem2_content:
If my love could ever escape, 
This self-constructed wall of fear.
In the presence of doubt and anxiety,
While 
          Everything 
                            Falls 
                                    Apart.
I hope it finds you, holds you,
In ways that I could not.
In breeze, in scent, in memories,
I hope the subtlety of my love cherishes you.
I hope my love flows and lingers around,
Just enough till you're found.
I hope it answers the strand of hair, 
wanting to be tugged behind your ear.
I hope it listens to you,
Whenever you need someone to hear.
Thought my love alone was never enough,
I hope it was complete.
I love you,
                      despite 
                how
              i
     failed 
you.

poem1_theme: Timeless love and the beauty of belonging together while remaining unique.
poem1_content:
For the next life,
I wish you and me
to be different flowers,
but in the same garden.

There would be flowers galore,
but you will be the one
whom I will adore.
As for me, you will be my core.

When somebody brings water
and let it pour,
the fragrance of love will
gently outpour.

I will see you,
From dawn till dusk.
when the wind blows,
I will hug you as a passing whiff.

and together we will become,
the charm of the garden.

poem1_title: Of All the Ways to Lose a Soul
poem1_theme: Heartbreak, Loss and Grief, Emotional loss, mourning, change
poem1_content:
Of all the ways to lose a soul, 
Death is the kindest, the only whole. 

You can lose them to time, so sly, 
Days turn to months, and months just fly. 
Conversations fade, the laughter dies, 
You’re left with ghosts, but no goodbyes. 

You can lose them to love, to someone new, 
They’re smiling at you, but their heart isn’t true. 
You replay their words, you analyze, 
And find yourself trapped in hopeful lies. 

You can lose them to distance, not miles but thought, 
They’re right beside you, yet never caught. 
Their eyes look through you, their hands feel cold, 
And suddenly warmth is just a story you’re told. 

You can lose them to change, slow and unseen, 
They’re alive, they’re present, but not what they’ve been. 
You reach for their hand, it slips through the seam, 
They’re someone else now, someone out of your dream. 

Death does not linger, Death does not deceive, 
it comes, it takes, and it lets you grieve. 
It gives shape to sorrow, and space to mourn, 
it lets you close doors that life has torn. 

And so, Of all the ways the world can part, 
Death is the kindest to the aching heart.

I envy those cheeks of that person,
which were cherished by your lips, my love.
What am I thinking?
I don’t know about anything.
Am I sad? Or angry?
Or do I want revenge for what you did to me?
Or should I just give up on everything and move on?
I don’t know about anything.
For me, you were my world,
and you will be—
until my last words in this world.
poem2_title: A PLACE WHERE YOU FIND PEACE
poem2_content:
Have you ever crossed your paths with a person who always folds arms, even when they are surrounded by people? In this time, they are just controlling their urge to be in someone’s arms, and melt little by little, piece by piece, till the end of the dawn.
They are not weak or frail; they want someone—someone who knows what’s there in their mind. But the irony is that they always seem to be as quiet as the ocean bed seen from above, but oftentimes they are the most violent swirl you will ever see…

poem1_theme: The quiet ache of distance and the gentle acceptance that follows love without closure.
poem1_content:
Winter taught me how space looks.

It looks like breath turning white before it reaches someone else.

It looks like roads that remember footsteps, but forget the sound of your voice.

We stopped speaking— not suddenly, not cruelly, but the way snow falls: soft, until the world realizes
it has changed.

Your name stayed with me. Not loud. Not asking. Just resting in the quiet places of the day.

Evenings came early, pulling the light away as if the sun, too, understood restraint.

I won’t call it heartbreak. There was no breaking, only distance doing what it does best— teaching patience, teaching silence, teaching how to miss someone without reaching for them.

Winter moved on. It always does.

But it left me this: some people are not lost— they are simply kept far enough to be felt forever.

All whom I  know  or  not  
Are my  children  And for them
My heart is pounding, my quench is fulfilled, 
My Soul is at peace. 

I accepted all sufferings
Borrowed from them
Whom I know or not,
For that my thoughts are resonating, 
My Soul is at Peace.

The melancholy I saw of them
Could cause a bit for me,
For my know
Those are my creations
For that 
My Soul is at peace. 

If the world is a tragedy for me
Be sure,
I feel it, I cry for it ,
I die for it.

If I think the society I live in
It would be a great joke for me
If I would not feel  or think
My soul is at peace. 

I find the worth in you,
You find my tears,
Is this not the DIVINE META LOVE?
I love you all
Not because I trust you 
But I see Nonviolence in your eyes.
You may not hear me
I being so loud
Because the world has not been 
A scaffold but a terrain of mud.
I know  still I love you
Not gone so far
For the sake the things are so bad
Not that you are.

The warmth you burned me in,
that’s what will keep me alive,
even if my body freezes at -1.

The way you hugged me so tight,
that’s where I can finally breathe,
even if it bruises my lungs.

The way you shined so bright,
that’s where my world turns clear,
even if your light draws tears.

The way you said my name,
that’s the rhythm my heart will keep,
even if it bleeds in its sleep.

The way you live in my thoughts,
that’s where I find you near,
even if the world won't bring you here.

The love you drowned me in,
that’s where my soul will stay peacefully dead,
even if my body decays far away in distance.

poem1_theme: Love, and longing for a lover who's no longer alive. Love, lost and healing.
poem1_content:
Sonderlust

Learning pain,
before I learnt patience.
My tender, old soul in this world
showed compassionate forbearance.
Every day, I stood in front of my
mirror-stranger.
I stared not in emptiness,
but with remembrance.

For hours and a godly amount of time,
I was lost.
But you weren’t there to hold me.
I fell and fell, my love.
I kept falling until I couldn’t.
This almost ghostly, cold night,
and the floor—frost.
I was relieved I couldn’t fall
anymore.
That dim, warm light
made your face look
ash-soft.

My aching heart is torn apart.
I saw you in flames
before I could hold your hand.
But when you were here,
my love, you were my muse
the one I scattered myself into,
the starry lands.
Without you, I haunt the
same streets.
I keep returning to a place that
no longer exists.
The winter I loved so much
was gone,
as if your footsteps in sand.
You are now a mystery,
a sonderlust.

I found your unfinished
letters, and you called
me your “little one.”
Those memories are somehow
very distant, loving memory. 
With you I was happy.
But now my soul is hurt.
I’m in love with your life,
which I cannot touch,
even when you’re gone.
My lil sun...
I’ve learnt to live again,
or I think so.
I wanted you here with me,
but I’m moving now.
I’ll smile again, but
at what cost?
My Sonderlust

And beside the ashes
There was a foot
That sat on a chair
Forgetting its roots.

And it didn’t shout
Despite the calls its master made around,
Cold waves shimmering,
Shaking the man’s ground.

And all he held were memories,
Because those letters were burnt to wipe them out,
As if removing stains from cutlery.

One fine day he dreamt of a life,
A groom and a bride,
The night was so bright,
She’d become his wife.

But the day didn’t meet the night.
Some named it misfortune, some cold tides.
She had to go to a better place,
A truth so shivering he never thought he’d face.

And the ashes that moulded her letters
Were never able to help.
He tried forgetting things,
Looking for warmth
But miserably failed.

And till now, after seven winters have passed by,
Everything moved, but
Those moments behind the blanket of love stayed.
It rained snow-white,
Leaving everything there, right.
Shsh

poem1_title: I've raised a kid ever since I was three
poem1_theme: The poem is about the unresolved grief that follows after you split paths with your younger sibling, the ones you've loved like your own kid. And are supposed to figure out your separate lives without having the other by your side.
poem1_content:
I've raised a kid ever since I was three, 
And now he's a part of me I wouldn't ever wish to undo

I realised a little too late
that I liked my bread half-stolen
And my chocolates unevenly shared.
That I liked staying up till four in the morning,
talking about school, sharing gossip
about the times we pretended not to care.

I didn’t notice when his shoes grew bigger than mine.
I didn’t notice when he stopped being small enough
for me to hide
In my head, he’s still that little boy
wind in his feet, tears ready to spill from his eyes.

I miss, in ways I can’t explain
the space beside me
where his noise lived like it had been born with me.

Now all I see are the empty spaces, 
Quiet, stretched-out places
holding its breath, waiting, unaware of how to feel.
How do I pack twenty years of us into a bag
and walk ahead with mine
As if it didn’t leave me a little scarred
to watch you go and figure out your own life.

poem1_theme: Inner chaos, unhealed wounds, emotional reality, heartbreak, caution
poem1_content:
Active defences when the chaos sets in,

Coming in batches, these unhealed stitches.

The loud noise and the unsettling within

Seems to get caught in the web of witches.


The shriek will lead

My ears to bleed

Along goes the soul

Falling deeper than I had hoped for.


Gullible similar patterns

Knock on my door of self-actualisation

These roads are known to be messy before it burns.

Flighting through to an unknown destinations.


So what would smarter people do?

Would they keep spells and wands handy?

Ward off the danger coming to you

Yet remember not to share the anxiety?


My careful steps to my peace stay

No loose rope, nothing to pull

Yet the witch wins on a bad day,

How can I be so sincere yet forgetful?

I thought love came like a storm—
Dramatic and wild.
Or perhaps it was like the rain—
Beautiful but fleeting.

Everything that starts will have an end.
And I believed in this destiny
That I was sent.
“This too shall pass, a time will come.”
I buried myself in that ideal thought.

But pictures last forever,
Even when nailed to the past.
They stare into your future
Though you hung them up to rot.

And that’s when I realised—
Love is not fleeting.
It is ever-present, like air.
You can never forget it.
It is embedded everywhere.

One sound. One look.
One smile was all it took.
One word. One oath.
And two of them were hooked.
Love isn’t a word. Nor a feeling.
Love is a promise. A step into home.

Two hearts, one soul.
Two beats for one tune.
And two beings to make it whole.

poem1_title: The Seat I Saved For Love
poem1_theme: Patience in love, emotional abandonment, and acceptance of unanswered longing
poem1_content:
Waiting for you to love me
took more of me
than I ever accounted for.

Days moved—
I called it patience.
Nights grew heavier,
and I named them hope.

Carrying the weight of maybe.
Staying,
even when leaving would have hurt less.

I kept a seat for you,
to watch the sunset with you,
to feel that love,
to fill that empty space—
a corner of my heart
where I imagined you arriving.

But instead,
I watched the sunset alone.

The waves came and went.
They touched the shore,
then pulled back again,
as if they knew
staying too long
only causes damage.

Petals fell beside me,
one by one,
fragile and unnoticed-
like pieces of my heart.

Still, I waited.
I waited to watch that sunset again,
this time with you.
I waited to feel chosen,
to feel that love return,
to feel your presence
fill the silence you left behind.

I waited to be with you-
to believe the empty seat
was only empty for now.

The chair I placed
with so much expectation
never moved.
It collected dust, memories,
and all the words I never said.

Now, it is only a memory.. 
of how deeply I loved,
of how long I stayed,
of how I learned that
sometimes love doesn’t come back,
no matter how patiently
you keep a seat for it.
poem2_theme: Self-choice, independence, and courage to follow one’s own path.
poem2_content:
I’m the penguin
who walked away, 
not because mountains are cool,
but because I wanted to. 

The winter wind whispered my name,
icy and soft,
like a gentle flame.

Snowflakes danced around my feet,
while the world said,
I didn’t belong to the heights, that my place was only ice and tide,
but I knew it is a choice, a feeling, a brave kind of pride.

 I felt a calling
deep in my chest, 
a quiet, wild heartbeat that wouldn’t rest.
So I stepped past the sea,
past the familiar blue,
into the mountain’s hush,
into the cold and the new.

I left the shoreline behind, 
where the world knew my name,
and I walked into the hush of snow,
 where no one could judge my flame.

The air was thin,
the nights were long,
but my soul found rhythm
in the winter’s song.


In the snow’s silence,
I learned to be free, 
the penguin who chose
his own destiny.

poem1_theme: The poem explores healing as a slow, compassionate journey where pain is not erased but understood, and strength grows through self-forgiveness, patience, and emotional honesty. The emotion moves through tenderness, quiet ache, introspection, and finally into gentle resilience, capturing how the heart learns to remain soft without breaking again.
poem1_content:
How the Heart Learns

Healing did not arrive
the day my heart felt light.
It came the day I stopped
asking my pain
to disappear overnight.

I let it sit beside me,
unnamed, unafraid,
no longer a burden
I tried to evade.
Some wounds do not ask
to be erased
they ask to be held
with patience and grace.

Not everything broken
longs to be fixed.
Some things need stillness,
some time to exist.
Some need silence
instead of advice,
some need to be left
without being made nice.

There were days when remembering
weighed more than rest,
nights when my thoughts
pressed hard on my chest.
Still, I stayed present,
still, I remained,
refusing to leave myself
alone with the pain.

I forgave the version of me
open and bare,
who loved without armour,
who hoped without care.
She was not foolish,
nor wrong, nor unwise
she was honest in heart,
and brave in her tries.

Healing taught me
what strength truly means:
not closing the door,
but choosing your seams.
Not shrinking your feelings
to fit someone’s space,
not dimming your truth
for another’s pace.

Healing means being understood, not by words,
but by silence that listens
to all that is unheard.

Now my heart moves gently,
aware yet free,
knowing when to open,
when simply to be.
It knows when to pause,
when to step away,
without bitterness,
without needing to stay.

I am not returning
to who I once was.
I am learning to honour
who I am because -
because I endured,
because I remained,
because I softened
without being changed.

And slowly, without notice,
my heart remembered its rhythm.
Not perfect, not fearless
just steady enough
to move forward
with tenderness
instead of fear.

That, too,
is how the heart learns.

In winter's cold and knife like wind,
I just loved and believed him like  blind.
I never wanted to fall in love,
but other plans his attractive eyes have.
Whenever, with love I looked at him
to me, has come an another feeling.
Sometimes, I wanted him to be deleted,
instead of this, in my mind he has just spread.
To remove my untold, one sided pain 
I just ask myself in every moment again and again,
if it was love or other thing like attachment .
My heart replied to do always enjoyment 
by looking at his eyes from far away 
or feeling his presence in every way.
Whenever I notice his shaking lips 
my heart wants to take a little sip.
Why did he steal my soft mind
When he will not give any loving sign.
Whenever little, little happiness he shares,
or he does any type of little cares,
I just lose myself properly to him
and in his love's sea, I just swim.
Till today, it's definitely an unsolved confusion 
if it's love for him or just an attraction.
Just like winter's snowfall, my heart is completely white
but I want to make it colourful by his sight.
He may come like wintry wind 
But I would like completely to bind
by my love's inseparable, strong rope
in little happiness and little hope.
I just want to bloom in his heart like winter's marigold 
He should be told when I will be bold.
May be, he is the best and favourite man 
Whom I never express how badly I just want.
It's okkk that after winter spring will come soon 
To make my heart with peace and beautiful blossoms.

தென்றலடி  நானுனக்கு
சுவாசமடி நீயெனக்கு!

கதிரொளியடி நானுனக்கு
காலைப்பரிதி நீயெனக்கு!

குளிரடி நானுனக்கு
வெண்மதி நீயெனக்கு!

மழைத்தூறல் நானுனக்கு
கார்மேகம் நீயெனக்கு!

விண்மீன் நானுனக்கு
வானமடி நீயெனக்கு!

சாளரமடி நானுனக்கு
உயிர்க்காற்றடி நீயெனக்கு!

இலையடி நானுனக்கு
கிளையடி நீயெனக்கு!

முள்ளடி நானுனக்கு
மலரிதழடி நீயெனக்கு!

கவிஞனடி நானுனக்கு
கவிதையடி நீயெனக்கு!

பாட்டடி நானுனக்கு
பாடும்பொருள் நீயெனக்கு!

உறவடி நானுனக்கு 
உயிரடி நீயெனக்கு!

தமிழ்ப்பித்தடி நானுனக்கு
காதலடி நீயெனக்கு!

A starry night moved in the light as
She stood there waiting for the
Right time to make a move on her
White prince, his face shining brightly as
She moved gently with grace, 
Holding her heart tightly, moving
Ever so lightly, but her breath 
Was interrupted by a girl 
In her 20's who shone brightly 
Like the sun, she made a run
For the slight turn towards
Her desires and dreams that lay 
At the train station, taking her
Past the horizon, to the mention 
Of her destination, 
She moved past her hesitation and
There was no mention of her past pain 
Which had reduced her 
Will for redemption,
Her salvation is way past her
Past pretentions, 
Her salvation is just on the horizon.
Her salvation is way past her
Past pretentions, 
Her salvation is just on the horizon.

The girl moved around with purpose,
There was a twirl in her braid,
As she shunned the stuff that held 
Her from getting laid with sensation,
She met with her desires in the oblivion,
She set her eyes on the bright canyon,
Overlooking the Sun basking in 
Her passion, an emotion
Beyond the ordinary and 
She moved to write her story,
Not touched by casual reciprocity
Of others, A gentle smile 
Followed her as she walked to
Fulfill her destiny
Her salvation is way past her
Past pretentions, 
Her salvation is just on the horizon.
poem2_content:
Ohh pretty lady ! Ohh yeah, why are you so full of mystery ;

I remember you sipping latte at a cafe,
Don't you wonder about the little gaffe’s
Your friends are making, girl !
You are original, shaking up the scene 
Of the cafe, the way you lean in the
Glittering lights, the nights are empty
Without your dreamy sights, the 
City moves to your dance beats
So groovy, you love diary but i still
Dunno your story
Ohh pretty lady ! 
Ohh yeah, why are you so full of mystery.
We met at the park when it was chilly and
A bit soggy, i bet you found it 
Stark and noisy, even though
Your name is Rosy, you smelled
As fresh as a daisy, I mailed you 
My thoughts, you didn't bothered,
I told you my feelings, you hundred 
With no fillings, but then
Fate was kind, I pushed my mind
Away from you and the timid rain
Showed up and brought a tad bit closer,
You realized i am your only lover.

Our story started with rejection,
I went into tension but
You became my infatuation,
You became my passion,
You became my obsession,
Ohh Yeahh ! 
Ohh Yeahh !
My only obsession,
Rosy, you are truly a sensation,
My only obsession.

poem1_content:
LOVE
What is love?

Four letters.
A word people romanticize like it’s safe.

When we hear love, we think of soft hands, sweet gestures, and someone choosing us. And we whisper, I want this.

I wanted it too.

But love never came to me gently.
For me, love is winter
water frozen so long
it forgets it was ever meant to flow.
They say heat can free it,
but fire and ice don’t survive each other.
One always disappears.

I don’t know when it began.
Maybe the day I became the eldest daughter.

My parents said they loved me.
But love meant responsibility.
Be mature.
Understand.
Don’t cry.

My siblings were allowed softness.
I was taught endurance.

Then people started leaving.
The girl I called my best friend
the one I begged not to abandon me
left without a goodbye.
Silence is cruel like that.
It doesn’t explain.
It just teaches.

My childhood best friend left too.
No reason.
No closure.
Just absence.

And slowly, quietly,
I learned the lesson everyone seemed determined to teach me:
love does not stay.
Love looks at me and walks away.

I tell people I don’t believe in love.
I tell them I don’t believe in marriage.
That is a lie.

It’s armor.

Every eldest daughter is the first lamb to slaughter
raised to bleed quietly,
taught that sacrifice is affection.

Still
somewhere deep inside this frozen place,
I wonder what would happen
if fire touched ice and didn’t destroy it.

They say love can happen
even at minus one degree.

I still believe this:
One day,
fire will not burn me.
Ice will not numb me.

One day,
I will be loved
without becoming less

I am still standing here,
waiting to see if that’s true.

poem1_theme: The theme of this piece is eternal love beyond memory, body, time and death. At its core, the poem explores the idea that true love does not belong only to the heart, the mind, or even memory — but it belongs to the soul itself.
poem1_content:
If I die ,
my heart may betray me- 
for its remembering only
as long as it beats. 
Only dies is the body – 
the part that needed breath..just to feel.

The heart will stop, yes
and with it memory may collapse,
like a clock breaking 
when time refuses to continue.

The soul is not built of time. 
It does not rote,
does not forget, 
does not need proofs to believe.

The body loves in moment,
the mind loves in reason –
but the soul loves
without asking why.

So when this life closes,
and my name dissolves into Earth,
my soul will still know you 
the way fire knows warmth,
the way night knows the moon
without ever touching .

Across the universe,
where stars are born only to collapse,
my soul will pass through eternity
carrying one familiar ache –
You.

I will not carry your name –
names belong to language. 
I will carry your essence,
the way the universe carries gravity 
without ever seeing it.

Across Galaxies collapsing into nothing, across birth that erase histories,
my soul will still lean 
toward the same direction – 
You.

I may be Born Again 
with different eyes, 
different hands,
a different story written on my skin. 
Yet somewhere deep –
beyond memory, 
beyond language,
beyond fate -- 
there will be a quite pull.

A knowing without memory
A longing Without Face.

But the moment my soul feels that ache again ,
it will bow --
as it has been met before.

because some loves
are not stored in the heart, 
they are carved into existence itself.

And now if even god tries to wipe my memory clean, even if worlds begin again, 
my soul will still Whisper-
    There was someone...
    loving them was the reason
    I learnt eternity.

That's why every lifetime I return
I won't search happiness
I will search for the feeling...
   Not to love again

But to remember you 
without knowing how.
poem2_theme: Love’s final form is not union — it is understanding. Not noise — but silence. Not possession — but peace. It portrays the moment when love stops trying to be chosen and learns to exist without being seen.
poem2_content:
How audacious lovers are -
They love with fearless fire,
giving dreams, sleep, dignity,
calling sacrifice desire.
          They bleed And call it devotion,
          mistake pain for proof,
          Believing love must be earned
          by being endlessly aloof.
But when fate does not agree,
And efforts failed to bind
their courage turns into silence 
their strength learns to resign.
           Fragile then, yet deeply wise,
          They stop asking for to love twice
          Accepting love not as possession 
          But as a scar- worn without question.

poem1_content:
We both met after ten years.
I was sitting in the balcony, a whisky glass resting loosely in my hand.
Inside, my group was chilling—laughing, dancing, moving to loud beats that felt distant to me.
The music was high, the lights were warm, but I stayed where the noise softened.
I wasn’t waiting for anyone.
At least, that’s what I told myself.
The distance of years between us—I don’t even know how to explain it.
It still felt like yesterday to me.
And then, suddenly, she came.
Not dramatically.
Not announced.
Just her silent presence.
She walked towards me, and I didn’t even realise at first.
It felt like the room shifted around me.
She started talking about our past, standing there, saying sorry, almost pleading in front of me.
I ignored her.
At least, that’s what I told myself.
But she kept talking.
She got emotional.
And I couldn’t handle that anymore.
That night, we talked till 4.
We forgave each other—for our mistakes, for everything that happened in our past.
We accepted what we were and what we failed to be.
Somewhere in between those conversations, we realised there was nothing left between us now.
Words felt less.
We didn’t even know who was wrong—her or me.
So we both blamed the situation, telling ourselves that maybe the timing was wrong.
But the sad truth was something else.
It was ego.
We both had a different level of ego,
and it slowly broke the healthy relationship we once had between us.
And then she said,
“Kaash uss waqt ek dusre ko samajh lete.”
That line hurt more than anything else.
That reunion remains one of the best memories of my life.
But something stayed back in that balcony—
the last moment where we were in the same room,
not knowing it would be the last time again

The eyes that told a story before it began,
The smile that holds every memory it can.
The beauty, the charm, the love that you carry,
Is something so rare, so tender, so wary.

How could I ever forget you?
When everything I am is filled with you.

The way you softly call my name,
The stolen glances when I try to see your face.
The love in your gaze speaks without a voice,
A feeling my soul cannot quite define.

I hold you in the quiet between my breath,
Where love lives softly, untouched by proof or depth.

I care for you in ways words can’t explain,
In silent prayers, in joy and in pain.
In every moment, both near and apart,
You live so gently inside my heart.

If only you could see yourself the way I do,
You’d know my heart has found its home in you.

WHEN YOU WHISPER
I love your eyes,
Big becomes bigger–
When you whisper.
I remember when you hug,
To express your love.
Close become closer–
When you whisper.
I love to have your head
On my arm,
When you sleep beside me–
I feel worm
Our lips become closer
When you whisper.
I pray to divine,
Please keep us fine–
I never want to be far
Oh my dear, as I love
When you whisper.

*ढाई अक्षर*


यूँ तो मैं लेखक हूँ,
अपने शब्दों के सहारे
लिखता हूँ सबके लिए।

कभी हँसी,
कभी पीड़ा,
तो कभी जोश—
सब कुछ लिख पाता हूँ।

परंतु तुम्हें लिखने में
असमर्थ हो जाता हूँ।

तुम्हारे समक्ष, प्रिये,
मैं निशब्द हो जाता हूँ।

जोड़-तोड़-मरोड़ कर
सिर्फ़ ढाई अक्षर
लिख पाता हूँ।

लिखना चाहता हूँ
उपन्यास
तुम्हारी सुंदरता के,
तुम्हारी घनी जुल्फ़ों में
खो जाने की
दुआ लिखना चाहता हूँ।

यूँ तो मैं
नहीं कोई गोताखोर,
पर तुम्हारी आँखों में
डूब कर
विलुप्त होना चाहता हूँ।

अपने मन के मंदिर में
तुम्हें
अजंता की मूर्ति
लिखना चाहता हूँ।

और मेरी गुज़ारिश है—
इतना श्रृंगार
न किया करो,
तुम्हें देख
मैं अपने शब्द
भूल जाता हूँ।

जोड़-तोड़-मरोड़ कर
सिर्फ़ ढाई अक्षर
प्रेम के
लिख पाता हूँ।

It was cold evening 
and the moon was slightly shining ,
suddenly he came and 
apologized her for waiting.

They were deciding what to order
Minutes past and then
he said with smile ,
" I love a cup filled with love,
So cappuccino is fine...
I would rather have a coffee
than have a glass of wine"..
And iced coffee for you am I right?

Oh but last time she remembered he ordered tea ;
So now really curious where it goes she said it in mind, let me see...

They were just having coffee
Except it wasn't really coffee ;
It was more like soul catching fire
Blushing cheeks and 
Hearts fluttering 
In between each sip of his steaming coffee!

While sipping , 
Caffeine strained her lips ;
He took tissue and said, 
I wish next time I could taste that strain
And I will wait for the poetry 
On how well I clean that strain..

His eyes were as brown as 
the coffee she was drinking..
That intencee eye gaze
made her heart skipped a beat..

poem1_theme: Love & Relationship, The beauty of chosing each other
poem1_content:
Love isn’t born in flawless grace,
it grows with time, it finds its place.
In late-night talks and early fights,
in wrong-day moods and restless nights.
It lives in laughter, soft and true,
and in the tears we don’t outgrow.
A relationship is not a flame
that burns too fast, then dies in shame—
it’s embers warm in steady glow,
teaching hearts what they don’t know.
We clash like storms, we bend like trees,
we learn the art of disagreeing with ease.
Your flaws don’t scare my soul away,
they teach my patience how to stay.
Love is a promise we renew
when days feel gray and skies aren’t blue.
It’s choosing “us” when roads divide,
walking slowly, side by side.
Not every chapter rhymes with bliss,
yet even pain becomes a kiss
when held by trust, by honest care—
by two hearts brave enough to dare.
So let the world rush, fall, or stray—
we are the love that learns to stay.

poem1_theme: Bittersweet longing mixed with haunting uncertainty
poem1_content: I first met Priya on the Yellow Line during that heavy Kashmiri Gate rain when everyone pushed too close together.Her dupatta dripped water and she gripped an old diary against her chest. I stood up and gave her my seat. Our fingers touched lightly. "Priya," she said softly. "Aarav," I replied.We rode together every day after that. She shared poems from her DU classes while we sipped chai in Hauz Khas. I showed her sketches of buildings I wanted to design. We ate golgappas at CP and walked hand in hand through Lodhi Gardens. Kissing her under the trees made everything feel right.Sometimes she read quiet stories from her diary about loves lost long ago in old Delhi. She always closed it fast if I leaned in to see more. "Bas private hai yaar," she said with a small smile.Her phone buzzed all the time. "Family stuff," she called it. One night at India Gate with fireworks overhead she pulled out a faded photo from the diary. The boy in it looked exactly like me. "Dekho kitna milta hai tujhse," she said laughing. I held her close.A kurta-wearing guy started showing up at stations behind us. Priya whispered one time "Agar main wahi na hoon jo tu samajhta hai?" The train rattled on and she dropped it.Rain poured that monsoon night in Sarojini. We shared pav bhaji under an awning. Her diary opened to sketches of my face drawn months earlier. Her phone buzzed again. "Kal yahin milna okay? Promise kar," she said and walked into the downpour. Messages stopped. Her Mukherjee Nagar room stood empty. I rode the lines alone searching faces. One evening at Hauz Khas a dupatta lay on the Blue Line bench. A single diary page inside showed my sketch with her handwriting: Knew you'd find this. Last stop is your choice.Train pulled in. I saw her face in the window. Doors slid open to empty seats. I got on. Delhi blurred past.

poem1_theme: Self-respect asserting itself against silent, manipulative love. This is not a poem about heartbreak. It’s a poem about realization.
poem1_content:
विरह की बेड़ियाँ बाँधते समय, 
मेरे हाथों में एक क्षण भी काँपे नहीं तुम्हारे हाथ।
तुम्हारी मूक दशा से समझूँ क्या मैं,
ये वियोग का मौन, या मुझसे नाराज़?
दुत्कार कर मुझे चारों दिशाओं से,
मेरे आगे किस बात की अग्नि उफनाते हो?
जब पाठ पढ़ाया संयोग का तुमने,
तो प्रश्न अलगाव के कहाँ से लाते हो?
निस्तब्ध खड़ा मैं, देख तुम्हें डर से घबराता हूँ,
क्या योग्य हूँ मैं अनकहा छोड़ने के?
मैं सामंजस्य नहीं बिठा पाता हूँ,
षड्यंत्र समझूँ क्या इसे मैं?
नहीं भोलेपन वाली मुझे ये बात लगी,
थक कर हार गए सच दिखाने वाले,
ये नज़रे तो थी प्रेम में पगी l
क्या और भी बाकी रूप हैं तेरे?
या अंत कर दूँ कहानी का l
तेरे ऐसे होने से न होना भला।
प्रवासी ही ठीक, प्रिय तू,
अपनाना तुझे है कठिन बड़ा।
क्या सोच रहे मुझे किसी अनजानी,
 राह में छोड़ आए हो?
 जागो प्रिय, तुम मेरे घर में
 मुझे ही बाँधने आए हो!
जब तक प्राण हैं इस शरीर में,
 मुझे ऐसा प्रेम स्वीकार नहीं।
 गलतियाँ मेरी मत गिनवाओ,
 यदि प्रेम तुम्हें मेरा ज्ञात नहीं।
अगर कभी वो याद आए और
 उन रास्तों पे वापस लौटो तुम,
अगर कभी मेरा नाम पुकारते
मिले खड़े मेरे द्वार पे तुम 
तो हो सके तो उसे लाना,
जिसने महीनों मेरा साथ निभाया,
हो सके तो उसे लाना, जिसने
सफ़ेद फूल हाथ में थमाया
और हृदय स्कंदन का संचार कराया।
हो सके तो उसे लाना और
 हो सके तो तुम ना आना।
तुम जैसे से प्रेम एक बार
 तुम्हें मुझ जैसा सा हो जाए,
तब समझ में आएगा तुम्हें कि,
 पत्र की लिखावट गंदी क्यों थी,
और अन्याय भरी ये संधि क्यों थी।
क्यों गा रहा भला ये व्यथा मैं अपनी?
इन मृत पत्थर सी आँखों के आगे,
जब बदले में कर्ण-बधिर तेरा मौन पाया
तब भी स्वाभिमान को मेरे ये समझ ना आया।
की अब जाने की हो ज़िद पे तुम,
अब जाने की हो ज़िद पे तुम।
अबकी बारी ‘मैं’ मौन रहा,
डर से नहीं, सम्मान से।
देख तुम्हें निस्तब्ध खड़ा l
तुम खोल सलाखें सोच रहे 
बंधी मुझे बनाओगे
ज़ंजीरों से कैसे, प्रिय तुम
इस आत्मा को बाँध पाओगे?

- श्रेयश शर्मा

क्यों अकसर उसी मोड़ पे आके खड़ी हो जाती हूं  ?
तुम्हें समझते-समझते खुदको समझना भूल गई, 
औरों से ज़्यादा मैं खुद से ही खफा हो जाती हूं ,
कोशिश की थी खुदको तलाश करने की पर;
भीड़ से दूर होकर भी अपने वजूद से खो जाती हूं ।
  
यादों की किताब अब पढ़ना नहीं चाहता ये दिल,
फिर उस मजहर पे तुमसे मिलना नहीं चाहता,
ना ही मिले हम अगर तो बेहतर होगा,
अंधेरा ढलकर फिर नया सवेरा होगा,
क्योंकि अब ये दिल फिर से तुम्हारा होना नहीं चाहता ।

मुस्कुराकर अपने हर ग़म को यूं ही सजाती हूं,
आज फिर गलत कहूंगी कि ' मैं तुम्हें भूल जाती हूं ! ' 

~ अमी ♡

poem1_theme: Quiet acceptance of an honest heartbreak, marked by restrained grief, emotional coldness, and resilient self-awareness.
poem1_content:
At −1°C the morning held my name,
Alarm too late, yet guilt felt just the same;
Your last text blinked—seen—then went still,
I warmed the kettle, trained my hands to chill.

You left your hoodie folded on the chair,
Its sleeves preserving nights we used to share;
Betrayal wasn’t anger, nor a spiteful fight,
It was your honesty that vanished with night.

You loved as you knew—complete, sincere,
Devotion never promises to stay near;
Some hearts remain loyal even when they leave,
And truth can wound far deeper than deceive.

I learned acceptance in the mirror’s stare,
Unshaved mornings, the quiet weight I bear;
I typed your name in nights that wouldn’t end,
Then let it go—love isn’t ours to defend.

The city moved—cold buses, borrowed days,
Street dogs curled tight in winter’s haze;
The world agreed with you and carried on,
As if my loss were right, and not wrong.

At −1°C I understood this part:
She didn’t break me—she was honest from the start;
Love froze, yet meaning still persists—
She chose her truth. I still exist.

GHAZAL (SILSILE)



Ye  silsile  mulakaat  ke rahne  de 
Toota hi sahi ek khwaab rahne de


Jo tu  rutha  to sab bikhar jayega
Kuch to ishq ka bharam rahne de


Kitna  bebas  main or sang dil tu
Inteha ho chuki itna sitam rahne de


Har ko mayassar nahi sukoun jahaan main
Ek umid a zindagi baki rahne de


Kitna chahke hai dekh ke Dil
tujh ko
Apne chehre ki chamak aankhoun main rahne de



          ग़ज़ल (सिलसिले)



ये सिलसिले मुलाकात के रहने दे,
टूटा  ही  सही एक ख्वाब रहने दे।

जो तू रूठा तो सब बिखर जाएगा,
कुछ  तो  इश्क़  का भरम रहने दे।

कितना  बेबस  मैं और संग दिल तू,
इंतहा हो चुकी इतना सितम रहने दे।

हर  को मयस्सर नहीं सुकूं जहां में,
एक उम्मीदे ज़िन्दगी बाकी रहने दे।

कितना  चहके  है देख के दिल तुझको,
अपने चेहरे की चमक आंखों में रहने दे।


@Dr.javedahmad

1. *दिल की आवाज़*
दिल टूटने के बाद फिर जुड़ जाता है

बुरा होने के बाद फिर अच्छा हो ही जाता है

अपने ग़मों को क़ाबू में रखकर यह ज़िंदगी जीनी पड़ती है

क्योंकि अपने ग़मों से किसी को कोई फ़र्क़ नहीं पड़ता है

यह दुनिया बहुत ज़ालिम है
यहाँ कोई किसी का नहीं है

यह एक जंगल है
जहाँ शेर भी सुरक्षित नहीं है

डर सबको लगता है और
 सबका दिल घबराता है

यहाँ सबके साथ रहते हुए भी

 एक अकेलापन महसूस होता है

जब जीवन की सच्चाई सामने आती है

तब जीवन सच में कठिन नज़र आता है

यह ज़िंदगी एक बहुत ग़ज़ब का खेल है

और यह अच्छे से अच्छे खिलाड़ी को आज़माता है

प्यार, रिश्तेदारी और दिल टूटना

यहाँ आम माना जाता है

दिल टूटना यहाँ कोई मायने नहीं रखता

यहाँ तो प्यार और रिश्तेदारों से ही इंसान हार मान जाता है

किसी की ख़ुशी यहाँ दूसरों को ग़म दे जाती है

यह दुनिया है यारों, यहाँ हर इंसान की बलि दी जाती है

खून के रिश्ते यहाँ पानी बन जाते हैं

क्या करें, इंसान यहाँ हैवान बन जाते हैं

चाहते हुए भी इंसान यहाँ खुश नहीं रह पाता है

क्योंकि जो वह चाहता है, यहाँ कभी नहीं पाता है

हम तो प्यार से जीना चाहते हैं

पर यहाँ हर कोई अपनी क़ीमत बताता है

इस दिल से कभी पूछो कि वह

 प्यार में क्या-क्या सह जाता है

बनावटी हँसी में यहाँ इंसान

 खुद को भूल जाता है

यही सच्चाई है दोस्तों

यहाँ हर एक इंसान खुद को अकेला पाता है।
poem2_content:
2. *एक एहसास का सफर*
प्यार एक एहसास है,
दो अजनबियों के क़रीब आने का राग है…
जब यह दिल किसी दिल को अपना मान ले,
तो वह कुछ मधुर संगीतों का साज़ है…

इस क़दर एक-दूसरे में बस जाती है जान,
एक की आबरू बने दूसरे का मान…
एक पल वही तुम्हारी पूरी दुनिया बन जाता है,
जिसके प्यार के आगे सब फीका पड़ जाता है…

प्यार का मंज़र कितना ख़ूबसूरत है, दोस्तों,
जब दो दिलों का एक नायाब सफ़र तय हो जाता है…
अनदेखे रास्तों पर चलते हुए वे क़दम,
एक ही राह के हमक़दम हो जाते हैं…

अनजानी कहानियों को सँजोता है दिल,
रोज़ उसी के ख़्वाबों में खोता है दिल…
प्यार वह जादू है जिसे पा कर,
दो जिस्म एक जान बन जाते हैं…

poem1_content:
Among all my deaths,
the one at your lips was by far the best.

I’ve died in streets that never learned my name,
in nights that stole my faith.
I’ve bled in silence, stitched myself again,
and learned to lick my wounds in shame.

I’ve fallen to the grind of borrowed days,
to quiet hungers dressed as self-restraint.
The world taught me to live in lesser ways,
to pay in years, tears, bones, and tolls of pain.
But among all my deaths,
the one at your lips was by far the best.

I’ve died paying bills with scraping courage.
I’ve died dragging mornings,
tearing my seams and broken dreams.
I’ve died trusting, drinking, giving,
and loving people who never stayed.
But, my dear, among all my deaths,
the one at your lips was by far the best.

I died when you first looked at me—
that was when I started grading my deaths.
I held my heart and practiced on my own,
mimicking in front of the mirror
all the times I bid the world alone,
with farewells of a lifetime
and sickening groans.

I knew you would ruin me.
I knew you were going to be another one,
a device for my demise.

And if I must be honest,
if the courts demand a final count,
I’d say among all my deaths,
the one at your lips was by far the best.

Maine kaha tha na tumse,
Mujhse wafa mat karna.
Rahunga har pal tera saaya banke,
Mujhse aisi umeed mat rakhna,
Maine kaha tha na tumse.

Dekho tumhe ab jaana pada,
Aankhon me ashq liye, dil me dard liye,
Apne jazbaaton ko ab dafnaana pada.
Maine kaha tha na tumse,
Mujhse mohabbat mat karna,
Dard milenge behisaab toh,
Meri tarah ban jaoge,
Maine kaha tha na tumse.

Mere nashe me tu kyun jee hai piya,
Iss tarah mujhse kyun pyaar hai kiya?
Ye ishq raas nahi aayegi mujhko.
Maine kaha tha na tumse,
Mere qareeb mat aana,
Samandari toofano me akela tairta hoon main,
Saath mere jo aaoge, 
Toh tum bhi doob jaoge,
Maine kaha tha na tumse.
poem2_content:
Gali gali me hai nera naam,
Gali gali hoon main badnaam.
Main insaan nahi haiwaan hoon,
Khud ka hi abhimaan hoon,
Raah chalte parindo ka darinda hoon,
Apne apraadho pe naa sharminda hoon.
Pehchana mujhe?
Arre main hoon aapka apna,
Aapka apna Rapist.

Kya ukhaad liya tumne mera,
Candle march aur protests karke,
Main to aaj bhi khula azaad hoon,
Naa hi tumhara kanoon badla hai,
Naa hi badle hai hum.
Ye silsila barso se chalta aaya hai,
Aur chalta rahega,
Kyunki,
Kanoon mera hai, main kanoon ka nahi.

Main chahoon kisi ko bhi apna shikaar bana loon,
Fark nahi padta 3 mahina ki bacchi ho ya 60 saal ki budhiya,
Fark nahi padta jeans me ho ya burkha me,
Fark nahi padta mandir me ho ya masjid me,
Fark nahi padta insaan ho ya jaanwar,
Kyunki,
Main rapist hoon, meri doosri koi jaat nahi.

Jaante ho hamari jaat sabse zyaada idhar hi paayi jaati hai,
Auron ke yahan to maut ki saza hai,
Hamare yahan hi sirf maza hai.
Isliye,
Main yahan apni haiwaniyat karta rahunga,
Tum apna insaaniyat saabit karte rehna.
Main ek aur Nirbhaya, Asifa, Priyanka ko jalaunga,
Tum bas mombattiyan hi jalate rehna,
Na rok paayega koi kanoon tumhara,
Na hi rukunga main,
Main phir balatkaar karunga,
Koi mera ghanta kuch nahi ukhaad sakta,
Har baar ki tarah tumhara hi kanoon mujhe bachayega,
Kyunki,
Kanoon mera hai, main kanoon ka nahi.

Kab khaulega khoon tumlog ka?
Kab tak kanoon ke bharose baithe rahoge?
Kya pata kanoon tumlog ke bharose baitha ho.
Hamein apne wajood se mitana hai agar,
Awaaz uthane se acha hai kanoon uthao magar,
Tumse naa ho payega.
Isiliye main darta nahi kisi se,
Na tumse, na tumhare kanoon se, naa hi uss khuda se,
Kyunki,
Kanoon mera hai, main kanoon ka nahi.

poem1_content:
मैं,
एक पुल हूं ।।

और तुम

एक नदी...


मुझपर हर दिन हज़ारों कदमों के भार रहते हैं 
...कभी कमज़ोर नहीं पड़ा


मगर आज टूट जाना चाहता हूं

इसीलिए नहीं कि मैं कमज़ोर हो गया हूं और अब मुझपर इन कदमों का भार और सहा नहीं जाता


मैं,
अब टूटकर 

बिखर जाना चाहता हूं

तुम्हारे आंचल में ।।




मुझे अपने आंचल में बिखर जाने देना...


मेरे टूटने पर
कदमों के भार मुझे शायद खंडहर कहें
 
मगर तुम मुझे अपने सीने से लगा
अपने तल की गहराई को पूर्ण करने वाला श्रृंगार बना लेना


“यूं बहती रहना ।।”



मुझे खुद में विलीन कर
मेरी राख को अपने भीतर समाए
वेग में बहा, अपने जल से मेरे इस कठोर अस्तित्व को सहलाकर शीतल कर देना


और बस...
“यूं बहती रहना”







किनारों को नहीं समझ
केवल ठहरे रहना प्रेम नहीं

पुल कभी नहीं टूटते
बल्कि विलीन हो जाते हैं उस प्रेम नदी में जिसे उसने ताउम्र गुज़रते देखा ।।


पुल किनारों की तरह नहीं ठहरे
परंतु साहस दिखा खुद को नदी में विलीन कर लिया

विलीन कर लेना
ठहर जाने से कई अच्छा है ।।




“पुरुष को प्रेम में ठहरे हुए किनारे नहीं बल्कि टूटकर बिखर जाने वाले पुल बनना चाहिए”

I rarely read books of love like you . I'm neither an artist nor a writer or musician who you admire . Nor do I lose myself in romantic movies . It's not that I hate love or love stories . I know love is the most beautiful feeling in the world . But I'm unlucky , with love , with confidence to fight or even search for love . " I have a side character energy " . I feel like I was never meant to be the hero of my own story . I do see myself in those frames , in those stories . Not as a lover but the one who observes from the sidelines . Watching , but never stepping in . The one who's smile never fades even when it hurts . The one who hide his sorrows in quiet corners . Loves from a distance and first one to clap at your achievements . The one who see's you slipping into another man's arms yet chooses to remain silent . And whispers to himself :- 
" Maybe this is how it was meant to be "
By Aryaveer

Gazing at the distance
The window foggy by my breath
Fireflies dancing around the garden
As i wait for my lovely maiden

Is waiting supposed to hurt this much? I ask
Under the moonlight my body basks
Her laughter echoes beyond the shore
As i dare to drown in those waters to hear them more

White petals of the flower withers away
Only thing remains are the pollens of hope
Is it the end of something? Or birth of something
And i tie myself to that with a rope

But I could feel the waves receding
And the birds started to swirl
There she came with her arm reaching out
Her eyes shining brighter than a pearl

The heavens smiled , the angels sang
As I felt her warmth in my soul
Waiting can make you weary, perhaps
But never severs the love filled scoul
poem2_content:
And when the stars align
That's when I could see her eyes
And when the clouds abide
That's when I can say her goodbye

And when the clocks stop ticking
That's when her whispers grow in my heart
And when the butterflies gather
That's when her scent starts growing apart

And when there is silence
There is her
And when there is chaos 
There is also her

And when there is nothingness
Her arms wrap around me
And when I finally notice her gaze
That's when she is nowhere around to be

poem1_content:
इस तन्हाई की इंतहा नहीं है,
मेरे दर्द की ज़ुबाँ नहीं है।

​रो भी नहीं सकती और मुस्कुराया भी नहीं जाता,
उसको याद नहीं करना और वो भुलाया भी नहीं जाता।

​वो ज़ेहन में बसा है और हर कतरे में उसका नूर है,
वो शख़्स एक लत सा है मुझमें, मुझमें हर पल उसी का सुरूर है।

​आँखें हैं नम मेरी और टूटी हुई सी मुस्कान है,
सबकुछ क्यों है बदला सा? क्यों बिखरा ये मेरा जहान है?

​काश! खुद के अशकों को मैं पोंछ पाती,
काश! अपने बीते हुए कल में मैं पहुँच पाती।

​तो फिर थाम कर रखती उस शख़्स को मैं, या फिर खुद को उससे मिलने ही न देती,
जी लेती मैं खुशनुमा सी ज़िंदगी, या फिर खुद के अक्स को रोने ही न देती।

​मैं खो जाऊँ जिसमें, वो गहरा ख्याल हो तुम,
मैं हर बार खुद को हार जाऊँ तुम पर, इतने बेमिसाल हो तुम।

​तुम्हारा ख्याल मुझमें, मुझ ही को बेअसर कर जाता है,
जाने क्यों किसी शख्स से इस दिल को इतना लगाव हो जाता है?

खुशी राऊत
qe
poem2_content: qef

poem1_content:
We’ve always known Love and Time
They cannot exist without each other
Strung together in a brittle bond
Twisted between our slick fingers
And the hands of an ignorant timekeeper
Pounding at the heart, never the head
The string creaks with every desperate pull
Away from the tender grasps of our loved ones 
And towards the galaxies out of our reach,
Then comes the day when the string whiplashes—
Straight into the knot that ties us all together
With our untouched love and burned fingers,
Reaching out with shaky hands to the space    
Where our loved ones smiled for the final time.

For the first time,
She got a strange feeling,
Which took shape in her heart.
She was confused and scared,
At the same time,
As she was getting such a feeling for the first time.
It was something she was afraid of.
It was the so-called love,
Which everyone speaks of.
She was not ready to confess,
So, She hid her emotions
From everyone.
As her heart was not a sack,
With a heavy heart,
She expressed her feelings, 
To the person she never wanted to.
She realised that it won't have,
A positive outcome.
Slowly, she started to step back.
She tried to delete him.
She tried to erase him.
The  realisation of being in love
Made her feel,
Scared and sad at the same time.
As days passed, She realised
One thing, 
To be in love was not meant
For her.
This  realisation made her 
Change everything,
She had for him.
In the end, She realised one thing,
"Some love is better when not fulfilled".
And the realisation,
Made her happy and sad,
Both at the same time.
poem2_content:
She didn't know she would 
Get attached,
He didn't know that,
It could be him.
She was in a state of dilemma,
He was in a state of confusion,
Perhaps surprised.
So, she moved on.
She might have said that to him.
Or she might have said that,
In her mind.
"Attachment can bring happiness,
But sometimes the same
Attachment can kill the happiness too!"

poem1_content:
کِسی نے خُوشبُو کو میرے تکئے پاس بکھیرا
مَیں نے سِسکِیوں میں سر اپنا کانٹوں پہ رکھ دیا

چُپ چاپ سی محبّت کو گُماں رکھّے
اِک طرفہ تھا جو رِشتہ، فغاں رکھ دیا

چاند آنکھوں، دھوپ ہاتھوں میں لانے والے
اُن کی چاہ میں مَینے دِل اَنگاروں پہ رکھ دیا

وہ جو ہونٹوں سے دُعا بن کے اُتر آئے تھے
مَینے اُس لفظ پہ عُمرِ جاوِداں رکھ دیا

تُم نے لوٹ آنے کا وعدہ نہ کیا پھر بھی
مَینے سجدوں میں اپنا حق، غَرور و گِلہ رکھ دیا

میری آنکھوں میں جو ٹُوٹا ہُوا آسماں تھا
مَیں نے اُس خواب کو مِٹی میں جہاں رکھ دیا

محبّت کے وُضو سے، حق کے حُضور میں جھکا سَر
مَینے ہر درد کو سینے میں نہاں رکھ دیا

مَیں نے چاہت کے کفن سی لیے آنسُوئوں سے
اور اُسے صبر کے مقتل میں اذاں رکھ دیا

تُم جو قسمت میں نہیں تھے میری
مَینے تُمہیں مانگنا بهی خُود پہ حرام رکھ دیا

اللہ کی رضا میں سب وار دیا زاآرا
دِل کے ہر گوشے میں تمہاری نُورِ ہدایت رکھ دیا
