I loved you knowing you were fiction,
Still you felt like fate’s prediction.
You taught my heart how love should feel,
Even though you weren’t real.

I memorized your smile in chapters, 
A love that lived in ever-afters.
You ruled the World in tailored suits, 
Spoke harshly hid your darkest truths.

Danger rests in your name,
Yet with me you weren’t the same.
Your hands were clean, your past was not,
A gentleman with battles fought.

To her, he wasn’t deals or fame,
Just someone gentle beneath his name.
A ruthless man, yet soft at core.
The kind of love she read before.

I hope I find a love like yours, so deep,
A fire for me alone, a possession to keep.
A love that’s relentless, yet tender and sweet, 
Where danger fades when our eyes meet.

I long for a man who sees me like you do,
A storm and a shelter, both fierce and true.
I hope I find a love like yours, so true,
Where every beat of his heart belongs to me too.

Fierce to the World, yet gentle in my hands,
A love that burns hotter than any plans.
I want a heart that aches when I am gone,
And melts in my arms at the break of dawn.

I loved you knowing you were fiction,
Still you felt like fate’s prediction.
For love at minus one this is-
A love in ink I can’t kiss.

~Riyanshi Malhotra

Love should never be judged,
and we never love someone conditionally,
because loving someone is never partial
and never judgmental.
I loved someone from the depth of my heart,
but sometimes not all stories end with a smile;
some stories just end…!
I love her like she is my only future.
I love her like she is my only dream.
I love her like she is the queen of my kingdom.
I love her like she is my only day and night.
I love her like she is the only moon for me.
I live for her because she is my only life.
I love her because she is my only condition.
I fight for her because I believe she is my only fighter.
I love her because I have respect for her.
I love her because she is my friend too.
I love her because I feel warm when it’s cold.
I love her because I am unfiltered with her.
I love her because she is an angel for me.
I protect her like she is my baby.
I earn like she is my treasure.
I loved her at my 100%,
but at last, I am alone with my minus one (-1),
because she is the only 100…!

Everytime I adore you in silence,
My heart sounds louder ,
A slight tremor starts in my hands ,
My eyes gaze at you like a selenophile looking at the moon.
Illude you at Oasis,
I would walk barefoot throughout the desert. 
Though you're cold to me,
Every second,I just burnt my heart to made you feel warmth.
Your presence is a salve to my chaotic soul.
Maybe this is what I'm calling it as love...
poem2_content:
I loved you to the moon and back
because, you felt like coming home
Every heartbeat of mine whispered your name.
Though you left ,
I loved you in ways I can't explain 
but, Now I bleed in silence 
calling it as love...

poem1_content:
ठहराव था उसकी बातों में,
बहकाव था उसकी यादों में।
हुआ रुबरु दिल से वो,
दर्द था उसकी आँखों में,
तब भी झुकाव था उसकी मोहब्बत में।

खामोश थे हम,
चुप रहे तुम।
मनाया तुमने,
तो हँस दिए हम।
समझाया तुमने,
तो समझ गए हम।

रो दिए हम,
खो गए तुम।
निभाते रहे हम,
और खामोश थे तुम।

Love, in the Smallest Things
Love is not always roses in bloom,
Or moonlit promises whispered in June.
Sometimes it’s silence, shared without fear,
A hand that stays when the end feels near.
It lives in questions softly asked,
In tired eyes behind daily masks.
In waiting patiently, learning to bend,
In choosing to stay, again and again.
Love is the courage to show your scars,
Not hiding your cracks behind shining stars.
It’s forgiving before pride can speak,
Finding strength in moments weak.
It’s laughter that heals a breaking day,
And tears that words can’t wipe away.
Not perfect stories, flawless or grand,
But two imperfect hearts trying to understand.
poem2_content:
We, in Between
A relationship isn’t built on perfect days,
It grows in the pauses, the unsaid ways.
In mornings that start without a smile,
And evenings where silence stays a while.
It’s learning the weight of each other’s moods,
Reading the heart beyond words and rules.
Holding space when answers don’t come,
Standing together when the world feels numb.
Some days we argue, some days we bend,
Some days we forget why we chose to begin.
Yet somewhere between the hurt and pride,
Lives the reason we still don’t walk aside.
It’s choosing honesty over being right,
Choosing to stay through another night.
Not fixing, not saving, just being there,
Sharing the burden because you care.
A relationship is not a flawless art,
It’s two real people with a fragile heart.
And if after storms, we still remain—
That quiet survival is love, not pain.

poem1_theme: An exploration of asymmetrical love, where devotion becomes endurance and tenderness is spent on a silence that never answers. The poem traces how fidelity to an unchanging beloved turns inward, consuming the giver until affection itself becomes a form of disappearance.
poem1_content:
She burned with love the stars could scarcely hold;
he paid her fire in currencies of cold.
She pitched her longing in his fields of frost,
stitched heat into the shadows that he lost.
Each dusk he peeled her gentleness away
a pomegranate split by wintry clay.
Still she remained a lantern in his storm,
braiding light through hail to keep him warm.
The dunes bent low to hear her breathing pray,
while glaciers crowned the words he would not say.
She wrapped the blizzard tighter round her skin,
called every wound a door to let him in.
He stood unmoved a citadel of snow,
a peak that never learned how suns can go.
When winter climbed the ladder of her chest,
she sang instead of cursing what had pressed:
Her love was the sun beneath his storming skies;
that cold outlived her pulse, her flame, her cries.
Upon the snow lay one last soft adieu.

poem1_title: Why I wasn't supposed to love you?
poem1_content:
I wasn't supposed to love you,
But that day every breeze whispered in my ear,
Every bright star burned into my eyes,
Every clapping wave of the ocean made my heart feel heavy, overwhelmed,
That day , every jolt shook my soul and I ended up loving you.
I loved you so deeply that my lips couldn't find my words and my eyes found no escape.
The only thing alive within me was a sound, a rhythm that aligned with you,
And in that sound I realised,
I had already fallen in love with you.
Even though I wasn't supposed to,
Cause I knew you weren't mine
poem2_content:
You were a season that forgot its end,
A monsoon stitched into my sky.
I built my home from your weather,
Then drowned when you passed me by.

My heart is a clock with missing hands,
Still ticking your name in rust.
Every second leans toward yesterday,
Every tomorrow collapses to dust.

I am a lighthouse with no sea,
Shining for ships that won’t return.
Your absence is a winter sun—
Bright enough to see, too cold to burn.

Love was a language written in smoke,
I breathed it in, it scarred my lungs.
Now I speak in ashes and echoes,
Of songs we never sung.

You left like a borrowed book unfinished,
My margin notes still bleeding through.
And I remain the open page,
Waiting for a reader who isn’t you.

poem1_title: Cold Hurts Less than Loving You
poem1_theme: This poem explores love in the harshest cold, at −1°C, where the world is silent, frost bites, and warmth is scarce. It captures the dark intensity of passion that refuses to die even when everything around is frozen. The emotion is raw and haunting—aching desire, obsessive longing, and the sharp pain of a love that both consumes and freezes the heart. It’s about the tension between the icy external world and the internal fire of feelings that cannot thaw, portraying a love that is beautiful, dangerous, and inescapable.
poem1_content:
At minus one, my blood runs slow,
The city holds its breath in snow.
My lips are blue, my hands are numb,
Still your name makes my pulse drum.
Ice in veins, frost in bone,
Every night I freeze alone.
The world says feelings learn to die—
Yet you refuse to say goodbye.
The cold bites deep, it splits my skin,
Still your absence burns within.
I shake, I ache, I break, I bleed,
You are the warmth I do not need—
But crave like fire craves the air,
Like lungs crave breath they shouldn’t dare.
At −1, love turns cruel,
A fever breaking every rule.
Ice outside, inside—flame,
Freezing breath, burning name.
If this is pain, then let it stay,
I’d rather freeze than feel okay.
For even numbness whispers true—
The cold hurts less than loving you.
poem2_theme: This poem delves into a love that exists in the frozen margins of the world, at −1°C, where silence weighs like ice and the heart shivers in isolation. It captures the haunting paradox of longing that burns while everything else freezes, where desire and pain are inseparable. The emotion is raw and relentless—a quiet obsession, a piercing ache, a love that defies warmth yet thrives in its absence. It is not comfort or joy that drives it, but the stark beauty of endurance, the bittersweet tension between surrendering to the cold and refusing to let go. In this frozen landscape, love is both a torment and a revelation, fragile yet infinitely persistent, proving that even in numbness, the heart feels with devastating clarity.
poem2_content:
At minus one, the night feels endless,
Your absence sharp, your presence relentless.
We move through frost, two shadows entwined,
Hands that ache, hearts misaligned.
The cold seeps in where words should stay,
It chills the smiles we try to display.
Yet in the silence, your breath I hear,
A fragile warmth, impossibly near.
Our love is a storm beneath frozen skies,
A trembling flame that never dies.
We fight, we falter, we push, we plead,
Yet cling to each other like roots to seed.
I see the frost lining your eyes,
The quiet hurt, the hidden sighs.
We argue, we break, we drift, we bend,
But something stubborn refuses to end.
The nights are long, the wind is cruel,
Yet even ice cannot undo this duel.
Our hands may tremble, our voices may fray,
But hearts at −1 still find their way.
Love here is sharp, it cuts and heals,
It’s the ache you feel when nothing feels.
We burn in silence, we freeze in touch,
A paradox too cruel, yet not too much.
So let the world outside shiver and moan,
Our hearts, though frozen, are never alone.
At −1°C, love survives the frost,
For even in cold, we’ve found what we lost.

poem1_content:
Your love of sapphire 

It's tough, living knowing your last moments are near, and I have always read in books that in your last times, you always get flashbacks of the person whom you love, and he is now just ruling my mind with the flashbacks. I don't know why he has come to my mind, Summi, but I am not surprised that he is in my mind right now. I remember every line he told me and also everything that his eyes conveyed but not his words. I feel sad to have hurt him that day ; I feel sad for every tear he cried that day, and I couldn't fix him or give him hope. 
Summi asked, "Then why didn't he come today? Does he really love you?" I knew she would ask me this. I smiled and looked at her, and said, "he will also not come after I leave this body because of that day he promised me."

It's been three months since we last met, and today we will finally meet. Now things have changed ; both of us have a fear of losing each other. After I found out that he likes me, I know he won't say it to me because he feels the fear of losing this bond, but he doesn't know that I like him too. However, I can't express my feelings to him either because of this lupus, which is damaging my kidneys. I thought that this skin infection was just a result of inadequate nutrition and bad eating habits, but even after changing everything, it didn't improve until I got diagnosed at the city hospital and learned about lupus.

I saw him entering the gate, and I was experiencing such bad fatigue that I can't even speak to call him. But he saw me and came towards me . I feel so exhausted just walking awhile for a seat , and he noticed and without saying anything , He handed his water bottle to me and gestured to have some water. I had some water, and now I feel better. He sat beside me , held me , and adjusted my sitting posture on one side of the bench . Then he looked at me for half a minute and then looked away. I said with a smile “ kya hua ?? Baat bhi nahi karoge mujhse ?!! Naraz ho kya mujhse jo 3 mahine se mene kuch apne baremain nahi bataya isiliye?! Sorry vidhur ; i am really sorry!! “ he again looked but didnt reply then he stood up and walked in front of me, and with his hand gestures, he told me to stand. He raised his hand towards me to help me up, and when I stood, he looked into my eyes, slowly raised his hands near my shoulders , and hugged me gently, saying “ main abhi bhi naraz hu tumse bhot , phone hote hue bhi na call karti ho aur na receive goan se itni dur rahe rahi ho ye checkup ke liye chinta nahi hoti kya tumhari mujhe!! Janti ho ki tumhare bina main akela hu tab bhi esa kar rahi ho mere sath , ye sahi nahi hai “ I was listening to him, but this last line again took me back to the moment when I used to just enjoy the whole day with his company and jokes. By hearing this , I hugged him more tightly; he also hugged me more tightly. It's just clear that he cared for me every day for the past three months . For more than a minute, we just hugged each other, and I got that relaxed feeling that I had been missing for the past three months.


Then we sat down, and I sat close to him and held his hands. He was smiling without showing it in his face. Then he again stood up and stood in front of me, opened his bag, and showed it was full of gifts like bangles, dresses, sweets, and many more. I asked him why so much for me. He looked at me and said “ shayad tumne mujhe in 3 mahino main yaad na kiya ho par 6 festival aur tumhara birthday ke liye mene har baar tumhe khus dekhne ke liye ye sab liya tha , ajj moka mil raha hai so isiliye dena chahta hu , atleast birthday main to mil leti ye meri icha thi par koi nahi tum mili mujhse ye bhot hai “
I was overwhelmed with this gesture. I know he missed me every day, but he waited. That makes me blessed that I have him.

After that we walked together holding hands. He told me everything that happened in my absence in the village, and I felt happy that he didn't want me to miss everything that happened, and then he said “ yaad hai , tumne 100 meter race main bhagne ke 2 lap main thak gayi thi aur then mene tumhara haat pakada aur race complete karwa aur tab se humari batte start hui “ i said smiling to him “ then , again mera haat pakadke mujhe ye park se bhahar le chalo bhuk lag rahi hai “Then we started to run, and while running with him, it felt like I was reliving our first meeting, and as fast as we were reaching the gate of the park, I felt sad because I knew he was going to get hurt today, and the happiness at the start of running had faded at the end when we reached the gate.

I got fatigue again just because of lupus. As I knew now, this is the reason, but in those days I used to cry because I couldn’t run or dance just because of this and even got bullied by fellow classmates just because my face had redness, and everyone would insult me, saying, “Are raccoons aa gayi tu?” I started to just hate raccoons just because of this. Even sometimes teachers in fun would call me “raccoon,” and my innocent parents would just always try to console me after these insults And this thing started me to feel insecure for him that his friends and others judge him just because of my face, and I started to distance myself, but I never thought that this one thing would make me start to fall in love. He noticed that and came to ask what happened, and at that moment I should have just tried to ignore it, but for the first time someone had politely asked me about the issue, and I couldn’t resist. I told him, “Yaar, tum mere sath na raha karo; mera chehra acha nahi dikhta just because of this redness. Tumhe log judge karenge bhot,” and he said, “Ye redness bikul butterfly jesi dikhti hai aur isse tum bhot sundar dikhti ho, trust me.” 

As the time was passing, I was breaking down, thinking that I would never meet him ever. In fact, I was so happy with him, but the time forced my heart to tell him about my illness, and I didn't want to do it, but I didn't have any options.

We both sat on the ground near the flower bed, and I held his hands and looked at him, and he was happy at that moment. He wanted to enjoy the time with me before the pupils shrank and that smile dropped just after I showed him my medical reports. I thought he would ask many questions, and he also asked, but not by words but by his eyes, and when he started to talk, he said, “Mere akelepan main mera sath dene ka wada kiya tha, ese hi chale jayogi?”

He asked, “Tumhe janna hai na?? Operations ki date likhi hai; 4 din baad observation main bhi rahna hoga. Ayo, tumhe drop kar deta hu.” And he pulled a flower and handed it over to me, and I took that and asked him, looking at him, “Whydon’t flowers live forever?” He replied, “Because they bloom just long enough to teach us how to love, then leave us to remember.”

Summi said, “Then why not him?? “She said, ‘Kyuki mene roka tha jab main apne ghar ke pass aa rahi thi na, uske sath he was crying just imagining that I will be no more for him, and I was also emotional, wiping his tears, and when we reached near my house, he said, “Main nahi ja raha wapas; main rukunga tumhare akhri saas thak yaha.”’ (Then she started to breathe heavily, and Summi ran to call the doctor, and when the doctor reached, she was breathing heavily and was crying also. The doctor tried to handle it but was not in control, and she pulled Summi and said, ‘Agar wo tumhe mile to usse kabhi mat batana ki main usse bhot pyaar karti hu; wo jee nahi payega,’ and she stopped breathing.) 

He was waiting outside the corridor, and I noticed him when I heard his voice crying after knowing that she is no more with us. I walked to him and asked him, “Kyu nahi aaye uske samne?? Kyu??” He said, “Usne kaha tha, ‘If I die, then don’t come to visit me at ritual because I know you can’t handle your tears and I am not there to make you feel okay at the moment.’” And main apne ap ki roke nahi paya usse dekhke bina.

That day I understood that love is not about being together; I understood that “it's about being forever.”

I learned your name like borrowed prayer,
soft enough to not despair.
I loved you quiet, out of sight,
the way the moon loves borrowed light.

You spoke to me with honest ease,
like mirrors hear without a plea.
Never knowing words could stay
and slowly wear my heart away.

I was the chair you never moved,
the space you kept, but never proved.
Not lost enough to be goodbye,
not close enough to ask you why.

You never hurt me—that is true.
One-sided love has no clear you.
No fault, no lie, no wrong to blame,
just one heart burning without a flame.

I cheered for joys I couldn’t claim,
heard celebration, missed the name.
And still I smiled, still wished you well,
learned how to lose without a yell.

So when I left, I made no sound,
no shattered heart, no battleground.
I chose myself when love stood still
the quietest, bravest act of will.


                                              -Siya

poem1_theme: Heartbreak, yearning, humility, dread, devotion, self destruction.
poem1_content:
A year passed, then another,
and yet another drifted quietly by.
The pages of my book keep turning,
but the chapters remain unwritten.

My heart cried out,
my ears leaned into the silence—
yet not a single word reached me from you.

Often, I stood at the edge of reaching out,
fingers trembling with unsent intentions,
but it never made sense.
I know I am not the chosen one.
I know I can never take the place
of the one who already stands beside you.

Still, something in me hopes—
I don’t know what to call it.
A man who never believed in myths or rituals
now looks up at the sky
and sends quiet messages to the stars,
like a child chasing a crock of gold
at the end of a fading rainbow.

In your absence, my eyes keep searching for you.
I wander near your street,
just for the mercy of a glimpse.
Yet when you are near,
my courage abandons me,
and I forget how to look at you.

Everything I have ever done
now belongs to the past—
but had those moments been with you,
they might have become memories
I would have cherished forever.

Yeah, I know everything I write will never reach you,
But I still write, knowing there is no chance—only hope.

My friends try to make me laugh, to help me heal and move on.
But how can I heal,
When pain is the only thread that links me to you?


In the end, all I have of you are your pictures—
Pictures that are pain disguised as gain.
But the pain itself is of a different kind—
Bitter, yet the best feeling
When I see you smiling in them.

And if this ever finds its way to you,
if your eyes ever rest upon these lines,
just tell me if you need anything.
For I would burn my own house to the ground
if it meant giving you light
in your darkest hour.

Life lays its riches at our feet,
Yet something aches—still incomplete.
A story told with practiced art,
Pages turn, but miss one heart.

The sky still wears its silver clouds,
Full of noise, yet never loud.
Not rain it seeks, nor stars above,
But borrowed heat—remembered love.

Once, I was warmth without a flame,
A quiet glow, a steady name.
I softened nights, I held the seam,
I was the fire within the dream.

Now cold has learned my former place,
It wears my calm, it keeps my face.
Cold doesn’t wound, it doesn’t cry,
It simply stays and asks me why.

Warmth heals the cracks we never show,
Cold survives, but doesn’t grow.
And though the sky still looks the same,
It misses what once fed its flame.

I do not wish to blaze or burn,
Just ache enough to still remember—
That I was once the missing part,
The breath, the glow, the living heart.

And if the world feels dim, unsure,
It’s not because it can’t endure—
It’s just still learning how to be
Without the warmth that once was me.

ऐ काश के हम कभी और मिले होते,
तुम हमसे जुड़े होते, हम तुमसे जुड़े होते,
दोनों के मन में ये प्रेम के फूल खिले होते,
ऐ काश के हम कभी और मिले होते।
ना तेरी कोई गलती, ना मेरी कोई गलती है,
ये तो वक़्त है शैतान, जिसकी हमें देखके जलती है,
साथ होते तो शुरू खुशियों के सिलसिले होते,
ऐ काश के हम कभी और मिले होते।
वक़्त होता अलग तो क्या ही माहौल होता,
गुम तुम्हारी आँखों में, मैं शायद खुशहाल होता,
नाराज़ होती तुम, तो हाल मेरा बेहाल होता,
लड़ते कभी-कभी, कभी-कभी शायद बवाल होता,
खुश होते, नाराज़ होते, पर दर्द ना कभी सवाल होता।
ना होते कभी शिकवे, ना ही कभी गिले होते,
ऐ काश के हम कभी और मिले होते।

poem1_theme: The unconditional love between the father and the daughter!
poem1_content:
Dear APPA,
You loved me from a distance,
quietly carrying my name.
I mistook your silence for absence,
envying children whose joy was loud with light.

I didn't know,
you spoke my name in rooms, I wasn't in,
loving me from afar,
and shielding me without words.

Then, death stood before me-
uninvited, unforgiving.
In your final hours, you answered no one.
The world could not reach you,
but, my voice did!

You whispered,
I'm fading, slowly, forever.
I heard your last heartbeat.
I watched the moment,
love became memory!

I stayed quiet, swallowed the pain,
held my tears, so others wouldn't break.
Inside, my heart screamed,
don't leave me, Dad!
But, my lips never learned courage.

Only after you were gone,
did the truth arrive-
that, I had  always been loved,
just never aloud.

Now, I carry you in every breath.
Your dreams walk with mine.
Your suffering ends,
where my living begins.

I live for myself 
and for you too,
because, somewhere beyond regret,
I know, you always had my back.

With all my love,
Your daughter!

poem1_theme: Tangled Knots explores the paradox of loving someone who unsettles you—when closeness feels heavy, distance feels painful, and the heart remains caught in between.
poem1_content:
To love you is my only sin—
A turmoil, I can never win.
The distance in between unsettles me,
Nearness disorients me, let me be.

When you're here, I try to avoid—
Your presence turns me paranoid.
Your absence, too, hurts me a lot,
And here I stand with tangled knots.

I try and try, I always try—
An unanswered try leads me to sigh.
Despite our trying, love is lost—
No matter the sun, feelings remain frost.
© hudabilal

यह दिल भी एक घर है जो बनता प्यार भरे,
रिश्तों की ईंटों से मज़बूत होता रहता हर दिन,
अपनेपन से भरी बातों व शब्दों के छींटों से,
लेकिन क्या होता जब यह दिल का घर अचानक से टुटकर बिखर जाता,
समझ आया दिल को की वो रिश्ता महज़,
एक धोखा हर शब्द जो उस मुंह से निकला,
वो झुटा व फरेब था भरा हुआ,
दिल के दरवाज़े पर जो बजी थी घंटी,
खोल दिया था दरवाज़ा आज उसके लिए,
करने दिया उससे इस दिल में प्रवेश,
लेकिन जैसे वो अंदर आया उसके कदमों से,
पड़ गया इस जिंदगी में क्लेश,
ऐसा किया दिल में बसे प्यार का इस्तेमाल,
अब क्या करुं कितने करुं मैं उससे सवाल,
बस बचा रही हुं दिल को अपने और,
ज़्यादा टुटने से,
यह दिल कि इमारत लगता जैसे भुकंप से,
पीड़ित है,
देखा है मैंने मौहबबत को नफ़रत के बिस्तर में,
सोते हुए आईने में देखा खुद को मैंने,
रात -दिन रोते हुए,
चला गया वो दरवाज़ा खोलकर आंखों में,
धुल झोंककर,
क्यों उससे गले लगाया मैंने सब कुछ,
छोड़कर,
बेवफ़ाई समझ में आई अपने आपको,
अंधकार की भुलभुलिया में खोकर।

poem1_theme: Life and relationships through the vision of visually impaired
poem1_content:
तुझे आंखों में भर लेती, जो इन आंखों में नज़र होती,
तुझे नजरों से पढ़  लेती, जो इन आंखों में नज़र होती।
जहां के लाख हसी नज़ारों में, ग़र किसी नज़ारे पे नज़र होती,
महज़ तुझ पर नज़र होती जो इन आंखों में नज़र होती।

तुझे देखता, तो देखता ही जाता, जो इन आंखों में नज़र होती,
् तू मेरे लिए संवर्ती, मैं तुझे देख  गुनगुनाता जो इन आंखों में नज़र होती।


तेरी मासूमियत की पहरेदारी फ़र्ज़ है मेरा,
हर एक बुरी नज़र को मैं अपनी नज़रों से हटाता, जो इन आंखों में नज़र होती।

कोई रास्ता, कोई मंच, मुझसे न छूटता, जो तेरी आंखों में नज़र होती,
महसूस कर ज़माने की अनदेखी को खुद से यूं न मैं रूठता, जो तेरी आंखों में नज़र होती।
तेरी नजरों से खुद को देख कर, महसूस मैं करती,
कुदरत  कि इन रंगीनियों से मेरा नाता क्यों टूटता, जो तेरी आंखों में नज़र होती।

किसी को मुश्किलों में देख, क्यों हम छोड़ कर जाते अपनी आंखों में जो नज़र होती,
हज़ार मौकों पर सबको देख कर, क्यों खुद को बहलाते अपनी आंखों में जो नज़र होती।
नजरों के कई मसले कभी जो हल नहीं होते,
सब मसले ही न सुलझ जाते, जो अपनी  आंखों में नज़र होती।

Love,
How should I start?

Well—
I may say the uncertainty principle of us.
They say love is measurable,
like
a wavelength stretched between two hearts,
a force proportional to trust,
a vector pointing towards infinity.

But this is not all real.

I know how even the smallest shift
like
a whisper, a glance, a pause too long
can alter the equation,
tilt the balance,
leave me solving for x
in a problem with no answer.

If I describe you,
how should I?

You are the sun,
pulling everything towards you,
a center of warmth, light, and laughter.

Not just as simple as that.
Not just for me.

I, in your orbit,
trace the same ellipses,
never quite close enough,
always fearing the moment
when your gravity weakens,
when another stronger force calls you away.

And you know me
and my friends—
they are planets too,
shining in their own ways,
their laughter like distant constellations of sky.

How do I explain this burning feeling in my chest?

I pretend not to notice
when you turn towards them,
when their light outshines mine.

But inside,
I am maybe a collapsed star,
holding too much weight in too little space,
swallowing my own worth
without making a sound.

I know I’m different,
but not an outcast.
I don’t know.

How do I describe this feeling?

Every morning,
I stand before the mirror,
measuring myself against reflections
I was never meant to be.

A paradox—
to love you,
yet doubt that love could belong to me.

I watch how they make you laugh,
how effortlessly they exist,
while I calculate every step,
plot every move,
like a philosopher questioning reality itself.

If I cannot prove my love,
does it exist at all?

Or is it just another illusion,
a trick of perspective, you know
like
a shadow cast by brighter flames?

There’s a quote written
on the wall of my room:

It all starts with a friction.

I don’t know
how to explain.

Jealousy is like force
a drag against movement,
slowing the trust I try to build.

And the numbers do not add up.

Are we still us,
or just a fraction
of what we used to be?

I try to reason with the logic of love,
but love does not obey equations.

It’s not logic.
It is irrational, like pi—
infinite, ungraspable,
a series of digits stretching beyond
what I can hold.

And so I am left with friction,
with the slow erosion
wondering if I will wear down to nothing.

One sentence, misplaced,
and the whole structure shudder.

A sigh.
A silence.
A hesitation
before you answer.

Each one a ripple,
a butterfly’s wingbeat
that could summon storms.

I try to silence the voices in my head
you know,
it’s not easy
the ones that tell me
you will wake up one day
and realize I am not enough.

But how do you stop
an avalanche
once the first stone falls?

And yet, despite it all—
despite the gravity,
the friction,
the variables I cannot solve
I choose to stay.

Why?

Because love, for all its flaws,
for all its shifting equations,
is still the only law
I want to obey.

Because it’s love.

I don’t feel defeated.

Because
even the weakest light
can defy the darkness.

You know what I mean.

And even a collapsed star,
for all its silence,
was once burning.

And maybe, just maybe,
if I can hold onto that truth
because I know everything,
the dreams,
the reality
if I can learn to believe
in the constancy of you,
then I won’t need
to measure love anymore.

I will simply feel it.
That’s love.

What an unforgiving winter has this been,
every ache my heart has seen.
Our love flourishes on the screen,
stark against the white snow lush and green.

Yet behind the shut doors it fails;
in bleak silence my heart wails.
I am right here before your eyes,
yet to my face-your gaze fails to rise.

No warmth, no wave of love,
cold hail hitting from above,
washing me against my will
as I hold on, leaning against the window sill.

Standing in the light, at least see me now-
my screams have failed me somehow.
I am sick of decorating the cracks in my heart;
I want to shatter it-tear it apart.

I turn away to swallow the words untold,
finally accepting our love couldn’t survive the cold.
This winter has been unforgivingly cold-
a love so fresh is now just rotten and old.

Thousands of unspoken thoughts,
Found their way on a piece of paper.
The moment it got inked with emotions,
They started calling it a letter.
Although it knew its destination,
Yet lingered somewhere in the path.
Attempted countless times to step ahead,
But refrained cuz it feared facing the wrath.
It wasn't any ordinary script,
Instead it carried some quiet confession.
The kind which wasn't to be heard beyond the core,
Only meant to be hearts secret possession.
At the dawn of my writing,
It was crafted to let my inner world unfold.
Yet time and again I rekindle through it's end,
To refresh the yesterday it holds.

Time rushes past, each second gnawing for an answer I cannot give. Through the window, trees wave for me to join them. If only I could, I would have, long ago.
In that moment, I wonder what is holding me back.
Why am I so afraid to love?
Millions of thoughts flood in, choking me. My throat tightens– angry at my indifference– as hot trails of despair run down my cheeks, singing a symphony that breaks the heart. I never received a flower, I saw myself as barren, not worth cultivating. 
And so, I never learned when to water my own garden– or someone else’s. 
Snapped out of my reverie, I realize the next stop is mine. In a hurry, I grab everything and reach for the exit. Beside it stands a mounted mirror– its surface blurry, reflecting only my outline, reminding me of the hollowness inside, gnawing from within. 
I lied– I always do.
I am desperate for a hug, a kiss and a man who loves me for who I am.
I want to be held close. 
I want to be loved.
I want to be someone’s lover.
Inside the cab, I look out and soliloquize again. I’m grown now, but the leaves don’t shine green. I’m tall now, but I don’t feel strong. I breathe, yet I long for life. If we were seasons, I would be winter– stripped of warmth, a remnant of what once lived. 
And if I am winter, how could I ever teach spring to bloom?

poem1_theme: A story about love that endures loss and learns to survive without warmth.
poem1_content:
We had learned love in classrooms and canteens, in borrowed notes and shared silences. Back then, life felt obedient. Plans stayed where we placed them.

When we married, it felt like the world had finally agreed with us. Six months later, it changed its mind.

He was sent to the Jammu–Kashmir border. He said it gently, as if distance would hurt less that way. I nodded, because I had always known this about him-that loving the country was stitched into his spine. I never asked him to choose between us. Some loves don’t need proof.

On my birthday, the call came.

I don’t remember falling. I remember sitting on the floor long after everyone else stood. Someone kept speaking, but the words refused to arrange themselves into meaning. All I knew was that he would never come back.

They brought his body home wrapped in honour. I shook him once, then again-harder than I should have.

“Dev, tum naraz ho kya mujhse?”
“Baat karo na.”
“Tumne bola tha hamesha mere saath rahoge.”

A month later, a letter arrived. He had written it long before, when the nights were colder and the future uncertain. He told me to live well, to be careful with strangers, to not disappear into grief. He wrote about duty and love as if they could stand side by side without breaking.

Tum meri rooh thi, he wrote.
Aur hamesha rahogi.

That morning, the temperature in Kashmir was minus one degree. I remember noticing it because, for the first time, the cold outside matched the quiet inside me.

I still talk to him sometimes. Not because I expect answers, but because love does not end with death. It learns a colder way to exist.

I live now-carefully, quietly-the way he asked me to. And some days, surviving feels like the last promise I have left to keep.
poem2_content:
We talked
as if time were generous,
as if tomorrow were promised.

We stopped
without knowing
it was the last time
our words would reach each other.

The mountains kept breathing.
They always do.

At -1°C,
the cold does not scream.
It only stays.

Snow fell that night,
soft enough to forgive,
heavy enough to keep
what it covered.

Beneath it,
two bodies rested,
hands drawn close to the chest,
as if instinct still believed
for warmth.

“Bas thoda aur ruk jaate.”
“Ek baat aur kehni thi.”

No farewell survived.
No final sentence.
Only love, unfinished,
settling into silence.

The snow wrapped them gently,
the way we never learned to-
holding without asking,
staying without fear of leaving.

At −1°C,
nothing freezes completely.

Somewhere beneath the snow,
two hands still remember
what it meant
to reach
for each other.

poem1_content: Lingi lingi lingi

I once loved you the way my hunger fancies the bone,
Quiet at first, with the appetite unknown. 
It knocked so softly at my guarded chest,
For I let it in, believing this destiny knew what's best.

You came to me with your emptiness in tow,
Calling it fate, the only desire was for you to show. 
You asked me to Sty, and I mistook the sound,
For Choose me, for a love that never wanted to be found.

You fed on all the silence, for as long as I could hold,
My patience stretched, on warmth I gave you bold. 
I mapped your wounds like war countries I could save, 
Like a fool in love,
I mistook this repair, the strength for brave.

Each time you broke me, I offered up my skin,
A quiet sacrifice, I thought so as a win. 
You wore my giving like a second name,
And left me bearing all the weight of blame.

You fancied me like a note, I know you'd never keep,
You vanished so gently, careful not to seep. 
You engulfed my softness, said it might return,
As if my heart was something that won't really burn.

Still I stayed, believing love is yet another name for ache,
That endurance was the vow, I still yearned to make. 
If I somehow left, who'd hold your splintered core? 
If not the one you've already torn before?

But hear me now, the truth I trust,
The love that feeds one mouth is only lust. 
The consumption dressed in sacred speech,
Devotion used as something you'd leech. 

I'm not bitter, just clearer in my blood,
I refuse to confuse your need with being loved. 
I shall not lay my rhythm down at starving feet,
And call the loss romantic or even remotely complete. 

And if I love again, it surely won't be this way, 
No more silent theft, no slow, and devouring stay. 
Just two starving souls learning how to give, 
Leaving the diner whole, the free will to live. 

And if you wonder why I don't taste the same, 
Acknowledge that I left, before I lost my name. 
I learned what Love should never, ever be,
When I finally stopped letting love feed on me.

poem1_theme: An elegy for a former self, centered on identity loss, longing, and emotional alienation.
poem1_content:
I look in the mirror and ask, “Who is he?”
For none of his traits or emotions feel like me.

I am strong, I am smart, and I try to be brave.
He is timid and weak — love’s all that he craves.

In heartbreak or failure, all he does is cry.
Not me — I stand again, give it one more try.

He is kind, he is gentle, with love left to share.
I lack those now, perhaps I've become too aware.

He feels like apricity, warm even in despair,
While I curse the past, asking, Was any of it fair?

I ache with hiraeth — a home I can’t name or reclaim,
A longing for someone I was, and no longer remain.

He danced with tarantism, loved petrichor’s call,
I’m weary, wearing faces, a hidden core and all.

He’d twattle through moments, eager to roam,
I lie like a clinomaniac, dreading existence itself as home.

All there is left in me—
Are the pieces of a person I used to be...

But I won't let anyone ever see,
Since he died long ago, and must be left as a forgotten memory.

So yes, I face the mirror and ask, “Who is he?”
And though I hate to admit it — I wish, like him, I could be.

The streets still lead forward,
but they no longer lead to you.
I know the season has passed,
but I still remember the warmth.
Leaves crunch beneath my feet,
but not in the same way.

There I go again, in the same coordinates of feeling,
back to the time when our mouths learned each other,
when the night let us believe in forever.
It’s all still alive, in some corners of me.

You showed me your family pictures on your laptop screen,
To the time when I entered your life without knocking.
Any second now, you might name it love,
but love was always unspoken,
to the point where what we had started to die.

There I go again, in the same loop of trying,
back to the time when we danced in the dark,
when the night let me believe it was real.
It’s all still alive, in some corners of me.

Maybe you loved the idea of me,
and maybe not the person speaking.
Maybe I was too soft for how loudly you lived.
Maybe it was the depth that frightened you.
Maybe I was too scared to tell you the truth,
because maybe I knew you were going to leave anyway.
And maybe what we had was the best
until you declared it wouldn’t work out.

There I go again, in the same phase of crying,
back to the time when you tried to justify all your doings,
to make me believe it was for the greater good.
It’s all still alive, in some corners of me.

I’ve safely buried love six feet under.
As they say, loving is an act of surrender.
Maybe this love was never meant to find you,
never meant to trace your face
with all the gentleness of the world.
Maybe love is blue.
Maybe amnesia would heal me just fine.

There I go again, in the same death of dying,
back to the time when I decided to never see you again.
I’ve written my last letter, and these are my last verses for you.
It’s all still alive, in some corners of me.

●When we were together then there were three things-a room, us and our love.
But now we are not together and still there are three things-it's me, Walls and Books.
◦The room isn't a room anymore that is only a bunch of Walls.
◦Love is still there but that love isn't for you anymore it's for the books.
◦The happiness of looking at you isn't possible anymore...but cinema always gives me glimpse of yours.
◦If something isn't anymore that is us because 'us' replaced with 'me'
'We' got split with 'I' and 'you'

●There was time when you held my tears, you wiped them and you made me happy again
◦Still there are walls who try to hold my tears, 
◦There are books who try to wipe my tears,
◦There is cinema who makes me happy all the time.

●There was a Spark when I used to see you everytime
◦And now that Spark is converted into a darkness which may take years to go away.
◦There was a Time when your hands were in my hands 
But how ironic is this...our hands are still full but 
◦yours with someone other's hands and hands of mine are filled with scars, emptiness and grief.

●When you were with me what actually I had and after seperation I lost-
◦A happy mood which converted into a gloomy mood,
◦An inner child which got butchered brutally,
◦very good nights which got replaced with sleepless cage,
◦A charming nature which got changed with a unbreakable silence,
◦A glowing face which turned into a mirror with lots of dust,
◦Two sparkin eyes and they got became black holes,
◦A conciousness which has been snatched away,
◦Two beautiful hands and now they became trembling non living gloves.

●Don't you remember
we used to gazing the stars at every night for hours with our endless talks
But it's been years they got stop coming in my village.
We used to walk through the our favourite paths 
All the plants and flowers blinked their eyes when they watched our flipped warm hands 
Now
When I go the same paths they didn't even look at me their heads are down like the are in condolences.

poem1_theme: 'Evolution of love',Being loved is a blessing and a girl hated by society in the poem experience the miracle of love,A loved women shows an evolution ; In past tense a retrospection! Her tale interfere the poet's (my) writing hour.
poem1_content:
RETROSPECTION OF LOVE 
: Nikhasab.s

Once lived 'a rotten platonic lover' in her erotic frenzy 
Her toiled brain did boiled in wild coy of  trigger

Unbother; Its just a teen tremor and she's holy fine 
Unchained lucid dreams ate her in midnights

Hoofbeats of horses thundered  the arrival of a prince
A prayer so high from a mosque claimed her eardrums

She let the cathartic envy enter and met with her royal depression
Then she saw a tint of loyal love ; naivest ever!

Heavy; when she came in my poems and compel me to pen 'her life'
Words shrinking in my paper And you will never ever know why!

Until and Unless I get my soul back from this retrospect 
I will compile my insomnias into poetries for her!

poem1_theme: The poem is about a girl who always gave love without asking for anything back. She trusted someone deeply but was hurt when that trust was broken. In the process, she lost herself and her sense of who she was. Through time alone and quiet reflection, she slowly found herself again, learned her own value, and grew stronger without losing her gentle heart.
poem1_content:
WHAT REMAINS AFTER LOVE?

She learned early how to give
without ever asking for return.
At home, care came on time—
meals, lessons, folded routines—
yet love rarely spoke her language.
So she mistook quiet for closeness
and provision for warmth.

She looked beyond those walls—
first in laughter, then in loyalty.
One presence felt steady,
a space where she could lean.
She trusted him with hours,
with unguarded words,
with corners of herself she never named.

Time thinned her quietly,
days slipped past unnoticed.
When truth cracked, she held his name
like fragile glass, defending it still.
The lie came once.
Then again.

What shattered her was not loss,
but how easily trust could be bent.
Friends faded, time dissolved,
and somewhere along the way,
she forgot who she had been.

Walking away was her quiet courage.
She pieced herself together,
learning the strength of solitude.
Her heart remains gentle—
but her self-worth no longer bends.
poem2_theme: The poem is about learning to love yourself after searching for validation from others. It shows how we often give our hearts to people or places that cannot truly see us, and in the process, lose parts of ourselves. Through patience, reflection, and self-kindness, the speaker discovers that real love comes from within. The poem celebrates self-acceptance, inner strength, and the quiet power of knowing your own worth.
poem2_content:
THE PLACE I CARRY ME

I used to look for love in other hands,
in voices that promised warmth,
in smiles that faded too soon.

I forgot the quiet voice inside,
the one that whispered, “You are enough.”
Days passed chasing echoes,
and I lost pieces of myself
to the weight of wanting to be seen.

Slowly, I began to turn home—
not to walls or faces,
but to the skin I live in,
to the heart that never left me,
to the small, steady pulse that knows my name.

I learned to hold my own hand,
to speak kindly to my reflection,
to forgive myself for every fault,
to celebrate the courage in each breath.

Love returned—not from someone else,
but from me,
quiet and unshakable,
anchoring my soul
in the place I carry me.

poem1_theme: Vulnerability, Love, Heartbreak, Self-discovery, Raw, Poignant, Hopeful
poem1_content:
Fragmere
 -Srijita Maiti                                                      
I’ve been the one who stays too long, 
Calling almost-love my favourite song. 
I build forever in passing time
Then act surprised when they don’t align. 
	
I learn the language of someone’s eyes
How silence bends, how goodbye lies.
I give my light to rooms half-lit
Call it love…then drown in it.

I trace their shadows along the walls,
Hear their laughter through empty halls.
I count the cracks where light once bled
And press my heart to every word unsaid.

I love like fire that learns no fear
I burn too bright, I stay too near. 
I make a home out of borrowed ground
Standing alone when it’s burning down.
	
They say stay, their voices sweet as sin
Then vanish quietly, leaving me within.
I cradle the echo, they hold the keys
I count the heartbeat…they drift with ease.

I stitch my heart with fragile thread
Convince myself I misheard what they said.
I wait on nights that never ring
Like hope is a stubborn, foolish thing.

I give my all in quiet ways
In folded prayers, in endless stays.
They take the warmth, they take the light
Then exit clean into someone else’s life.

And still, I rise from the wreck I know
With careful steps, but eyes still slow.
I don’t love less; I just love wise
I read the truth before the lies.

I keep my softness, not my chains
I learn from loss, I name the pain.
I walk away when love feels thin
But never punish my heart for feeling.

And if I bleed, let it be proof
That I lived uncovered, not bulletproof.
I’d rather ache than never feel
I’d rather break than never be real.

Love began like an easy day,
No hurry, no fear, no plan in sight;
Two hearts met along the way,
And everything felt simple, light.

Time grew deep in common hours,
Shared laughs, long talks, familiar air;
Hope lived in ordinary powers,
Trust slept quietly everywhere.

Mistakes came without a sound,
No loud fights, no burning flame;
Just small doubts moving around,
Till closeness didn’t feel the same.

The story ended without a sound,
No final talk, no last reply;
Days kept moving all around,
Life went on, so did the sky.

Time passed like it always does,
Faces changed, routines grew new;
Memories faded, words became dust,
Most moments slipped right through.

But the hug did not leave with time,
It stayed behind, learned how to wait;
Quietly crossed every moving line,
Refusing to accept its fate.

It returned at random hours,
In empty rooms, in tired nights;
Not as pain, not as flowers,
But as a weight that feels just right.

No voice came with that memory,
No face, no fight, no tears;
Just arms remembering honesty,
From a life that once lived here.

That hug asks nothing, proves no love,
Doesn’t beg the past to stay;
It only reminds the heart of
What was real in its own way.

The story is done, nothing remains,
No hope, no anger, no demand;
Yet memory still gently claims—
The hug understood, when hearts couldn’t stand.
poem2_content:
Her laughter echoes in these empty halls,
Whispers of yesterday linger and call.
Pictures frozen in a frame so clear,
Her voice in the wind still brings her near.

Memories painted in shades of blue,
Every corner here reminds me of you.
The silent nights whisper her name,
In this old world that feels the same.

How do I let go when she’s everywhere,
Her shadow’s in the room but she’s never there.
Fighting to breathe in this haunted space,
Her presence lingers like she’s in this place.

Her scent lingers on the clothes she wore,
Every heartbeat feels like it’s for her.
The ghost of love dances in my sight,
Turning day into a sleepless night.

Every step I take pulls me back to when,
Her touch was more than just pretend.
But she’s gone and I’m still here,
Holding on to phantoms I can’t clear.

poem1_theme: Break up and lost faith in love
poem1_content:
A girl with a wounded heart
never learns the language of lies.
She believes—too purely, too deeply-
first in her parents,
whose love should have been shelter,
but became distance, silence,
and lessons wrapped in womanhood’s weight.
She grows up early,
learning to swallow pain with grace,
learning that affection can be withheld
without explanation.
Still, she believes.
Because hope is the only inheritance
she was never taught to abandon.
Then love arrives—
soft as butterflies,
disguised in human form,
calling itself soulmate.
For a while, it heals what family forgot,
touches the bruises gently,
makes her think the universe finally chose her.
But even butterflies leave.
Even promises learn to break.
And the same hands that held her fears
become the ones that walk away.
Not everyone hurts.
Not everyone leaves.
But someone does—
and that someone is enough
to reopen every unhealed place.
So she stands alone again,
not because she is weak,
but because she loved honestly
in a world that keeps mistaking
sincerity for something expendable.

A relationship should feel like walking into spring, 
even when the world is still winter.
Like hands that do not just hold you,
but keep your fears from falling.
It should feel like silence that knows how to sing,
like laughter that learns your heartbeat,
like being chosen in a room full of forever-maybes,
every single day.
In an ideal love, you are not the question,
you are an answer.
Your flaws are not shadows,
they are soft places someone rests their trust in.
A relationship should be built like the sky,
wide enough for both to breathe,
strong enough to hold storms,
gentle enough to carry dreams.
It should feel glamorous not in photographs,
but in the way two souls guard each other’s peace.
Soft, yet certain.
Quiet, yet unbreakable.
Love should never feel like weight.
It should feel like light —
like tea waiting at dusk,
like a name that feels like prayer.
An ideal relationship is not about being perfect.
It is about two imperfect hearts
choosing each other
until ordinary days learn to call themselves forever.
poem2_content:
A relationship should feel like coming home,
not like proving your worth.
Like hands that don’t just hold you,
but understand you.
It should feel like shared silence
that are never awkward,
like inside jokes that become memories,
like being chosen — quietly, every day.
In an ideal love, you don’t fear being replaced,
you feel irreplaceable.
You don’t hide your flaws,
you watch someone love you even more because of them.
A relationship should be built from trust —
the kind that lets you breathe,
the kind that doesn’t check your phone,
but checks your heart.
It should feel soft, yet strong.
Glamorous, not in pictures,
but in the way two souls protect each other’s peace.
Love should not feel heavy.
It should feel like light —
like laughter in tired evenings,
like support in quiet breakdowns.
An ideal relationship is not about perfection.
It is about two imperfect people
who choose each other,
and make forever feel gentle.

poem1_theme: Letting go without noise, dignity after pain, choosing peace over conflict, silent healing, rediscovering self-worth.
poem1_content:
The biggest revenge you can take after a breakup is not confrontation, not explanations, not proving who was right or wrong. It is moving away—quietly. No questions. No dramatic exits. Just silence. Silence is powerful because it denies chaos the attention it craves.
When you stop asking why, you reclaim your energy. When you stop explaining your pain, you protect your dignity. Silence is not weakness; it is self-control in its purest form. It says, I owe you nothing anymore—not even my words.
Move on as smoothly as if you were never hurt. Not because you weren’t broken, but because you chose healing over performance. You don’t need to announce your growth, your recovery, or your happiness. Let it happen privately. Let it bloom where no one is watching.
What makes this the strongest form of revenge is its psychological weight. The other person begins to wonder. They question their importance in your life. They replay moments, searching for signs of damage they expected to cause—but don’t see. Your calm confuses them more than anger ever could. Your peace becomes the unanswered question they carry.
Happiness lived quietly is unsettling to those who expected you to shatter. When you don’t beg, don’t explain, and don’t react, you break the cycle. You close the chapter without tearing the pages.
A breakup doesn’t need a battlefield. It needs distance. Growth doesn’t need an audience. It needs space. And revenge doesn’t need cruelty—it needs grace.
So walk away softly. Heal deeply. Live fully. Let your silence speak for you, and let your happiness be the proof that nothing destroyed you.
poem2_theme: Reflection of strength, control and dignity.
poem2_content:
I didn’t leave with words to fight,
I didn’t beg, I didn’t stay.
I packed my hurt in softer hands
And let the noise fall away.
No why’s, no how’s, no last demand,
No tears put up for show.
I chose the art of stepping back,
The grace of letting go.
Silence isn’t empty air,
It hums with steady breath.
It’s strength that learns to stand upright
In the quiet after death.
I moved like I was never cracked,
Smooth as a passing tide,
Not to lie about the pain—
But to keep my soul intact.
You look for proof I didn’t heal,
A wreck, a trace, a sign.
But all you meet is calm restraint
And a life that’s doing fine.
My joy makes no announcement,
My peace won’t argue back.
It simply lives, it simply grows
Along an unshared track.
This is no revenge of rage,
No bitter parting cry.
The loudest win I ever claimed
Was learning how to fly—
Without your name upon my lips,
Without your weight on me.
I left in silence, held my worth—
And chose my own victory.

poem1_theme: A memory of love that blossomed in winters and stayed forever
poem1_content:
Those were a couple of lovely hours 
Charming, cheerful, cherished 
A part of that night got engraved in my heart 
And a face that will forever be missed 
The smiles will shine in flashback 
The laughter will echo in my ears 
The twinkle lights will shimmer 
That Christmas tree will never wither 
The smell of the coffee you made 
That fragrance will never fade 
In that wonderful picture
We shall always remain together 
Our smiles will never dwindle 
The empty lanes I walked with you 
The twinkling stars, the crescent moon 
The smell of the mist, the falling dew 
Will keep haunting me at times 
With its winsome rhymes
The breeze that felt colder than ever 
Will pass by me, whispering a forever
Sending chills, freezing my soul 
The flickering of the streetlamps
Will always reminisce this walk 
You came and you left 
Leaving a myriad of memories 
And from now on, everything I see 
Around me 
Will remind me 
Of that misty winter night 
Of those empty lanes 
Of the walk 
Of the smiles 
Of the laughter 
And of you 
From now on, December will never be the same 
It will be about you 
Remembering you 
Missing you 
Everyday 
Every moment 
And how, just in a couple of hours 
It became too painful to let you go.

poem1_theme: प्यार , अपनापन या प्रयासों का दुःख
poem1_content:
एक चाय का प्याला 
एक शख़्स है जिसे चाय और चाय बनाना दोनों बेहद पसन्द है , कितनी इलायची , कितना अदरक , हर मसाले की सटीक खबर है । चाय में शक्कर न कम न ज्यादा रखती है , उबलते चाय की अफ़नाहट में खयालों का हिसाब थोड़ा आधा रखती है । हमेशा कहती है कि उसे खुद के हाथों की चाय ही भाती है , पर उसकी आँखें कहती है कि दूसरे के हाथ की चाय का वो कभी मौका ही नहीं पाती है । पर क्या हो अगर , क्या हो अगर एक दिन उस शख्स को कोई और एक प्याले भर चाय का प्रस्ताव दे , वो बैठे सुकून से  , और मध्यम आंच में पक रही चाय बेफिक्र हो निहारने का उसे भी मौका मिले । आज चाय की उबाल को नजरअंदाज कर वो गुम हो अपने ही खयालों में , कहने की आज़ादी संग सुनने की क्षमता हो आज दोनों तरफ के प्यालों में । उसे भी मिले वक्त पकाने का भोज - सिर्फ बातों का , चाय की गुड़ गुड़ में उसे जवाब मिले अपने भीतर के सारे सवालों का , और आज उसे भी  हो एक नया अनुभव और ये पहचान करने का मौका मिले कि कोई और भी पका सकता है उसके जैसा या उससे भी बेहतरीन चाय । और , अंतिम बात ये कि यहां बात सिर्फ एक प्याले चाय की हो ही नहीं रही । बात है प्रेम की , दो लोगों के प्रयासों के फर्क की । प्यार लूटाने वाले वाले भी प्यार पाने की आस में होते है , सबकी सुनते समझते जब थक जाते है , वो भी थोड़ा समझे जाना चाहते है ।
~ एक हाथ में चाय और दूसरे में कलम के साथ , यश शास्त्री
poem2_content:
सुनो , प्रिय 
यूं ही नहीं मनुष्य सहसा बदल सा जाता है 
तुम अनदेखा कर देते हो ... 
तुम अनदेखा कर देते हो , 
जब दर्द हजार सहकर भी वो अपनापन जताता है 
पीड़ा पूर्ण छुपाकर वो प्रेम बेहिसाब लुटाता है ।
तुम्हारी बुराइयों पर पर्दा डाल स्वयं को गर्व से तुम्हारा बताता है
फिर भी न जाने क्यूं वो रातों में खुद को तन्हा पाता है ।
फिर एक दिन ... 
उसको घूट घूट जीता देख वक्त स्वयं एक बदलाव लाता है 
काल्पनिकता से बाहर खींच उसे
वास्तविकता का सामना कराता है । 
थक के घुटने पे गिरने वाला अब संभालना सीख जाता है
तुम पर अशर्त मरने वाला तुम बिन जीने लग जाता है 
मगर ! क्या तुम जानते हो , 
बाहर से खिलखिलाता वो अंदर से तन्हा मरता है 
वो मृत आत्मा ले मात्र जीवित काया रह जाता है । ~ यश शास्त्री

poem1_content:
जब उसे देखा तो देखकर पता लगा  
कि सुकून किसे कहते हैं  
खुशी के आँसुओं की वो बूँद किसे कहते हैं।  
जब मैं पहली बार उससे मिला था  
तो हमने तो ढंग से नज़रें भी नहीं मिलाई थीं,  
लेकिन मैं जान गया था  
कि कजरारे नैन किसे कहते हैं।  
इश्क़, मोहब्बत, प्यार…  
अरे जाओ, बहुत छोटी बातें हैं ये।  
उसे देखकर जाना था कि  
दिनभर अकेले बैठने के बावजूद भी  
चेहरे पर आने वाली वो मुस्कान किसे कहते हैं।  
अरे ना देखी मेरी तरफ़, ना मुस्कुराई थी वो,  
लेकिन कोई पल तो था  
जिसमें मेरी तरफ़ देखकर  
शायद कुछ तो बुदबुदाई थी वो।  
उसकी नज़रें मुझ पर एक बार तो रुकी थीं,  
मुझे शायद याद नहीं  
पर मुस्कुराई भी थी वो।  
उसकी सादगी उसकी आँखों से झलकती थी,  
उसकी सुंदरता तो उसकी बोली में बसती थी।  
और क्या कहूँ मैं उसके बारे में…  
हज़ारों भीड़ में भी  
मेरी आँखें तो उसी पर टिकती थीं।  
और ये मोहब्बत इतनी है उनसे कि अगर  
ना भी मिली वो तो उन्हें नहीं भूलाऊंगा।  
बल्कि ना मिली तो कृष्ण सा बन जाऊँगा,  
उसे राधा सा मान दिल में बसाऊँगा।  
प्यार तो उनका भी पवित्र था ना।  
उसे भाग्य में ना लिखा तो क्या हुआ,  
मैं तो उसे खुश देखकर ही  
खुशी से झूम जाऊँगा।  
पर बस मैं उसे नहीं भूलाऊंगा।।

Within The Envelope


A delirium again? With more blissful delight,
silence reverberations filling out the boundless heart,
although an oversleep eyes, needs no an overwipe.

Counting fingers as an art of console,
never beloved,never unloved me and my alone soul.

Never got a welcome as bright as dawn,
Just being an idle absent-minded;
instead remains untold goodbies ,
after the dusk is gone.

A rock with a patience heart,never becomes a part of art,
a dawn which is gone always makes a picturesque strong.

Whatever so I wrote, my ink begs for happiness,
sometimes if I don’t,the day leads to emptiness.

poem1_theme: Honour killings as systemic violence — as socially sanctioned brutality carried out in the name of tradition.
poem1_content:
“They Called It Honour”

Radha loved like a quiet prayer,
 spoken with trembling hands, knowing devotion can be dangerous.
He loved like a steady flame—
 not loud, not reckless,
 but foolish enough to believe love could outwalk fear.

Their love lived just long enough to be seen,
 just long enough to be judged,
 just long enough to be punished.

Then came the gathering.

 A circle of elders closed in—
 centuries crouched in their voices,
 authority borrowed from the dead.
 No blade at first.
 Only lineage.
 Only caste pronounced like law.

 Only the ancient lie that honour weighs more than a woman’s pulse.
Radha’s name became a verdict.
 Her choice became a crime.
 Her body, the proof.

 They called it necessity.
 They called it tradition.
 They said love had crossed a line
 drawn long before she was born.

He watched helpless— every word a blow,
every silence a noose tightening.
In his chest, something split open and stayed that way.
A space forever reserved for the woman he loved and could not save.

Radha did not scream.
 She understood mercy would not arrive.
 That honour kills patiently,
 convincing itself before it destroys….

They twisted it slowly.
Life left her inch by inch.
When she was gone, they wiped their hands clean and called it justice.
poem2_content:
“This Valley, This Love”
Iqbal and Zainab sat on their usual weathered bench near the Lidder River, watching the water rush past with practiced urgency. Across the meadow, ponies grazed, bells chiming softly, while tourists drifted in clusters—cameras raised, wonder briefly borrowed. A hawker passed, paper pinwheels spinning wildly in the cold mountain breeze.
“People think Pashminas are light because they look soft,” Zainab said, her fingers tracing a folded fringe. “They don’t know how heavy it feels—to carry them all day, to bargain, to smile, to stand invisible by the river.”
“Ponywallahs are the same,” Iqbal replied. “We are only noticed when the ride is too slow or the hill too steep.”
They spoke, as they often did, of the first time they met—her anger at a cheating tourist, his quiet readiness to intervene. They laughed at shared tea in chipped cups, at dreams once spoken easily: a bigger stall, a second pony, a warm room for winter.
Then came 22 April 2025.
The valley had felt uneasy that morning. Iqbal was ferrying four tourists—two women and a middle-aged couple—when gunfire split the air. Panic tore through Pahalgam. Before fear could settle, Iqbal dragged them off the path, forced them behind a rocky embankment near the river, his body shielding theirs.
“Do not move,” he whispered, steady despite the thunder in his chest.
When silence returned, twenty-six civilians were dead.
That evening, Zainab found him by the river, clothes streaked with mud and blood that was not his. She touched his face, shaking. “You could have died.”
“So could they,” he said simply.
After that, tourists vanished. Fear replaced footfall. Zainab sold no shawls. Iqbal sold his pony. Loss came in layers—money, sleep, certainty.
One dusk, on the same bench, he told her he was leaving. His father was ill. The valley had taken enough.
“Take me with you,” she said, tears breaking free. “Staying is uncertain too.”
As the Lidder rushed on, carrying grief downstream, Iqbal took her hand. In a land that bled often, they chose love—quietly, fiercely—knowing that here, love had always been an act of courage.

poem1_content: ssss

Title: ​"Shikast-e-Muhabbat-e-ek Tarfa" 

Poet: Aks–e–Aziz.

​Kiyu! Kahan? Kon hai wo shakhs?

Jiski wafa ka tum dawa kiya karte the?

Shikasta-par!! Shikast–e–rang!! Shikast–e–dil!! Kis liye hai,

Lagta hai faqat tumhi muhabbat kiya karte the,

Shikast–e–khwaab!! Shikast–e–qeemat!! Shikast–e–faahish ho chuke ho,

Na kisi se na ussey na khud se,

Shikwa karna bhi toh khuda se,

Isme tumhari koi khata toh nahin,

Magar tum nafs aur khuda hi jaane,

Ham is mein kuch nahin kar sakte hain,

Ziyada se ziyada kiya?

Sukoon–e–dil ki dua hi maang sakte hain,

Tum shikast–e–faash ho chuke ho,

Ab kiya hi kar sakte hain

poem1_theme: Authenticity vs appearance, emotional masks, truth beneath perfection
poem1_content:
It seems so easy to fake everything...
like placing plastic flowers in a vase,
They’ll never wither, never fall apart,
always perfect, always smiling.
But the truth is,
they carry no scent, no soul.
They’re just there...
a decoration, not a memory.

Real flowers may die,
their petals fragile against time,
but for a moment they breathe life,
their fragrance lingers,
etching itself into our hearts.
They leave behind something
fake ones never could...
a memory of being alive.

Humans are no different.
We fake smiles to impress,
hide scars to fit in,
build masks so heavy
that even we forget who lies beneath.
But deep inside,
we know...
only the real version of us
has the power to leave a trace,
a memory that doesn’t fade
when the act is over.
poem2_theme: Acceptance, gratitude after loss, gentle love
poem2_content:
Goodbyes…
they don’t always arrive with warnings.
Some come quietly,
like a message unsent,
like a voice trembling at 1 a.m.
I’ve met goodbyes before.
I knew they were part of life.
So when yours stood in front of me,
I told myself
I was ready.
But readiness doesn’t stop the ache.
I don’t blame you for leaving.
Some people leave
because staying hurts more.
Some people walk away
because they’re afraid
of breaking what they care about.
And maybe that was you.
Thank you…
for remembering the little things.
For caring when you didn’t have to.
For making distance feel smaller
and nights feel less lonely.
Maybe you stayed for a moment,
maybe for months,
maybe just long enough
to remind me
what being seen feels like.
And even if you had to go,
even if silence is all that’s left now,
I’ll carry this quietly.
Because some goodbyes
don’t take everything away..
they leave gratitude behind.
That someone stayed.
Even for a while.

poem1_theme: The poem celebrates the quiet, resilient power of a soul that blooms within itself, unshaken by absence or loss
poem1_content:
She was not vociferous like tempests
nor incandescent with borrowed flame.
She harbored dawn within her ribs
and patience like molten silver in her gaze.
The world misread her stillness
as void,
never fathoming
that some oceans refuse to roar.
She loved without dominion,
waited without frailty,
nurtured faith like a lantern
that demanded nothing of the encroaching dark.
Time assayed her with gentleness,
then with cruelty,
and finally acquiesced
for endurance is a dialect
even destiny must heed.
Her heart fractured in pristine lines,
never jagged, never acrid,
as if sorrow itself
paid homage to her integrity.
She discerned that not all supplications
are fulfilled with arrival;
some are answered
through metamorphosis.
And so she became
not another’s perpetuity,
not a fading echo
but a presence so consummate
that absence could not diminish her.
Some women bloom for the world.
She bloomed for her own soul.
And that
was sufficient
to command the gaze of the cosmos.

poem1_theme: A quiet journey from unexpected love to heartbreak, ending in strength, self-worth, and the belief that love itself was never a mistake.
poem1_content:
I wasn’t looking for love
when you walked in.
I was busy living,
laughing without plans,
whole without trying.

Time did a dangerous thing
it stayed.
Words grew longer,
silences felt safe,
and somewhere between comfort and courage
we fell.
Both of us.
Honestly.

I loved without counting.
Without keeping exits.
And when my heart finally settled,
you left
returning me
to the same alone I once knew,
only heavier.

I won’t tell you I’m broken.
I won’t display my pain.
This ache is mine,
earned and temporary.

I promised myself
not to fall again.
A lie, of course.

Because love wasn’t a mistake.
My love was never wrong.
It came open-handed.
You simply didn’t take it.

I will rise
not bitter,
not hardened
but still capable of loving,
and finally certain
that being left
doesn’t make me less.

poem1_theme: This poem shows how  loving someone hurts , how trust turn into fear and  in the end there is no answer and repayment.
poem1_content:
Can u Pay?
U praise the patience I kept for u,
but never saw the storm I carried inside.
I said “enough” a thousand times in silence,
yet kept one trembling ray of hope alive.

I knew u wouldn’t come
still my heart waited at the door.
Tell me, can u pay
for hoping  when I already knew the truth?

Can u pay for the nights
when your memory turns my breath into sobs?
Can u pay for every place, every song,
that suddenly becomes us?

Can u pay
for forgetting me so easily,
while I turn u into art
just because I don’t know how to forget?

Can u pay for the tears
that fall without permission,
for the time I invested
in remembering someone who chose absence?

Can u pay
for the way I hurt myself
by still making efforts for u,
for trusting you when trust now scares me?

Can u pay
for the lies wrapped as promises,
for becoming the reason
I doubt every human heart?

Can u pay
for the thoughts that visit me daily,
where death feels like rest,
and living feels like punishment?

Tell me,
can u pay for this every day?
Or is this debt meant to stay
only with me?


30/12/25
poem2_theme: This poem told how much we struggle through whole life as a warrier. In every step of life , we face some obstacles and we deal with ourself in that situation.
poem2_content:
War

This poem speaks of not war between two countries,
but more than that,
it is the war within.

War with urself
when u are mentally exhausted
and no one stands beside u.

War with urself
when u are misunderstood,
when ur silence is mistaken for weakness.

War with urself
when u are criticized and judged,
yet forced to listen,
never allowed to speak.

War with yourself
when ur words lose value
and silence becomes ur only defense.

War with urself
when someone once permanent
slowly transforms into temporary.

War with urself
when u realize
not only people,
but everything is temporary.

War with urself
when trust fractures
and doubts take its place.

War with urself
when you have time,
but they choose to fade.

War with urself
during the struggling phase,
when survival itself feels heavy.

War with urself
when u are learning independence,
while carrying invisible pressure.

War with urself
when your decisions are questioned,
taken from u,
or never truly urs.

War with urself
when restrictions cage ur freedom.

War with urself
when u overthink endlessly,
unable to stop the flood of thoughts.

War with urself
when u feel left out of the world,
like a shadow no one notices.

War with urself
when memories trap u in place.

War with urself
when time is running,
yet your legs refuse to move.

War with urself
when praise appears only when needed,
and neglect greets u when u are not.

War with urself
when u stand alone against obstacles,
still choosing to rise.

War with urself
when u master the phrase,
“I am fine.”

War with urself
when loneliness no longer frightens you.

War with urself
when u run from situations,
yet cannot move backward anymore.

War with urself
when u begin writing
instead of explaining with your voice.

War with urself
when music and art
become ur home.

War with urself
is not a phase.
War with urself
is life.

poem1_content: ss

Love has something magical in it,
Turning the ordinary into the extraordinary.
Success is found in the will to try
And the peace of togetherness.
You are the one I never thought could reach
The furthest corners of my darkest spaces,
Becoming the light behind every smile.
Everything I do was never because of you,
But always for you.
Perhaps in another dimension, or a distant universe,
Laws of time and space may shift—
But the only constant is me falling for you.
Even if we only touch for a moment,
You are my love in every version of forever.

Love for Animals

"Love for animals means that one must care for all living beings in a kind, respectful, and compassionate manner. Animals are a significant element in our world because they share our Earth along with human beings. Animals do not possess the ability to speak like humans, but they feel the same way that humans feel."

Animals can be considered one of the most effective methods of showing how much we love them. It is never appropriate to beat, tease, or harm animals like pets, roadside animals, and wild animals. Rather, we should give them food, water, and a safe place to live in. During the extreme weather conditions of summer and winter, it is very important to give special attention to animals. Providing water to birds in the summer and clothes or shelter to animals in winter can prove to be the reason for saving their lives.

Animals have emotions, too; they are capable of feeling happiness, fear, pain, and sadness. If they are subjected to poor treatment, they feel scared and hurt. And in turn, if they get treated with love, they become friendly and trusting. Animals remember kindness; it prompts them into belief in response, loyalty, and affection. Pets like dogs and cats often become a part of the family, giving us unconditional love.

Many animals help humans in different ways. Dogs help the police and rescue teams, guide dogs help blind people, and animals like cows and goats provide us with milk. Farmers depend on animals for farming work. Birds help by spreading seeds, and bees help in pollination, which is very important for growing crops. Thus, animals play a very important role in maintaining the balance of nature.

Keeping pets at home also teaches a number of good values to children. Feeding pets on time, cleaning their space, and taking care of their health help children learn responsibility. Playing with animals reduces stress and brings happiness. Animals can be great companions for people who feel lonely. Their presence brings joy and peace to our lives.

Love for animals also means saving the animal from cruelty. Human beings for their selfishness cause pain to many animals in one or other ways. Some animals are victimized due to beating, abandonment, and poor conditions of survival. We must raise our voice against the cruel deeds executed by people against animals and try to help injured animals when we can. In case a particular animal seems to be in distress, it must be brought to elders, shelters for animals, and authorities. Wild animals also require love and conservation on our part. Destruction of forests and hunting of animals affects wildlife and disrupts nature. We should conserve forests and national parks in order to provide a safe environment for animals in their habitats. It is our responsibility to conserve animals from extinction because each species of animal has its importance in nature.

At schools and in homes, children should also be taught to respect animals. It is true love when, in small matters, children do not throw stones at animals, give food to stray dogs, and keep water bowls for birds. Animals should not be brought from pet stores, but should instead be adopted.

Conclusion
In conclusion, love for animals brings goodness to the world. Through love of animals, we learn the values of compassion, kindness, and respect. When we show love to animals, we will be good human beings. A society of loving and caring animals can be said to be a civilized society because such a society will be full of kindness. We should treat animals with love and care.

poem1_title: Nourishing Emotional Intelligence: A Pathway to Spiritual Maturity
poem1_content:
In unornamented words , an 'Emotion' is a response or reaction to an event. It may be both physical and mental due to neurophysiological changes. Its the degree of pleasure or displeasure on a more broader note. Emotions comprise of 3 distinct components : a subjective experience, a physiological response & a behavioral or expressive response. 

The subjective experience is the personal or internal feeling of the person to an event. The physiological response may be the several physical factors such as impulsive sweating , shivering of the legs , shallow or heavy breathing , feeling faint , etc. Lastly , the behavioral or expressive response is how we show our feeling to that event through facial gestures , body language and verbal expressions.

Humans , by nature can think about certain things and are motivated by their instincts, intellect and logic unlike animals which are incapable of reasoning and are simply motivated by their instincts. We are guided by our rational thoughts and reasoning ability for which emotions play a primary role. But what is emotional intelligence ? Why is it vital for a harmonious lifestyle ?

Emotional intelligence is the potential to recognize , understand , manage and put to use of our emotions in the correct way so as to lead a healthy and moral life with oneself as well as with others. It helps in decluttering of the mind and cleansing and maintaining of good , unpolluted thoughts. Healthy relationships , quality thoughts , improved mental health , change in perspective for the good , positive approach to situations , perceiving the silver lining even in the most challenging times can all be achieved through emotional intelligence. But how is it related to spirituality ? How can it help in gaining spiritual maturity ?

Conscious recognition and regulation of one's emotions and maintaining calm temperament allows oneself on the path of spirituality further leading to spiritual maturity. This starts from within. For instance ; 

You planned a rendezvous with a friend or loved one. You show up on time and begin to wait for a long time. This makes you restless, impatient and of course without a doubt frustrated. You decide to leave carrying all that built up anger and hurt. Further , when you talk later with that person you right out explode all your anger. But shortly after you are done expressing it , you get to know that they had a stressful workload that day or maybe that they were unwell and had to visit their doctor. Now you are in a deeply dug hole of guilt and shame as you never gave a thought as to why this particular person could have done this. Or that maybe it wasn't intentional or on purpose. Perhaps they were honestly held up with something quite serious and important that they couldn't keep up with your plan. But now, the damage is done and instead of being there for your friend through their tough time , you've made it worse by lashing out at them.

Instead of exploding at someone or at a situation , trying to give a moment's thought would take us so far.
We have to accept that we can't always be entitled and have to be the bigger person in the room sometimes as someone else has a considerably worser day or life than us. As mentioned in the above example situation , you only had to wait a certain amount of time but for your friend had to experience something even bitter. 'Empathy' is the word that should be the fundamental of our character. 

In this example , the friend had a much bigger problem than you. Understanding this is maturity. In order to achieve this , emotional intelligence is required as you need to control your anger , hurt and other extreme factors so that you would be able to maintain your cool. Once you have gained emotional intelligence you become set for the path to spiritual maturity - as only when you control your emotions they wouldn't cloud what may be right in front of you. If you weren't mad at your friend for not showing you would have thought of why he couldn't make it or if he was held up with something important.
