Not only during our tough times but we have to implement this in our happy times too. We have to accept that life will never remain consistent and that it has its ups and downs which happens constantly. " Change is the only thing which remains which remains constant " , one might say. We have to be grateful when life is good to us or when something is keeping us happy but at the same time be prepared to accept when all of this goes downhill and that this is just the circle of life. 

In essence , The journey of cultivating emotional intelligence is not always easy, but it is incredibly rewarding, leading to a more balanced, compassionate, and spiritually enriched life. Emotional intelligence serves as a vital pathway to spiritual maturity, guiding us toward a deeper connection with ourselves, others, and the divine. 

By embracing this journey, we open ourselves to a life of greater fulfillment, harmony, and spiritual growth.

On clods and pebbles.

We are forever confined by the love we think we deserve. The love we share. The love we give. And the love we receive. What makes each of them different from one another? What makes one more special than the other? What makes it important? What makes it make sense? 

Making love one-dimensional is a shame, and so is making it multi-dimensional. Love is devotion as much as it is obsession. Love is reverence as much as it is disregard. Love is thriving as much as it is rotting. 

One can be devoted enough towards a person to melt in their mould completely, and become a copy of what they are- but without the scratches. One can be devoted enough to see their reflection everywhere, so far as to even see your eyes in the mirror through theirs and therefore utterly lose your identity. 
One can revere enough to know every inch of their skin, every mole and every wrinkle imprinted in their handprint and fingertips. One can revere enough to memorise someone, ingrain them indelibly inside their tapestry, only to suffocate them like a predator measuring prey. 
One can thrive enough to recognise the opportunities for improvement presented and be crude enough to reject them bluntly- for one is so invested in the other, so blind in the illusions that rotting is inevitable. 

Love is everything and nothing. Love is the flowers blooming on your windowsill and the dirt in the pots supporting them. Love is the smell of coffee in the air but it is also present in the bitter notes of the roasted beans. Love is you, love is me, love is them- love is nothing but everything. 

Inspired by the poem ‘The Clod and the Pebble” by William Blake

poem1_theme: This poem explores love as something living and continuous rather than preserved or finished. The “museum” becomes a space where love breathes, grows, and is renewed through everyday moments and quiet devotion. The emotion is tender and intimate, emphasizing love as a conscious practice, one that survives through care, presence, and the willingness to nurture it over time.
poem1_content:
This is the museum of love, I bring you inside.
This is the museum of love, built from how I love, and still I do.
This is the museum of love, and every treasure here is only for you.

This is no sanctuary for what has concluded.
nothing here is forgotten, framed, or past.
Every step holds love in perpetual becoming,
from the beginning to the last.
love as I learn, practice, and carry,
love as I love you.

Here, love lingers in ordinary hours,
As life's candence becomes acts of devotion.
In how mornings are unfolded, wrapped in musings of you,
I surrender to sleep, your presence resting gently within me.
Arriving like a cherished melody, unskipped, 
a refrain I hum unconsciously.
I mould with my hands, dwelling upon you,
imperfect creations, lovingly formed with steadfast tending.
Art I revisit daily, not to decipher, but to behold.
It bids me tend myself with greater care,
for love, if it's for eternity,
must be nurtured.

You're the poem I've always longed to write, 
a living collection, wherein love is permitted to thrive. 
Thus, this museum lives with us,
as long as love does, as long as love does.

Scene 1

“Ha! All done!”
I gave a final swipe to my suit jacket before looking into the mirror. I looked- no, I GENUINELY looked good today. I was usually the type of boy girls called ‘cute,’ never ‘handsome.’ I wasn’t magnetic. But tonight? Tonight, I seemed to be dangling my feet right at the edge of the ceiling of my ‘category.’
I turned this way and that, checking the fit of the shoulders. Why was I so nervous? It was just Farewell day, after all. The day you’re supposed to remember for the rest of your life. All those hours in classes, the sleepless nights- it was finally time to have some fun with everyone.
I checked my pocket. My phone was there. My wallet was there. I patted my chest one more time, just making sure everything was tucked away and ‘perfect.’ It had to be perfect. I couldn’t afford a single hair out of place tonight. If I was going to step out of my category, I had to look the part.
“You’re ready,” I told the mirror. I grabbed my keys and headed for the door, my heart thumping a rhythm that felt a bit too fast for just a school party.
I bid my mom farewell- calling out a quick “Bye, Maa!”, before stepping out into the night. My car was waiting, and honestly, so was my ego. I was driving to school today. ME. I had recently gotten my license, and this felt like the ultimate moment to finally flaunt it.
I got into the driver’s seat and shut the door. The silence of the cabin felt heavy, like it was waiting for me to do something impressive. I turned the key. Grrr! The engine sounded, a low rumble that made me feel like the coolest person in the world. Usually, I’d immediately blast my favorite playlist, drumming my fingers on the steering wheel to the bass. But tonight?
Tonight, I reached for the radio knob and clicked it off.
I just wanted to hear the car. I wanted to hear the tires on the asphalt and the hum of the engine as I accelerated. The car’s interior smelt of recently polished leather and an upbeat fragrance of flowers.
 I drove with a practiced ease, or at least, I tried to make it look practiced. My heart was doing this weird thing where it wanted the ride to end so I could get to school as fast as possible, but another part of me wanted to stay in this bubble forever.
The streets were blurred shadows outside my window. I didn’t want to miss a single sound. I felt hyper-aware of everything- the gear shifts, the distant honks, the way my suit jacket felt a bit stiff against my shoulders. I was savoring it. Every second felt like it was being etched into my brain in high definition.
I wasn’t just driving to a building. I was driving toward a “before” and an “after,” and I wanted to make sure I arrived there exactly as I intended.
The drive felt shorter than usual, or maybe I was just moving faster without realizing it. Soon, I was pulling into the school parking lot. This was the place where I’d watched my teachers park their scooters and cars for years. It was a restricted zone, a place for “grown-ups.” But tonight, I was slotting my car right into one of those spaces. I felt a surge of pride as I killed the engine. I belonged here tonight.
I stepped out, the cool night air hitting my face, and made sure to lock the doors with a satisfying click. Before heading toward the lights, I leaned over and checked my reflection in the side mirror one last time.
“You look good, handsome,” I whispered. I said it like a challenge, trying to convince myself that the guy in the glass was capable of anything. I adjusted my cuffs with surgical precision, making sure everything was exactly where it should be.
The school entrance was decorated with flower garlands. As I walked up, I saw my teachers standing there, welcoming us. Usually, they were the ones giving us lectures or grading our papers, but tonight they were smiling at everyone like we were long-lost friends. When it was my turn, I did what I’d always done- I leaned down to touch their feet.
Then, something happened that I didn’t expect. They hugged me.
I’d always been a favorite, the “good student,” but the hugs felt different. It was like they were acknowledging that I wasn’t just a boy in a uniform anymore. They praised my look, telling me how grown-up I appeared, and for a few minutes, we just stood there talking. It was a boost of confidence I really needed. I felt like I was finally walking through a door that had been locked my whole life.
I walked into the building with them, feeling like I was finally ready to face the crowd. It was time.

Scene 2

The assembly ground was unrecognizable. I walked in and felt this huge surge of nostalgia mixed with something that felt like... righteousness. Like I had finally earned my place here. Everything looked special- even the domestic staff helpers were in beautiful sarees, and the teachers looked so smart. The lush green grass looked like it had been pruned to perfection just for this evening. I could tell just by looking at it.
But the biggest attention seekers were us- the students. People I had seen only in uniforms for years were suddenly dressed so eloquently! It was like a movie.
“Over here!”
It was Pranav. He was my only friend. He looked dazzling in his suit, and even though I secretly thought my own fit was a bit better (hehe), I was just happy to see him. Now before you pity me for having just one friend- I think it’s actually great. It meant we could just grab two seats anywhere and chat without any drama.
We were catching up when a few other classmates drifted over. Prachi showed up a little late, as usual, like she was allergic to being in a group. She was wearing a yellow dress that somehow made the whole place look brighter without trying too hard, and she kept pushing her glasses up her nose even though they refused to slide down. She was beautiful in a very unfair way- the kind that didn’t need announcements or reactions. We talked sometimes, in that confusing middle space where you know someone well enough to tease them but not well enough to steal their seat. She was the class topper, and usually sitting near her meant you HAD to listen to her rant about how Biology is the best subject known to mankind for the next forty minutes. When she did listen though, she did so with her head slightly tilted, nodding like she already knew what you were going to say- though knowing her, maybe she really did. All the while, she would absentmindedly twirl a strand of hair around her finger, like she didn’t notice she was doing it.
In short, being around her felt easy but mildly confusing, like reading a question you understand perfectly but still double-check before answering. I didn’t want her presence beside me right now because I thought it’d make me nervous.
“Mind if I sit here?” she asked.
“Sure, plenty of room,” Pranav said before I could say anything, keeping it cool. 
I sighed nervously. The fact that people were choosing to hang out with us tonight- especially someone like Prachi who had her own big circle, felt like a massive thing. I kept asking myself, Am I really looking that good tonight? The nervousness soon faded though somehow. I felt more confident than usual tonight, even in her presence. Pranav, however, for a brief period, remained quiet near her- only to finally regain his confidence after my constant pegging.
The event was starting. I leaned back in my chair, feeling completely content with the world.
The performances were actually great. The three of us spent the next hour just being kids- laughing at the jokes on stage and pointing out friends in the dance sequences. It was the kind of easy, mindless fun I’d been craving all year. I felt a weird sense of belonging. Seeing all our nearby classmates laugh at our stupid comments made me feel like I’d finally cracked the code of being “social.”
I was at the top of my game, throwing out one-liners that kept the energy up. I felt like the anchor of the group. Every time a song ended and the crowd roared, I felt my own confidence swell. Prachi seemed to enjoy our company more than she had ever before, and we talked as if we had been besties since our first day at school.
But as the final act finished and the house lights hummed back to life, the mood shifted. The “Farewell” part was over, and the “Goodbye” part was beginning.
Pranav suddenly went quiet. The guy who had been laughing just a second ago was now staring at his shoes, his face turning a shade of pale that actually worried me. He looked like he was vibrating.
“Hey,” I whispered, leaning over. “You okay?”
He didn’t answer. He just gestured toward the side corridor, away from the noise of the clearing assembly ground. I looked at Prachi, who was busy talking to someone else for a moment, and then followed Pranav into the shadows.
The corridor was a relief from the noise- quiet, dim, and cool. Pranav was pacing back and forth, looking like he was about to vibrate out of his skin. I watched him for a second, confused.
“Dude, what’s with the drama?” I asked, leaning against the wall. “You look like you’re about to face a board exam you didn’t study for.”
He stopped, looked around to make sure we were alone, and then leaned in. “I’m going to do it. Tonight. I have to.”
“Do what?”
“I’m going to tell Prachi how I feel,” he whispered, his face turning a deep shade of red. “We’ve been texting for months, man. It was always casual, nothing serious. But.. But I think... I think I’m actually in love with her. But look at me- I’m shaking! I’m going to mess this up, aren’t I?”
I stared at him. For a moment, the only thing I could hear was the distant thump of the music from the assembly ground. My best friend- the guy who usually couldn’t even order pizza without stuttering- was actually stepping up. 
“Prachi?” I repeated. I let out a short laugh, clapping him on the shoulder. “Wow. You’ve been keeping secrets, haven’t you?”
“I’m sorry I didn’t tell you sooner,” he said, looking at me with total, desperate hope. “But you’re the ‘charmer’ tonight, bro. You look like you’ve got it all figured out. Please... I need you. If you don’t help me, I’ll just go home and regret this forever. This night won’t come again.”
“You bet.”
“Man I’m sorry but please help me! I even brought a bouquet and everything for her. ...

poem1_title: The Colour Love chose not to be
poem1_theme: The rejection of sacrifice and heartbreak as necessary evidence of love. A reclamation of love from suffering.
poem1_content:
They say love is red, 
Threaded in fate, in blood, in ache
Why not the colour of autumn leaves, warmth and abundance 
A silk spun from sunsets and slow,
Sure hands that hold, not just reach? 

Why must love stain, bruise, burn? 
Which leaves poems in throats, 
Whispering apologises in mirrors,
For loving too much, too soon, too wrong. 

They call it fate, this thread. 
But being chosen second to someone else's forever? 
Is this tragedy really fate? 
Why must soulmates bleed to be bound? 
Why must the proof of love be found in loss? 

Red came like a storm, tightrope 
In the name of destiny 
Disguised as home in my ribs. 
Then left me constantly yearning,
Cutting all the twisted fibers. 

But golden, 
Like the song daylight,
it breathes Heaven's embrace into me
It will hold me, soothes the soul,
Not in fire but in light, in eternity. 

A love that glows like late sunlight,
On skin that's finally stopped flinching.
A thread that doesn't cut, but cradles the innerchild.
One that whispers "you are not too much" and stays long enough to proves it 

Threads that don't strangle when pulled too tight
Hands that don't twitch when the future walks onto the stage
Voices that don't falter when asked "will you hold me in the dark?" 

I loved him like the colour golden, I was gold.
But he choose red, Maybe his fingers only knew how to hold flame, not light
And maybe,
Just maybe,
Love was never meant to be red. 

I'll let him fly, have his crimson tales,
His fire and tragedy.
I'll wait for a love, stitched in silk and sunshine
Soft and certain, gentle and golden. 

And if that thread never finds me, at least I'll know--
It wasn't red that kept me waiting, 
It was hope--
For a love which never makes me question why it is red.

poem1_theme: The poem explores the quiet devastation of loving deeply while remaining emotionally undefined and unchosen.
poem1_content:
How do I know?
You must ask
Because I loved him
Deeply I must say,

Enough to gather courage and place it in a question
What am I to you?
I asked

His answer did not shout
Did not wound loudly
Rather simply stopped me in place.

“Someone’s Almost”,
He said
And abandoned the rest

But I wonder
what almost weighs like
and how frightening it is
to live so near love
yet never be
the chosen one.
poem2_content:
प्यार है तो हक़ है… मैंने सादगी से कहा,
जवाब में तुम्हारी ख़ामोशी ने आईना रखा।
वो लम्हा ठहरा जैसे वक़्त ने साँस रोकी हो,
और दिल ने धीरे से खुद को टोका हो।

तुम कहके भी ना कह पाए कुछ,
मैं सुनके भी सब सुन गई।
तुम्हारे लफ़्ज़ों की तह में, कुछ अनकही बातें थीं,
और मेरी हर मुस्कान… वहीं मर गई।

मैंने चाँद समझा तुम्हें… उजाला भी, दूरी भी,
तुम सूरज निकले… गरमी भी, मजबूरी भी।
हर ख्वाब को तुमने हक़ से जुदा कर दिया,
और मैंने हर दर्द को इबादत बना लिया।

प्यार है तो हक़ है,
पर अब वो हक़ सिर्फ़ रूह का रह गया।
यादें बरसती रहें,
और मैं…

poem1_content:
I dawned in a place that couldn't be amiss, 
Flickering my eyes, I woke to this bliss.
Watching my breath drifting into the blue, 
I lost myself in the wonder of this view.


Mountains stand fading in shades of brown, 
Donning white snow like a crystalline crown 
On the skin of the lake where thin ice lay, 
A beautiful reflection is painted away.


Swans so white, wove through the shivering bay, 
While snow kissed my skin and melted away. 
The cemetery garden where eternal souls sleep, 
Beneath the cold stones, their peace they keep.


From the high clock tower, the iron bells swung, 
Piercing the silence, the ringing sound flung. 
And there I was! A girl with a coat for her shield, 
To harbor a warmth that the world couldn't wield.


In Hallstatt's embrace, on my solo retreat,
At minus one degree, there was someone to meet. 
Smiling at my reflection in the water from above, 
I finally saw the one with whom I fell in love.


-- Supriya S

poem1_theme: This poem explores the quiet, freezing ache of loving someone who remains unaware or indifferent, using the metaphor of a world stalled just a fraction below the freezing point.
poem1_content:
The air is thin, a blade against my chest,
Where every breath I take is held in wait.
You stand within the circle of the rest,
While I am locked outside the garden gate.
The mercury has dipped beneath the line,
A single digit shy of warmth or grace.
I claim a heart that never will be mine,
And search for recognition in your face.
At minus one, the world is held in glass,
The puddles crinkle underneath my boot.
I watch the shadows of your footsteps pass,
But let my longing wither at the root.
It isn’t cold enough to kill the spark,
Nor warm enough to let the river flow.
I’m wandering the edges of the dark,
Caught in a light dusting of silver snow.
I see you laugh and tilt your head away,
The golden hum of life within your veins.
I have a thousand things I’ll never say,
Bound by these invisible, icy chains.
You are the sun on distant, winter peaks,
A beauty that I’m forced to view from far.
The color hasn’t touched my frozen cheeks;
I am the moth, and you the Northward star.
If it were colder, I might finally numb,
And cease to feel the biting of the breeze.
If it were warmer, maybe words would come,
And melt the silence of these ancient trees.
But here we stall at minus one degree,
The point where liquid turns to jagged stone.
There is no room for us, just you and me—
You in the light, and me forever lone.
My love is preserved in this sudden chill,
A flower trapped in layers of the rime.
The world is white, and quiet, and so still,
Suspending us within a slip of time.
I’ll stay right here, where frost begins to bite,
And keep my quiet vigil in the cold,
Wishing for the morning’s amber light,
But fearing that the truth is too bold.

उसकी आँखों में रूहानियत देख पाना,
उसकी खुली ज़ुल्फ़ों में अपने आप से खो जाना।
उसका हर फ़लसफ़ा जब अपना-सा लगता है,
इश्क़ की यही फ़ितरत है मेरा दिल कहता है।

उसके चेहरे की बनावट को सपनों में देख पाना,
उसके लबों को छूने का सोच कर ही शरमा जाना।
मुसलसल देखती रहने वाली मेरी नज़रों का
उसके आने पर झुक जाना अच्छा लगता है,
इश्क़ की यही फ़ितरत है मेरा दिल कहता है।

उसकी हर मुस्कुराहट पर यूँ मासूम-सा बन जाना,
उसका दीदार न होने पर मेरा दिल ख़ाक हो जाना।
उसकी नज़रों के अश्कों को अधरों से चूम लेना,
उसके शहर के शबब में दिल बच्चा सा लगता है 
इश्क़ की यही फ़ितरत है मेरा दिल कहता है।

उन गालों का मुझे देख शरम से लाल हो जाना,
यूँ ही बैठे-बैठे कभी उनसे प्यार बेशुमार हो जाना।
उनका मेरी चाहत से बढ़कर इबादत हो जाना,
मेरे अपने सपनों का भी उनकी आदत हो जाना।
उनकी पलकों का पर्दा रात की चादर-सा लगता है,
इश्क़ की यही फ़ितरत है मेरा दिल कहता है।

उनके फ़साने सुनकर ही जीवन का लुत्फ़ उठा लेना,
और हक़ीक़त देखकर तो लुत्फ़-ओ-करम पा लेना।
अब जब दिल बोला कि मोहब्बत की ज़ंजीरें तोड़ आना,
फिर भी महफ़िल में उनका आते ही
दिल का एक बार फिर धड़क जाना मुझे बेताब सा करता है 
इश्क़ की यही फ़ितरत है मेरा दिल कहता है।

poem1_theme: Core Themes: Social Hypocrisy   Betrayal   Self-Preservation   Sarcasm as a Weapon   Core Emotions: Indignation Contempt Resentment Defiance
poem2_theme: Core Themes: Emotional Balance   Self-Responsibility   Growth and Resilience   Acceptance of Change   Core Emotions: Hope Maturity Determination Serenity Optimism
poem2_content:
In the dark room, diamonds drop from the eyes;
Pearls shine from the thirty-two.
Madness ruins the sadness;
You are the master of your own happiness.
Clouds are passing, tied up in the chase;
The sky remains in the same place.
The sun and the moon are misplaced
In the journey of the busy bees.
A dove can bring you love;
A peace of kisses from your soulmate.
Pieces of hate to move past;
Win or lose, you must grow.
Make use of your saltwater to make your life better.
Cleanse your soul,
Pour your pain through the vessel.
Life has pink and blue;
Take green as your clue.
Be the yellow to your fellows;
Never be red to the dread.
In abundance or lack,
No one escapes the black.
Sometimes you are the rain,
The next day you may drain away.
One day you are the enchanted rainbow,
The next, you are a drop in the unwanted mud!
You can be the ice cubes in red wine,
Or the frozen ice in a mournful house.
Whatever it may be,
Keep your luster like wine at a fine dine!

poem1_content:
“Me & Him, A Quiet Storm”

In the quiet between beats, we find each other -
Not boisterous, not jarring,
But like raindrops getting to know your skin For the first time.

He, the stillness in my tempest,
The kind of silence that does not long to be shattered, Just heard me.
I-blaze in velvet,
A contradiction of steel and silk,
Trying to forget I’m afraid
Of how intensely I feel when he says my name Like it’s already past.

We don’t walk hand in hand in public,
But the air knows -
It hums between us, electric and still,
Like something sacred whispered At a godless altar.
Of how intensely I feel when he says my name Like it's already past.

We don’t walk hand in hand in public,
But the air knows -
It hums between us, electric and still,
Like something sacred whispered At a
godless altar.

He talks like midnight drives feel -
Aimless, honest,
Almost like he's mapping constellations From the
chaos of my thoughts.

I laugh like I'm unlearning sorrow.
He listens like he's memorizing the sound.

Maybe we’re not meant to last.
Maybe we’re a poem, Not a
story.

But if the universe carved him
Out of some stardust just to find me -
Even once -
That’s enough to keep me
Staring at the sky a bit longer.

poem1_theme: कभी कभी हम इश्क तो करते है मगर इज़हार नहीं। इस इश्क के बोझ तले ही जीते हैं, मैं अपनी कविता के माध्यम से उस बोझ का एहसास कराने आया हूं आप सभी को।  जो कभी खत्म न हो, कही जा न सके, सुनिए ऐसी कहानी , सिद्धार्थ कागज़ी की जुबानी।    एक सफ़र कागज से कलम तक
poem1_content:
इस भीड़ में इस कदर हम खो गए,
जिसका नहीं होना था हमें, उसी के हम हो गए।

समझ न पाए खुद को तफ़सील से,
जैसे कोई चादर ओढ़ के सो गए।

तनहाइयों का मंज़र ही मिला हमें,
मदहोशी इतनी थी कि कुछ समझ न आ रहा था,

कि वो आए फरिश्ता बनकर,
हमारे सारे ज़ख्मों को धो गए।

लफ़्ज़ों से बयान करना था मगर,
उनके आने पर नज़रें भी झुक गईं,

कई मर्तबा बयान करना चाहा,
पर जब नज़दीक गए, तो धड़कनें ही रुक गईं।

Some mornings arrive without a sound,
Yet everything feels slightly off the ground,
The clock moves fine, the walls still stay,
But something else has slipped away.

What once came easy now asks why,
What felt like truth now passes by,
I trace old paths without a plan,
Just trying to know where this began.

Entire futures fold in seconds flat,
Like dreams that forgot where they were kept,
The mind debates, the heart resists,
Both tired of unanswered ifs.

Some winters don’t announce their stay,
They settle in without dismay,
No storm, no sign, no shattered part,
Just colder rooms inside the heart.

Not cold enough to fully break,
Not warm enough to gently wake,
Things do not end, they learn to stall,
They pause, they wait, they slowly fall.

I walk ahead because time insists,
Not because I’ve learned to coexist,
And somewhere between holding tight
And standing still without a fight,

I learn how change needs no consent,
It simply comes, then calls it meant,
And leaves you standing in between
Who you were and what’s unseen.

Saans hamari tham si gyi
Jab hue wo hamse roo ba roo
Nambz hamari ruk si gyi
Jab wo dikhe hume jo hoo ba hoo
Hamare sapno mein aaya krte the
Hamaare dil mein samaaya krte the
Jo hume parwaane likha krte the
Jo hamare dil o Jaan mein
Tabaahi machaaya krte the
Ishq pe hume bharosa na tha
Jo unhe dekh kar yun ho gaya
Wafaahi se hamaara naata na tha
Jo ek pal mein unse jud gaya
Dar lagta tha dil lagaane se
Thokar dhokhe khaane se
Lagta tha na uth paayenge
Dhokhe se jo gir jaayenge
Ye nah socha tha kabhi
Ki hum ese pighal jaayenge
Unki ek muskaan ko dekh kr
Jo zeher esa pi jayenge
Bs shart itni hai ki janaab
Zeher ka pyaala wo thamaayenge
Marne se pehle kuch pal hum
Unke haathon ko sehlaayenge
Unke zalmon ko jataayenge
Apne zakhm ko dikhaayenge
Unmein basti hai hamari nambz
Unke haathon mein hamaari jaan
Unke aankhon mein hamaara dil
Unke ishq se hamaari pehchaan
Laila jaisa husn ne unke
Bana diya hume qais deewana
Laila jaise husn ne unke
Likh diya hamara afsaana
Ab pyaar mein hum unke
Ese hue baigaane hain
Jaise qais ka tha nasha laila ka
Bade hum unke dewaane hain
Deewangi esi sur pe chadh jaaye
Jo utarna ka naam na le
Dharkane jo yuh badh jaaye
Jo rukne ka naam na le
Jannat ki hooron se badhkar
Hai unke chahere ki chamak
Ek pal jo wo aankhein milin
Dil mein uthi pyaar ki gamak
Dil ko ese haara humne
Jab unse hui nazre chaar
Dil haathon aagaya tha
Man ka haal hua behaal
Kya batayein janaab
Wo hain hi itne khooobsurat
Har pal tak tak ke ab humne
Dil mein basa li unki moorat
Dil mein base hue hain wo
Banke wo shohar hamaare
Man mein chipe hue hain wo
Banke hamaara aankhon ke taare
Loot liya unhone hume
Hamaare sukh dookh aur chainon se
Loot liya unhone hume
Hamaare din dophar aur rainon se
Kya karein ab wo hi hain
Jo hamaare sab kuch lagte hain
Kya karein ab wo hi hain
Jo hamare dil mein rehte hain
Jo hamare dil mein rehte hain..
poem2_content:
Gale mein chubhte hain wo lafz
Jo bayan nhi kr paati
Dil mein ghoojhte hain wo shabdh
Jo bayan nhi kr paati
Hothon pe ruk jaate jo sawaal
Jo bayan nhi kr paati
Aankhein nam krte hain wo khayaal
Jo bayan nhi kr paati
Sochti hun
magar kuch keh hi paati
Jawaab chahiye ki
Kyun khush reh nhi paati
Lagta hai badal rhi hun
Magar kuch kr nhi paati
Khauf hai ki kho na dun
Jis khudko main dhoondh nhi paati
Man shaant sa ho gaya hai
Dhadkan ghat si gayi hai
Kahin na kahin meri zindagi
Ghamon mein bat si gayi hai
Kitna yun tatolun khudko
Jab aake mujhko girna hi hai
Kitna yun pukaare sabko
Jab aake mujhko marna hi hai
Jaan se nhi to dil se sahi
Man ne ye baat khudko kahi
Kitna samjhaogi sabko
Koi nhi tumko samjhega
Kitne ghum sunaogi sabko
Kya hi koi kar payega
Kya fayeda us mehnat ka
Jo aakhir tumko hara de
Kya fayeda us rone ka
Jo aakhir tumko thaka de
Bs karo ab chup ho jao
Haar maanlo bs kr jao
Na chup hua wo mera man
Na chup hui meri dhadkan
Ghoojhte rhe dono is dil mein
Kitni hui ismein uljhan
Kya kare ye lachaar dil
Dard ka maara bekaar dil
Kya rakhkha hai is duniya mein
Jo lakh samjhao samjhti nahi
Kya rakhkha un logon mein
Jo lakh pukaaro rukte nhi
Hn koi krta hai koshish
Koshish ise samjhne ki
Bebas se is dil ko mere
Koshish isko behlaane ki
Lekin phir wo haar jaata hai
Isko nhi samajh paata hai
Isko yun samajhte hue
Khudko chott pohochaata hai
Phir mujhse ukhar jaata hai
Phir mujhse door ho jaata hai
Aur phir ye bebas dil
suna sa ho jaata hai
Jeena to seekh liya hai
Is haalat mein maine apni
Kubool kr liya ghum apna
Aur kubooli haar ye apni
Chal pade hai zindagi ki rah pe
Kadam rkhe sambhal kr ab
Maut aaye to aahi jaye
Dekh lenge hum usko tab
dekh lenge hum usko tab..

poem1_content:
It hurts in a way abandoned places hurt. Not broken, just empty. Like sunlight hitting a place where no one has a claim anymore.

This isn’t the pain of loss. It’s the pain of time passing. Of coming to realize far too late that something beautiful and real was happening, and no one had bothered to put a name to it. Hours passed as if they were used up, exteriors discarded. And now, they come back to me, horribly, wonderfully clear. The way sounds were made, the ease, the safety, the assumption that nothing would ever have to alter.

Nostalgia doesn’t console. Nostalgia corners. It herds the mind back and commands it to live there for a while. Longing settles inside, not for a person, but for a type of existence that drew breath more slowly. For a series of days that were infinite until they learned how to slip by.

There was no ending to survive. No good-bye for healing from. Just space, growing quietly, until all that mattered was behind.

Some ties don't split, they expire. 

They stay in the past, unfinished and inaccessible, warmed by memory and beyond the reach of the present.

"अधूरी मुलाकात"
सर्दियों की शाम थी, और बारिश हो रही थी, मैं अपने कॉलेज के कैंपस में खड़ा था, जब मैंने उसे पहली बार देखा. वह बारिश में भीगते हुए अपने चेहरे पर मुस्कान लिए, एक किताब पढ़ रही थी। मैं बस देखता ही रह गया!!
उस दिन के बाद, मैं उसे अक्सर कैंपस में देखता। कभी-कभी हम बातें भी करते, लेकिन सिर्फ दोस्ती की हद तक। मुझे लगता था कि शायद वह मेरी अनुभूति नही समझती। एक दिन, मैंने हिम्मत करके उससे पूछा, "क्या हम साथ में कॉफी पिएंगे?" वह मुस्कुराई और बोली, "हां, चलो।"
हम कॉफी शॉप में बैठे, बातें कर रहे थे मुझे पता चला कि हम दोनों को एक ही तरह की किताबें पसंद हैं, और संगीत भी एक जैसा सुनते हैं। लगता था जैसे हम एक-दूसरे को सालों से जानते हैं। लेकिन जब मैंने उससे कहा कि मुझे उससे प्यार हो गया है, तो वह चुप हो गई बोली, "मैं तुम्हे दोस्त से ज्यादा कुछ नहीं मान सकती।"।
मैं उदास हो गया, लेकिन दोस्ती निभाई। एक दिन उसने मुझे बताया कि वह शहर छोड़कर जा रही है। मैं उसके घर गया, और हमने आखिरी बार बात किया और वह चली गई। और हम दोनों रात को रोज बातें करते कुछ दिन बीतने के बाद वह घर आयी और यह रिश्ता कब प्यार में बदल गया पता ही नहीं चला।

जब वह आयी तब मैं बहुत खुबसूरत बात कही की-
"तुम्हारी यादों की किताब में,
हर पन्ना है तुम्हारा दीवाना।"।
फिर वह मुझे हमेशा कहती, "तुम मेरी जिंदगी हो।" मैं भी उसे वही कहता, हमने साथ में भविष्य योजना किए, और प्यार में डूबे रहे।
लेकिन किस्मत को कुछ और ही मंजूर था, उसके घरवालों को हमारा रिश्ता पसंद नहीं आया। उन्होंने उसे दूर भेज दिया।
मैं उसके बिना अधूरा हो गया, हम बातें करते, लेकिन दूरी बढ़ती गई। एक दिन, उसने कहा, "हमारे लिए अलग होना ही बेहतर है।"
"तुम्हारे जाने के बाद, मेरी कहानी अधूरी है," मैंने कहा।
वह मुस्कुराई, और बोली, "शायद अधूरी मुलाकात ही पूरी कहानी है।"
मैं आज भी उस अधूरी मुलाकात को याद करता हूँ।।

कभी - कभी दूरी में ही भलाई हे l
जैसे कि उस नीले अंबर और 
बेरंग गहरे समुद्र l
उनके बीच में दूरी में ही दुनिया 
साँस लेती है  l

इनके बीच बड़ी दूरी में ही...
इस दूरी ही प्यार हे  l
इतना गहरा प्यार कि दूर रहकर भी 
उन्हें सुकून मिलता है l

एक ऐसा प्यार जो बिना छुए भी 
खिल उठता है l
एक ऐसा प्यार पर एक ऐसा लानत ...
कि इस दूरी कभी पार नहीं होगा l

ना तो आसमान टूटकर नीचे गिरेगा,
न नीचे आएगा,
या फिर समुद्र अपने प्यार की ओर 
चढ़कर जाएगा l

अगर ये हो गया, तो ये ही होगा 
इस दुनिया का अंत l

काश कहीं ओर ऐसा न हो l
काश कहीं ओर ये दूरी न हो l
काश कहीं ओर ये अधूरी न हो l

पर अभी के लिए इस दूरी में ही भलाई  l
दूरी में ही सुकून l
poem2_content:
ओ चमकती दरिया की धार !
कैसे करूँ तुम्हे पार ?
वैसे ही डूब जाऊँ ?
या पतली नाव बनके पार उतर जाऊँ ?

ओ गहरी बेरंग समुद !
तेरा बनूं मैं  सुंदर रंग l
तुझमे एक जैसे मिलकर ,
होगा थिरके लहरों संग l

ओ पुकारती सागर !
तेरे बाहों में खो गया मैं l
डूबते - डूबते खुद को पाया 
अब बता क्या होगा मेरा ?

एक तुझमें जैसे बनकर जी लूँ ?
तेरी बाहों में ही अपनी आख़िरी साँस लूँ?

poem1_content:
There lie empty cathedrals,
Unread scriptures whispering for embrace.
God remains unseen and unheard,
Yet His love keeps pouring still.
What heartbreak He must endure,
Watching His love drift away.

He searches for the lost and wandering,
Longing to breathe life into drying trees.
Yet they stand as touch-me-nots,
Hearts sealed against His reach.
He yearns to turn them into roses,
The kind that bleeds to grow.

What heartbreak He endures!
A silence echoing in the soul:
“How can it be measured?
It defies all measure.”
How can a mother look upon her child,
When the child no longer knows her?

Yet she never abandons her own.
How, then, could He forsake His Love?
Why, God, did You choose to create love,
Knowing it could be broken?
We cannot fathom Your forgiveness,
Nor the ache You bear as You watch it fade.

"Love That Cannot Arrive"

A beautiful morning it was, when I rose next to him, With love filled kisses, started preparing his breakfast. The glim of our love, and everything there was prim, Cherishing his family with dawn's feast, it was 7 half past.

For office pilgrimage, my hubby wearing his day armor, He, who was my harbor, was really looking like a charmer. His Mamma and I, then went for all the domestic rituals, Such a ladylove, his sister, showing me her artistic visuals.

Papa was loving like Dad, and his brother a quite pillar, I never thought, my life would be such a fascinating thriller. I cuddled him when he returned home, seems so drowsy, And it was his embrace that embraced, and his haven so cozy.

Spent great time in family, then moved to the twilight table, All of us sharing the love, and sharing our day-time fable. After all those evening rituals the bed, I slept in his arms, I can never forget it, Oh God! what a peaceful dream it was.

~Aleena Tariq

poem1_title: What He Didn’t Take with Him
poem1_theme: Love left behind is not empty—it teaches us how to live.
poem1_content:
What He Didn’t Take with Him:
Love left behind is not empty—it teaches us how to live.


Fahad was still asleep, his face turned toward her, one arm resting on her waist like a promise he made every night without words. Safa watched him breathe—slow, steady—memorising the rhythm, unaware that the heart remembers before the mind does.
In the kitchen, Safa hummed softly, sleeves rolled up, cutting fruits the way Fahad liked—small cubes, no seeds. Breakfast was served, lunch packed.
“You’ll forget your lunch again,” she said, tying her dupatta.
Fahad smiled—that familiar half-smile meant only for her. “That’s why I married you.”
Her heart felt full in that quiet way that doesn’t announce happiness—it simply exists.
The day moved gently.
Phone calls. Laughter. Shared tea in ceramic cups.
Fahad left in a hurry, turning back once at the door.
“Don’t wait for dinner,” he said. “I’ll be late.”
She didn’t know that was the last time she would hear his voice.
When he left, nothing dramatic happened.
No screaming.
No last conversation.
No closure.
Life just… continued.
And that was the cruelest part.
She kept his mug on the kitchen shelf for weeks—not because she believed he would return, but because removing it felt like removing proof that something real had existed. His toothbrush stayed in the glass. She rinsed it every night, carefully, like a ritual. Throwing it away felt like erasing him.
At night, her body still leaned to one side of the bed, muscle memory refusing to update itself. She reached out once in her sleep and woke up startled—not because no one was there, but because for a second, she had expected someone to be.
People said, Time heals.
Be strong.
You deserve better.
But no one told her what to do at 3:17 a.m., when the pillow beside you feels louder than loneliness.
She didn’t miss him the most.
She missed who she was when she was loved without effort.
Love didn’t arrive with butterflies anymore.
It arrived with silence.
With making tea for one and pouring it slowly, as if the other cup might appear out of habit.
There were days she felt broken.
But slowly, something shifted.
Without him, she could hear her own thoughts again.
She began asking herself, What do I need?
She spoke to herself kindly—not because she was healed, but because cruelty was no longer an option.
What was taken from her did not empty her.
It revealed her
She understood then—some losses are not meant to be replaced.
They are meant to transform us.
And in choosing to live gently, consciously, and honestly,
she turned her grief into a language—
one that could reach others still searching for breath. She rebuilt it wiser.
Rooted in truth. And strong enough to carry love—even when it comes without hands to Hold.
 
By,
Awataf Nezari.

Time never took her away all at once. It removed her the way old paint peels from a wall slowly, without asking, leaving behind lighter patches where something once belonged. At first, she was everywhere. In the cup that stayed warm longer than it should. In the pause before my phone screen went dark. In the way I still turned slightly to the left while walking, making space for someone who no longer walked beside me.
People say memory fades.
They are wrong.
Memory doesn’t fade. It changes shape.
Some days, it was sharp. It cut unexpectedly on a random Tuesday, at a traffic signal, in the middle of a sentence I was saying to someone else. I would stop speaking, mid-word, because my mind had drifted to the sound of her laughter ! soft, brief, as if it didn’t want to disturb the air. Other days, memory was dull. Heavy. Sitting in my chest like a stone I had learned to breathe around. Time watched all of this without interference. It never comforted 
me. It never explained anything. It simply kept moving, dragging memories along like shadows that refused to detach.
I tried to be practical about love. I told myself that feelings, like seasons, must end. That attachments are habits, and habits can be broken. I rearranged my routine. I stopped visiting places where we had been. I even changed the way I thought avoiding certain words, certain songs, certain silences.
But memory is not obedient.
It returned at night, when clocks grew louder and the world finally stopped pretending it was busy. I would lie still, staring at the ceiling, replaying moments I never realized I was storing.
The way she listened more than she spoke. The way her eyes softened when she was about to say something honest. The way she existed quietly, without demanding space, yet somehow occupying all of it. I wonder if she remember me in the same accidental ways.
Time did not answer.
There was a particular memory I avoided the most. Not because it was painful but because it was ordinary. We had once stood under a sky that wasn’t trying to be beautiful. No sunset. No rain. Just a plain, uneventful evening. She had said nothing important. Neither had I. And yet, something settled between us, a sense of this moment will matter later, though we didn’t know why.
That memory returned one evening without permission.
I was older then. Quieter. Less hopeful in careless ways. I realized how love does not always announce itself. Sometimes it whispers so softly that only time understands what was said. 
 I used to believe that forgetting was the final stage of love. Now I know better.Forgetting is not an ending. It is simply another form of remembering one that no longer needs accuracy. Time had altered her in my mind. The sharp edges were gone. The mistakes softened. What remained was not the real person, but the idea of her the version shaped by longing, distance, and things left unsaid.
I didn’t know if that was fair to her.
I didn’t know if love survives as truth or illusion when time stretches too far. Sometimes, I imagine meeting her again not dramatically, not in rain or coincidence. Just somewhere ordinary. A queue. A road. A passing moment. I wonder if time would collapse then, or stand quietly aside and let us recognize each other as strangers with shared ruins.
Would memory rush forward?
Or would it hesitate, unsure if it will still welcome? Time does not prepare you for these questions. It only makes sure you ask them when answers no longer matter. I have learned to live with this uncertainty. To carry love without direction. To accept that some stories are not meant to resolve, only to exist unfinished, echoing.
Even now, there are moments when I pause, sensing something familiar just out of reach. A feeling without a face. A memory without a date. Love, perhaps reshaped by time into something quieter, darker, and harder to name.
I don’t search for her anymore.
I don’t wait either.
But sometimes, when the night stretches longer than it should, I feel time slow down as if it is about to return something it borrowed long ago.
And I wonder
not if she will come back,
but if I would recognize the version of myself
that once loved her this way……

poem1_theme: A story about grief that refuses to release love, choosing fragile illusion over devastating truth in order to survive absence.
poem1_content:
She Was Here

Slapping the file shut, I exhaled sharply, my temples throbbing from hours of staring at the same line of text. The house was silent—too silent. I pushed back my chair and headed downstairs to make myself a cup of coffee.

As I reached the last stair, I stopped short. There she was, dancing barefoot in the kitchen, the hem of her dress swaying as she whisked something in a cup. Her laughter, soft and melodic, rippled through the air.

I blinked, confusion creeping in. She wasn’t supposed to be here.

Shaking off the thought, I walked toward her, as if pulled by some invisible thread. My arms wrapped around her waist, her warmth instantly grounding me. She tilted her head back, her hair brushing against my cheek, and I kissed her temple. Turning her around to face me, I held her close, feeling her heartbeat against mine.

Her arms found their way around my neck, and her lips brushed against mine—so light, so fleeting, it almost felt unreal.

"Quit staring," she teased, her voice teasing yet tender. "Have your coffee. I know you love working all night, but it’s not healthy."

I opened my mouth to respond, but the words caught in my throat. Something about this moment felt... wrong. Too perfect, too seamless.

She stepped past me, and I turned instinctively, reaching to brush my fingers through her hair. But my hand met only air.

I froze.

The kitchen was empty.

No sound of her laughter, no cup on the counter. The air was still, devoid of the warmth she had just brought into it. My chest tightened as my eyes darted around the room, searching for some trace of her. But there was nothing.

My gaze flicked to the mantle, where her photograph sat. The same photograph I stared at every day. The same photograph I sometimes spoke to when the silence became unbearable.

"Not again," I whispered, my voice trembling. I pressed a hand to my forehead, closing my eyes tightly, willing the image of her away.

But I could still feel her—her warmth, her touch, the ghost of her laughter echoing in my ears.

"I’m losing it," I muttered to no one in particular.

The kettle whistled, snapping me out of my thoughts. Mechanically, I poured the water, my hands shaking as I tried to steady myself.

This wasn’t the first time. She appeared often—when the nights were too quiet, when the weight of her absence pressed too heavily on my chest. I knew she wasn’t real. I told myself that over and over again.

And yet, every time she vanished, the loss felt new, raw, as if she’d slipped away all over again.

I gripped the counter, my knuckles white. "You’re not real," I whispered to the silence. "You’re just my mind playing tricks on me."

But even as I said it, I knew I would welcome her back the next time she appeared, clinging to the illusion of her presence. Because losing her once had been unbearable. And even if it wasn’t real, I couldn’t bear to lose her again.

वह जो अपना सा था 
अब अपना सा क्यूँ नहीं है  ?
जो खाते थे कसम कभी , हमेशा साथ  रहने का 
आज पराए से क्यूँ है 
जो हो जाते थे कभी घायल , जरा सी तकलीफ  में मेरी 
आज इतने खामोश से क्यूँ हैं  ?
पेशानी में मेरी धर कर कर अपनी हथेली,  जो भांपा करते थे मिजाज  मेरा 
आज नाखुश सा क्यूँ हैं  ?
सुबह - शाम दर्शन से मेरे , जो बनाते थे अपने दिन का माहौल 
आज जमाने से वो , नदारद क्यूँ हैं  ?
कितना आसानी से कह दिया था ... भुल जाना मुझे 
क्या खुद भुला पाए  ?
जो चले थे कभी मुहब्बत और सफलता का इतिहास गढ़ने
क्या- क्या गढ़ पाए ? क्या इजहार कर पायेंगे मुझसे  ?
जमाना हमसे है , हम जमाने से नहीं 
का दम भरने वाले .... आज आप स्वयं कँहा है  ?
सपनों की दुनिया में खोने वाले , कल्पनाओं की नित नई दुनिया बसाने वाले 
जरा बताओ ना अब अपनी सपनों एंव कल्पनाओं की दास्तान

वह शाम अब नहीं आती
----------------------------
वह शाम अब नहीं आती 
जब थी बरस चाँदनी आसमान से
मैं थी गुम अपने प्रागंण में बिछी चटाई पर
होकर गजलों में लीन 
तुम आए चुपके से और जिस तकिए पर टिकी थी मैं
उसे हौले से हिला खिलखिला पड़े थे 

वह बरसात की शाम भी नहीं आती 
जब ताबड़तोड़ बारिश में 
मेरे घर का मुख्य दरवाजा अंधाधुंध पीटा था तुमने 
और मेरे दरवाज़े खोलने पर 
मेरे हर सवालिया निगाहों को दरकिनार कर 
बारिश में खींच लिया था मुझे

वह शाम भी नहीं आती 
जब दोनो तरफ पकी हुई धान के खेतों के बीच की 
पहाड़ी डामर सड़क पर अपनी - अपनी सायकिलों पर 
हमारा लगातार फिसलते चले (चलाते ) जाना 

वह शाम भी नहीं आती   
जब दूर क्षितिज पर हो रहा था सुर्यास्त 
जग सारा लालिमाग्रस्त हो चला था 
जिसकी लालिमा मेरे सलोने चेहरे पर पड़ रहा था 
तुम अवाक हो जिसे देख रहे थे 
तुम्हारी उन आँखों की आब तो
मैं अब भी सह नहीं पा रही 

वह शरद् की शामें 
समय से पहले अंधकार 
ठिठुरता देह और 
शर्म की दीवार 

बहुत याद आता है 
हर सांझ तले 
सड़क किनारे मेरा तुम्हारा इंतजार करना 
फिर धीरे - धीरे तुम्हारा आना कम होना 
और फिर एक दम से आना बंद हो जाना 

बहुत कुछ सीखा 
बहुत कुछ जाना 
बहुत कुछ झेला 
उन शामों से 
जो अब नसीब में नहीं 
बहुत याद आता है 
क्योंकि वे शामें अब नहीं आती

poem1_theme: Quiet love and slow heartbreak — love that wasn’t loud or dramatic, but deeply rooted, and a loss that happened gradually through silence, distance, and emotional withdrawal.
poem1_content:
I 
loved you
in the quietest ways
not in grand gestures,
but in how my heart learned
your absence before your goodbye.
I loved you in pauses,
in unsent messages,
in prayers I never spoke aloud
because I was scared
love would hear them and leave.
You were my favorite feeling,
the one I trusted too easily.
I built a home in your words,
and you left without telling me
I was standing alone.
They say time heals,
but they never tell you
how it also erases
how slowly,
your voice becomes a memory
instead of a habit.
I don’t miss you loudly anymore.
I miss you in ordinary moments:
songs I skip,
places I avoid,
versions of myself
that only existed with you.
And maybe the saddest part is this
I would still recognize your pain
before my own,
even after you taught me
how disposable love can feel.
I didn’t lose love.
I lost the person
I was when I believed
we were forever.

poem1_theme: Duniya ki mohabbat ne pehle khud se juda kiya, Aur phir wahi faasla, sach se milaya. Woh ishq jo labon par muskaan ban kar reh gaya, Aakhirkar khamoshiyon mein rooh ka bojh ban gaya. Bahut guroor tha ke hum kisi ke liye kaafi hain, Phir ek sach ne dikhaya—hum bas saaya hain. Us ishq ne na chehra maanga, na wada, na qasam, Bas itna kaha: “Apne aap ko halka kar, aur chal.” Jab main apne “main” se haath uthaya, Tab jaa kar sukoon ne mujhe gale lagaya. Na woh mila, na main paaya, Magar is haar ne mujhe woh diya Jo jeet se bhi na mil paya. Duniya ka ishq shayad zaroori hai, Taake samajh aaye—jo khud ko tod kar jod de, Wohi asal mohabbat khelaye
poem1_content: Yeh nazm batati hai ke duniya ki mohabbat pehle insaan ko apne aap se door karti hai, phir jab woh toot’ti hai to sach aur sukoon ka raasta dikhati hai. Guroor aur “main” chhoot jaata hai, aur insaan samajh leta hai ke asal mohabbat wahi hai jo khud ko tod kar rooh ko halka aur haqeeqat se jod de.

poem1_theme: A Father's Love Towards His Daughter
poem1_content:
Love is eternal, but I would have never imagined it unless it happened to me. Born July 21, 1994, a girl child never knew where love could take her and what all love had the power to accomplish. I am this little girl, who has always been pampered and cared for by her father. 
I felt that I was the most blessed soul on this planet, who has been blessed by such an amazing dad. But, as we know, we have to pay a heavy price for all the good things in life that we take for granted. 
I was born to two wonderful parents who have been relentlessly working to make my life better. Being a lawyer, my father used to teach me the importance of discipline, hard work, and perseverance.
On 29 October 2011, my life was upside down as I lost my father forever. He passed away due to an ailment, and I was lost as to what I would do without him. 
This loss of identity felt like love at -1. I could not describe that feeling of shock chilling down my spine when I heard of this loss. 
Nevertheless, god helped me a lot on this difficult journey to accept the loss and move forward.

मैं उड़ना चाहती हू 
अपनी मंजिल तक जाना चाहती हू 

पता है रास्ते मे बहुत परेशानी है 
मैने हमेशा खुद को अकेला पाया है 
मै उसी अकेलेपन से कामयाब होना चाहती हू 

बड़े होते होते प्यार का मतलब समझ मे आ गया है 
वही प्यार मै सभी को देना चाहती हू 

छोटी थी तो पता नहीं था क्या सही है और क्या गलत 
पर अब गलत के खिलाफ लड़ना चाहती हू 

हमेशा मम्मी पापा ने मेरा साथ दिया है 
मै भी इनका सहारा बन कर इनके साथ रहना चाहती हू 

एक शख्स है जिन्होंने मेरा अतीत  जान कर मुझे अपना बनाया 
मै अब अपनी आखिरी साँस तक उनके साथ रहना चाहती हू 

वो कहते है ना ज़िंदगी मे रिस्क नहीं लिया तो क्या किया 
मैं उसी रिस्क को अपनी जिम्मेदारी लेकर सबकोगलत साबित करना चाहिए हू

Look! my burning home
Burning on the old stagnant rain and foam,
Oh! My home wishes a bath,
He is a flammable shelter upon mud;
Sometimes I wish it to burn down
There will be no fire on food to churn,
But, it's my home, my peace!
My flammable orchestration at ease.
My life here surrounds mist of stories 
The dark forest protects it’s glories;
Although they bring fire to the walls,
They are species of plants, not human dolls 
They share the pain and pleasure of fire,
Hugs tight, fear to lose even the liar;
That fire resembles stars long lost in power 
Burning all dreams from the ancient hour,
Only conscience takes light years to come
Burnt air calls Grim reaper to this home.
The long lost star say,
burning is the only way
Heartbeat loses the clock tide
My spirit keeps the eye open wide,
I scream on top of my lung
But alas! There's no one to hear the song.

In the early spring,
The luckiest flower,
belongs to me.

In that garden full of
Sweet fragrance. 
Lucky flowers that nurture 
With love.
Belongs to me.

In between,
the tip of my pen and the paper.
I found us. 
The chapter of love.
Chapter belongs to us.
Unlimited words,
Unlimited hopes,
Everlasting futures.
The luckiest flower,
Starts blooming.
In the garden, 
Of my heart.

The busy pen,
sharing the chapter.
Tirelessly....
My garden of heart,
Full of Sweet fragrance.

The plot change,
With the tempestuous skies.
The fragrance in the chapter 
Vanished...
With the weeping sky,
The luckiest flower
Destroyed.
The papers is still fresh.
The ink is still full.
The characters disappears.
The pen stays still 
At the same page.

The pen is still up,
To continue the chapters.
BUT
The character disappears,
The words vanished,
The questions,
That has never answer.

Still waiting for the answer.
Still waiting to continue the chapter.
Still waiting for the luck flower, 
To bloom again.
The chapter is still open,
But the pages are blank.
Still waiting for the chapters,
To continue,
That belongs to us.

poem1_theme: The poem represents the unrequited love ans quiet grief with emotional restraint.
poem1_content:
I love him, but not enough to turn the ache into art.
I love him, and it ruins me in parts.
I love him, knowing it will never be returned.
I love him, though my thoughts pause where his end.
I love him enough to carry the weight for two.
I call my love a home—it’s true,
but never one he’ll choose to move into.

I love it when they say, “May all your wishes come true,”
and I smile, because every wish is you.
How do you admit you’re loving someone
who will never call you the love of their life?
How do you survive the thought
of him choosing another as his wife,
living the dream I rehearsed in my head—
the kisses on countertops, the words never said?

I imagine how love will soften his gaze,
how her name will sound holy on his face.
How their eyes will meet and quietly decide,
and my calm will shatter—no place left to hide.
Love will hang heavy in the air they breathe,
and she’ll run her fingers through his hair with ease.
He’ll smile at her the way I waited for,
and wine glasses will clink like a closing door.

How do I hate a love so real, so kind?
How do I cure a heart left behind?
How do I stop calling you mine
when I was only a pause in your time?
She’ll be your sunshine, warm and whole—
and I’ll be the shadow that loved you alone.
poem2_theme: This poem speaks about how grief can turn you into something you lost!
poem2_content:
I love who I am after you.
More humble, more polite, more quiet.
More confused, more numb—but maybe right.
I love that I’m aware of the loss,
yet still don’t cry or whine.
I love that I’m over the begging and screams.
I love that I can roam and dress free.
I know it will bother me—it always will.
But now I also know I have free will.
I can pour my thoughts onto pages,
do the things I left for ages.
Your love was never toxic,
nor was it gentle either.
It made me feel full,
yet left me to wither.
But now that I’ve learned how to bloom,
I love who I am
when I’m not with you.

poem1_content:
Scattered pieces of glass-
Within me.
Scattered pieces of glass-
Bled me. 

My heart-
Fierce yet delicate. 
As hot as fire-
But-
As delicate as glass. 

Frau girly, Frau friend-
You broke architecture- 
Of my heart. 
Frau girly, Frau friend-
You are responsible for the pieces-
Of my heart. 




My mind clouded, 
As dark as grayish sky. 

My eyes filled,
With tears on the brim. 

The rain fell-
From my eyes. 
The rain fell-
Not from the skies. 

Your words hurt-
As being Stabbed in the chest. 
Your actions hideous-
A child playing hide and seek. 

Silence followed me-
Like my closest friend. 
Solitude followed me-
Like my dearest friend. 

My fractured mind came after-
Then,
Your cold absence came after. 
Then,
I pulled out-
A sword. 

A sword from snow shrine-
Bright and blue. 
To conquer the hue. 
Set up by you. 

It is because....... 

You left me-
Like an empty can in a bin. 
You left me-
With the broken pieces of glass. 

Alone.

poem1_title: To Kindle a Heart is to Burn a Life
poem1_theme: A heart unfrozen is a heart undone
poem1_content:
I squeeze my eyes shut as strong gusts of wind cut into my skin like knives. My knuckles turn white as I grip the iron railings of the balcony with one hand, and smooth the edges of my skirt to keep it from lifting with the other. The doors that lead into my chamber are halphazardly closed behind me, and the only noises that cut through the silence are the crashing waves hundreds of feet below and the jarring rhythm of my pulse.
My mind is calm as I look up at the spatter of stars trailing the falling sun. I fight the torrents of wind to hoist myself over the edge and loosen my grip on the railing.
The wind, finally free to devour its prey, wraps itself around me and hurls me down to its spiderweb. 
Air rushes out from my lungs as my captor’s hand reaches inside to pry out my final breaths. My watery eyes sting from the salt beneath us.
The seconds still as memories of my life flood my brain, filling the emptiness in my last moments. A merciless reminder of the curse I was born with, a heart frozen in ice, never to feel. 
	However, as I fall hundreds of feet through the air, a puzzle of forgotten emotions with splinters and missing fragments is pieced together slowly. It suffocates me, yet I cannot choke or cry out. This must be what they call pain. It is subduing. 
	                     I wish I could see my mother one last time. 
My heavy eyelids close once they have drunk the water of the sea, the clouds, and all the stars in the sky. Every choice I made brought me to this moment. I would never see, hear, taste, or think again. 
	Without warning, the wind makes a sharp turn, lifting me into its embrace. A firm, yet kind hand presses against the small of my back, leading me as we soar through the sunset’s crimson hues on a lush tapestry. 
	I let out a laugh as the wind laps at my face, swirling around us in exuberance. The one sitting behind me, powerful enough to harness the current and pacify its temper, had stolen me from fate’s grip.
 He steers us, and as we sail down towards the sea once more, I caress the waves with my fingertips. The air hums as we glide. 
“Who are you?” I whisper, shifting to clutch the carpet’s tassels. 
The figure behind me stiffens, his hand trembling for a moment, before he regains his composure.
He regards me with silence, which only fuels my curiosity. What could he be hiding?
I tilt my head toward the far east isles, hoping to catch a glimpse of him from the corner of my eye. 
	The form behind me leaves me as breathless as I was moments ago. I twist my body so we face each other. 
His long, golden hair was wild and untamed, like a beast’s, contrasting the darker tones of his skin. A pair of striking jade eyes lined in kohl pierced me with a gaze unlike any other I had met.  It sent shivers down my spine to behold, yet I couldn’t look away.
	He is the most bewitching man I have seen in all the kingdoms. My heart hammers fast, almost leaping out of my chest. 
	“I am Haizea, daughter of the sultan,” I murmur, scanning his features for a flash of recognition.
	None comes.
He merely tugs at the tassels to the side of the carpet, jerking it forward. Gently, he turns me back and tightens his grip so I face the horizon again.
	“Do not look back again, Haizea,” comes his warning in my ear, before we surge upwards, sweeping the clouds in our wake.
My heart throbs in this feeling of pain once more. A feeling that would ease with another glance at him. 
The moon guides us onwards as darkness swallows the sky. I cannot bear to see it. Even the light from the heavens is pale tonight. My insides burn with desire and curiosity as we ascend higher. I have never yearned for something more. To see his sculpted form, hear his enticing voice, to feel again. 
The gods have given me a reason to live with this gift, I conceive. To be his. 
Why could I not see what is mine by right?
With a burst of adrenaline, I whip my head around and take a long swig of him. His magnificence is endearing. My mother is going to love him. 
	His eyes glow brighter than the stars, and he locks them with mine. After what seems like an eternity, his soft red lips curve into a grin.
	The man’s hair is on fire, and soon, he is engulfed in its flames.
The scorching heat singes the hair on my body as I stare frozen in horror. What have I done?
My frigid heart cracks and begins to melt with his beautiful flesh like thick wax, and morphs his perfect features into a grotesque visage. 
I hear a thump, and then another as two massive wings sprout from his upper back.
Searing waves of heat blister my blackening skin as the carpet beneath me frays, threatening to crumble.
The creature, now sailing through the air, turns to look at me with scorn that stills to a softness like before.
I have done this, I realise sickeningly. I had so little time to know pain, to soar, to love. 
Perhaps my curse was not a misfortune, but a boon. Feelings make people repulsive, selfish, and greedy, but they also give them purpose and joy in the world. 
	The sea cradles me as I plummet into it, wrapped in this seal of fulfilled destiny, as salt fills my lungs and the water drags me to its depths.

Why did you burst while I was aching?
You changed everything 
And now I can't get over it.

I'm ripped apart ,
I couldn't sense it all ;
This feels surreally realistic.
I struggle to grasp this fleeting truth.
Am I cursed?

I feel something heavy 
Making my heart thump.
It pricks.
It bleeds .
It will.
It's who I'm, 
Vulnerable and soft 
Like a bloom in the garden.

The silence of the long morning wore itself on his face. His phone lay lifeless where he had placed it, in last night's hazy attempt, near the charging pod. He had forgotten in his overtaking slumber, to turn the switch on. And the morning was already dusking. 
3.40. He checked it one last time before finally deciding the creak halfway out of his bed, phone still low on power. Half a leg still tucked in, he reached for his sole companion these days. A smooth puff. The stale air around him filled up with the smokey scent, dragging on his days of survival. 
6th Feb. He crossed it out. 
A whole year and a month. What once seemed an outrageous undoing at life, was now survival. His belongings; the tattered pair of shirt and old denim, his toothbrush, the last few bites of the bagel he barely had the appetite for; were all he had left. And a cup of fresh-brewed coffee. 
The cold breeze danced at his fingertips. 
'If only this winter gave in...'
The restlessness settled into surrender.
Every day has been the same, every day since this very day he refused consolation for a life he never wanted. 6th February, 2016. 
The stained coffee cup, now empty and deserted, lay by the window where he was sat hours ago. At a corner, he sprawled on his chair and scribbled down in his old messy way, a restless mind trapped in an ailing body.
'You must be wondering, how February treats me. The busy world and its buzzing hum, the citylights upon its cold air, keeps forgetting me. And I forgive them. If you were here, I'd be hearing about caffeine, and my sins. This existence of my unwillingness stinks. If you were here, you would live. I would write u limericks. This morning, these stars, haunt me. You never died, you barely ever lived.'
Night sets in sooner than will. It lies crumbled, another in the lot that covered up the bin. He had run out of paper, his only other belonging.

The Boy I Never Held
was never a choice,
only a presence that stayed unnamed.
We existed in separate timings,
like sentences written in different tenses,
aware of each other
but never aligned.

Time moved.
I learned how to substitute,
how to accept what was offered
without asking whether it was enough.
I stood where I was placed,
answered when spoken to,
and let repetition replace desire.
It was efficient.
It was survivable.

When the past resurfaced,
nothing collapsed.
There were no confessions,
no visible damage.
Just a quiet recognition
that passed between two people
who already knew the outcome.
Some connections are acknowledged
only to be dismissed,
stored carefully where memory cannot interfere.

Life continued in an orderly way.
Plans were finalized.
Promises were arranged.
I adjusted my voice,
my expressions,
my expectations.
There was no resistance—
only compliance refined into habit,
practiced daily until it felt natural.

What remains inside me
has no shape, no demand, no future,
only the echo of something
that was never allowed to become real,
softly, unnamed, unfinished, unchosen, remembered, silenced, unresolved, lingering.

Love is not fireworks every night,
it is the quiet hand finding yours
in a crowded room of doubts.
It is learning the language of pauses,
of unsent texts, of tired eyes
that still search only for you.

Relationships are not built on promises alone,
but on showing up when the shine fades.
On choosing “us” on ordinary days,
when romance wears the clothes of routine.
It is laughter echoing after arguments,
forgiveness folded neatly into apologies,
and growth that sometimes hurts before it heals.

Love asks us to be brave without armor,
to reveal scars without shame.
It teaches that staying is a verb,
and effort is its truest synonym.
Two imperfect souls do not complete each other—
they walk side by side, learning, unlearning,
holding space for change.

In love, we do not lose ourselves;
we expand.
We become softer, stronger, kinder.
And in that shared becoming,
we discover that love is not possession,
but a partnership of patience,
where hearts choose each other—
again and again.

We were never a beginning
just a soft subtraction
of what could have been

You left gently
as if love were something
that could be returned unused

I kept the shape of you
not your name
not your promises
only the space you vacated

Some nights
I almost reach for you
then remember
absence has learned my hands

Love did not end
it simply stepped back
one pace behind my heart

Now
I live with what remains
not heartbreak
just one less
than love
poem2_content:
I hear your absence
in the quiet corners of the day
where words once lingered

Your shadow moves
just beyond my reach
not cruel, not kind
simply elsewhere

I trace the memory of your voice
like a bookmark
in a book I will never finish

Love did not vanish
it dissolved into echoes
small, persistent
reminders of what we were

And I wait
not for return
not for apology
only for the soft understanding
that we existed
once, together,
and that is enough

poem1_content:
Aee dil sambhal jaa zara,
Dard ke sahare jeena seekh le,
Khushi dhundne ka naatak bnd kar,
Gham ke saare jaam pii le.

Aee dil sambhal jaa zara,
Woh Gaya to use jaane de,
Mat ho naraz uski bewafai se,
Use tujhe aur tadpane de.

Aee dil sambhal jaa zara,
Uski khudgarzi ko ab tu bhula de,
Mat kar shikwa uski berukhi ka,
Naa uske fareb ka sila de.

Aee dil sambhal jaa zara,
Uski aadato ka nasha chhor de,
Bepanah mohabbat kar chuka hai tu,
Ab nafrat me saari hadein tod de.

Aee dil sambhal jaa zara,
Khud ko ab rab ke hawale karde,
Uski yaadon ko ab tu nikal de,
Aur use waqt ke hawale karde.

Aee dil sambhal jaa zara,
Uske laut ke aane ka intezar mat kar,
Tu mar jayega uske intezar me,
Apni maut ke aane ka intezar mat kar.
poem2_content:
Once again, I decided that I'll take a long walk to clear my head. I grabbed my headphones, played my favorite song and set out. And let me tell you there's nothing better than a long walk with your favorite song playing. 
So, as I was saying, I set out, walking on the streets, thinking about life and it's fluctuations. I walked into Starbucks, bought my favorite latte, and that was when I saw him. Again. The old man. Across the street, in the park, sitting on that same old bench. That was the nth time that I had seen him sitting on that bench. All alone.He was not from the neighborhood, neither did he talk to anybody, just stared at nothing in particular, or so I thought. 
Curiosity got better of me - or maybe because I had time in my hands that day - I walked towards him. As I neared the bench, he showed no acknowledgment of me approaching, whatsoever. Just sat there staring at the same spot. 
I sat down, not knowing what to do next. There was something enigmatic about that man. "You come here often," I started. "YOU come here often, I come here everyday. I have noticed you observing me from that coffee shop across the street." Oh! He knew! "Yes! You intrigue me," "What's so intriguing to you about an old man?" He let out a humorless laugh. "You're not from around here, yet you come here everyday, sit on this same tattered bench, and stare at nothing, that's not something old people do usually." He remained quiet for exactly 7 minutes. (Yes, I counted)
And what he said next was something that's imprinted on my to this day. Those were the words that I'll never forget. It was a story that'll remain carved on my soul for as long as I'm alive. 
"You see that building there? That's where the love of my life, the woman who rules my heart, and who once turned my house into a home, lives."
"Once?"
"Yes, once. Now she has a beautiful family. Children, grandchildren, and a man she calls her home."
I kept looking at him, waiting for him to continue, to complete the story that he had begun. I don't know why, but there was something in those weary eyes that glued me there.
"It was the spring of 1965, I was here, on this same bench, sitting and thinking about life, when I saw her for the first time. She was wearing a blue saree with white and pink flowers printed on it. Her brown hair were left open and they reached her waist. She had a silver waistchain and her anklets made a sound everytime she moved. Sunlight kissed her face and her face displayed beautiful dimples everytime she smiled. She was happily playing around with little kids, enjoying the bliss of fresh air, totally unaware about the havoc she had caused in my heart. A kid put a jasmine in her hair and my heart skipped a beat. 
From that day, I started coming here everyday, just to catch a little glimpse of her, just to watch her smile, just to hear the sound of her anklets. 
Eventually, she started noticing me too. A glance at my way, or a bashful smile. My mornings were brighter and nights were cozier. She ruled my heart, my thoughts, ran deep into my blood. I was madly, hopelessly, and irrevocably in love with her. 
I approached her, she smiled. I joked, she laughed. She cried, my heart bled. I embraced, she melted. I asked her to marry me, she said yes. Life was beautiful. 
She was there when I woke up, when I went to sleep, when I ate, when I walked, when I talked. She was everywhere. She turned my plain house into a nurturing home. And let me tell you once again, that life was beautiful. 
Until that one day, February 15, 1986. She met with an accident. Hit her head, and lost her memories. And I lost my everything. Those eyes- which were once filled with immense love for me- didn't recognize me. 
I tried. I tried making her remember. Brought her here. Showed her every corner where we spent time together, but couldn't bring those memories back. Eventually, my efforts started suffocating her, and I knew it was the time to let go. I did. I let her go. I watched her play with the kids again. I watched her enjoy the bliss of air again. I watched her smile bashfully again, just not at me.  I watched her fall in love again, just not with me. I watched her create a home again, just not with me, all from this bench. 
For 40 years, everyday, I have watched her. I have watched her live her life. I have watched her grow into a beautiful, beautiful woman. And I will continue to do so, till my last breath because 
"Woh Ishq hi kya jisame Fanaa naa ho"."
As he completed, I saw him smile at saw an old lady in a balcony, hand in hand with another man. He wiped a lone tear that fell, patted my head, and walked away leaving me staring into nothing.

poem1_content:
The ocean and I, we have the same hunger to take everything, to hold too much, 
to love the shore even as it turns away, 
to crash, to break, and to still remain.

I've loved you like the waves love the land, reaching, only to be send away, 
I pled with salt stained hands, 
but I know you don't belong to me,
You never did.

Morning cames by and I ask myself 
everyday, religiously! 
"should I kill myself or have a cup of coffee?" 
Should I sink into the deep where no hands reach, or find warmth in something small, something bittersweet, something that reminds me I am still here. 

It is exhausting, the war within me, 
the will to live, the will to leave, 
the hoping, of you might turn around 
and say my name like it meant something.

But you walk the world unburdened, 
never drawning, never gasping for air 
while I'm all storm beneath the sky, 
aching to be held, aching to be known.

The ocean refuses no river, 
yet you refused me 
And so I remain 
a tide untamed,
aching to be held, aching to be known.

In a world full of chaos, illusions, and addictions, humans live under the pressure of society’s expectations. People judge one another, compare lives, spread rumours, and tear others down whenever they get the chance. Misunderstandings and jealousy create confusion, and kindness is often mistaken for weakness.
But even in such a world, there are still a few people who care — who love deeply and help each other through time. This is a story about one of those rare souls.
Vasant was different from others. People said there was a light in his eyes — a quiet determination that whispered he was meant for great things. Born in the spring, he was cheerful, compassionate, and always ready to help. Yet his kindness often made him a target. Some people took advantage of him, others spread rumours, and many misunderstood his gentle nature.
Still, Vasant never complained. Deep down, he knew the truth about himself, and that was enough.
He studied Media Science in college because he wanted to become something greater — an actor who could tell real stories and change the world with his art. His parents, however, did not support his dream, believing it to be foolish. But Vasant refused to give up.
One winter evening, while returning from college, Vasant decided to stop at a small café to warm himself with a cup of coffee. The café had an unusual name — Dead Love. Curious, he went inside and called out softly.
A girl’s gentle voice replied from the kitchen, “Yes, I’m coming, please have a seat.”
Moments later, she stepped out — and for the first time, Vasant felt something stir inside him. Her eyes were calm yet deep, and her presence carried a quiet sadness. She placed the coffee on his table.
“Sir, your coffee.”
He looked at her and felt an unfamiliar warmth in his chest. But before she could walk away, he asked, “Why is this café called Dead Love?”
The girl smiled faintly.
“My parents loved each other… but life separated them. They divorced, and after that, love felt dead to me. I built this café with my grandparents to remember them — and to survive. This place is all I have.”
Her honesty touched Vasant. He felt a strange connection — something he couldn’t yet explain.
She introduced herself as Ritu.
They talked for a while, and Ritu soon asked about him. Vasant confessed his dream of becoming an actor, even though his parents didn’t support him. Ritu listened carefully, then smiled.
“Why don’t you practice in my studio? I rehearse my singing there. You can use it too.”
Vasant agreed, his heart quietly blooming with hope.
Growing Closer
Days turned into weeks. After college, Vasant went to Ritu’s studio, where she practiced her vocals and he rehearsed his scripts. They laughed together, shared personal stories, and slowly became inseparable.
They didn’t just become friends — they became each other’s safe place.
One evening, after rehearsal, they sat silently in a café. Their hearts beat faster, words trapped inside them. Finally, both spoke at the same time.
“I want to tell you something…”
Their voices overlapped — and they smiled.
Vasant gently took her hand and said,
“Ritu, I admire you. Your voice feels like it reaches my soul. Whenever you sing, my heart melts. I love you.”
Time froze. Tears filled Ritu’s eyes.
“I always loved you,” she whispered. “You filled the emptiness in my life.”
They embraced, and from that day, their worlds became one.
But happiness is fragile.
Rumours began spreading in their neighbourhood. People whispered, judged, and accused Ritu of being a “bad girl.” One day, Vasant’s parents showed him a picture of Ritu hugging him — proof, they said, that she was ruining his life.
Heartbroken, Ritu disappeared.
Her house was locked. Her phone switched off.
Days passed — silence.
Vasant finally learned the truth: his parents had forced Ritu to leave the city. They believed love would destroy his future.
Shattered, Vasant cried helplessly — until he remembered their promise:
“We will chase our dreams — and one day, we will find each other again.”
He swore he would become great enough to find her, no matter where she was.
Five Years Later
Vasant rose to fame — one of the greatest and wealthiest actors in the world. Yet success never filled the emptiness inside him.
One rainy evening, while driving through a crowded street, a woman stepped in front of his car. He ran out to help — and froze.
It was Ritu.
Tears spilled down her cheeks. She tried to walk away — but he caught her hand and says, 
“Please… don’t leave me again. I know what happened. I should have protected you.”
Ritu finally broke down. Both confessed everything that had remained buried in their hearts all those years.
Vasant knelt before her.
“I want you in my life — not as a memory, but as my future. Marry me.”
Ritu hesitated. Fear trembled in her voice.
“What if the world judges us again?”
He smiled softly and said something which makes ritu believe in love once again. 
“Then we will build our own world.”
Slowly… Ritu nodded.
They embraced, tears streaming down their faces — not of pain anymore, but of healing.
And together, they walked toward a future where love was no longer ruined — but reborn. 
There was a time when Vasant felt lonely and he wrote this-
“We Built Our Forever on Borrowed Time 
Promises cracked Like walls Left to decay. 
Your name still echoes in my room of dust, 
Where laughter once bloomed, now
Silent stays.
Even in Ruins, my heart call it love.”

poem1_theme: Poignant Regret, Unnoticed last meet dressed as December
poem1_content:
We never name the last time,
it arrives like December,
wearing an ordinary afternoon.

Our laughter stays warm,
breath fogging the air,
no coldness to warn us
that this is the final rehearsal.

You speak of tomorrow
as winter believes in spring,
I listened the way bare trees do,
patiently.. and still trusting.

Had I known,
I would’ve held your silence closer,
counted the seconds your eyes
lingered on mine,
kept the warmth you left in me,
like hands in a borrowed coat.

They say love fades.
I think it freezes,
preserved in December,
in the last meeting
we never knew
was the last.
poem2_theme: The poem explores a tender attachment to emotional coldness as a form of comfort, healing, and delayed goodbye — where winter becomes a refuge from intensity, longing, and the fear of letting go.
poem2_content:
Oh dear winter,
you know I love you.
The cold you hold
somehow soothes me,
like silence finally finding a home.

I loved you long before you arrived,
even when my hands reached
for warmth you never promised.

Your cold was never cruel
I missed you in summers
when the sun burned my skin,
I ached for you
when I couldn't wear my favorite hoodie 
I needed you
when coffee was just a beverage
and not a warmth between cold palms.

Oh, dear winter,
don’t leave in a hurry.
Stay...
until my bones forget the heat,
until longing learns to rest,
until I learn.. how to let go.

poem1_content:
Aligned Stars

Sitting and brainstroming
How to explain what love feels like?
Listenting to romantic music
Waiting for it to be figured out

Lets start
I wonder how the universe
Has it all written in parts
A meaning behind every story
Purpose of every character
Like its all planned 

The first chapter unfolds
An unplanned arrival
Or planned, as universe wrote
Who knows!
A wave so powerful
