Yet soft, hits a stone
Gently letting it know
That it finally reached the shore

Calming with its force
Amidst the chaos
That feeling of finally
I made it to home

His presence felt like that
Healed something he never caused
Just existing, standing there
Holding me tight
Fixing it all with all his might

Is this love? 
My heart asked
While my mind skeptical
But my heart smiled

Life felt fuller
Hugs felt safer
The soul felt loved
And finally I discovered

For all i have known 
This is love
Its the calm
The wave to the stone
The home
The blessing




The heart has a name
Written on it since long
Engraved deep enough
A scar it seems

A scar screaming
The stages of love
Climbing through pain
And passion for love

The universe has written it all
All the characters predefined
For all the hearts who concile
At the right click of time
The stars will align

Always lived with fear of being judged.
So she stayed away from the outside world.
Building a little universe of her own
Quiet safe and untouched 
Only a few were allowed to enter. 
Her heart was choosy n mind moody 
Yet to those who know her 
She was pure sunshine. 
Fun-loving, warm
Spreading smile and happiness to all

Laado of her loved ones
Pampered with every wish and fulfilled 
Their sweetest weakness 
Wrapped all that love 
All that protection 
And she felt she has already everything she needed  
She lived bindaas. 
Away from outside world safe in the warmth of the few who held her heart

Bubblychubbly by nature 
with a teddy look 
Those front teeth broken 
The part of her she always tried to hide

Feared how world will judge her 
Worried her pronunciation slips. 
Would make her look imperfect 
Always was teased laughed 
Made her shrink into silence and away from all

Beneath all that hurt 
Her soul was glowing 
Still soft 
Still warmth 
But full of life and love to laugh. 

So the question lingers in the quiet of her heart:
Will she ever rise beyond these insecurities?
or remain confined forever?

poem1_theme: Emotional Deep self comunication, all unspoken thoughts
poem1_content:
मेरी हजार ग़लतियों के बाद,
नाराजगी में उसकी,  गुलज़ार तलाश करने की।
मेरे नाम में उसके  जिक्र को,
 आदत है मुझे, हर बार तलाश करने की
आदत है उसके नज़रअंदाज़ में और,
उसके कीमती वक्त में अपना इंतज़ार तलाश करने की।
वो एहसास उसके जो ज़ाहिर नहीं होते,
एक ही बात के, मतलब हजार तलाश करने की।
जब कहता है वो कि उसे क्या मतलब है मुझसे?
उसके जज़्बातों  में इंकार तलाश करने की।
मेरी फ़िक्र में कह देता है वो,  उसे मतलब नहीं मैं कुछ भी करूं,
ना चाहकर भी उस वक्त रोकर अपनी इज़तइरआर तलाश करने की।
 इज़तइरआर- मजबूरी।

रओश्रन हो जाता है उसके नाम से जो मोमबत्तियों  की तरह,
आदत है मुझे अपना वो रुखसार तलाश करने की।
मेरे पागलपन पर जो सिर्फ मेरे लिए मुस्कुरा देता है,
जो सिर्फ मेरे लिए है उसका वो, किरदार तलाश करने की।
लाखों   ग़लतफहमियां भी कुछ नहीं, उसके एक यक़ीन के आगे,
आदत है मुझे उसमें, अपना तरफ़दार तलाश करने की।
 चाहे हालात हो जाए खिलाफ मेरे, उसे तो मुझ पर यकीन है, 
बिना कुछ कहे ये करार तलाश करने की।
आज शायद कुछ बदल जाए, नया दिन है, 
हर दिन यही आसार तलाश करने की।
उसने कहा था कि कुछ भी हो सिर्फ़ उसकी हूं मैं,
उसके लहज़े  में अपना छुपा हुआ हकदार तलाश करने की।
वो   चुप रहकर भी हर बार इतना कुछ कह लेता है,
आदत है मुझे हर कीमत पर बस उसका  प्यार तलाश करने की।
वो जैसे तलाश लेता है खुद को, बिखरे हुए अल्फ़ाज़ों  में मेरे,
बस उसी तरह उसका अनकहा इज़हार तलाश करने की
हो रही हूं मैं अपनी ही उलझनों  में,
आदत हो रही है मुझे, रूके हुए वक्त में रफ्तार तलाश करने की।
 संभाल ले अब तो मुझे, बिखर रही हूं मैं,
अब नहीं चाहिए मुझे ये आदत, हर बात में असरार तलाश करने की। 

क़ीमत  कुछ भी हो पर वो जो साथ है मेरे,
तो कैसे छोड़ दूं अपनी आदत उसमें शरार तलाश करने की।
 मेरे हर सही ग़लत का हिसाब है उसके पास,
मेरी नहीं है चाहत, कोई राज़दार तलाश करने की। 
कोई कह दे तो कैसे, हम दूर हो जाए,
लोगों की तो आदत है, चमकते हीरे में भी दरार तलाश करने की।
यहां हर वो नज़ारा ग़लत है, जिसके कायदे अलग हों,
लोगों की आदत है हर मुस्कुराहट में, कोई हार तलाश करने की।
पर ये सवाल किसी एक क़िताब में डूब जाने का है,
यह कोई आदत नहीं, हर नए दिन नया अख़्बार तलाश करने की।

poem1_theme: Core Themes: Maternal Cruelty Lost Innocence Betrayal of Trust Resilient Hope Core Emotions: Horror Pity Anguish Shock Emptiness
poem1_content:
What did this pitiable soul do to you?
What did this fool compel you to do?
What did she ask of you?
What did she need from you?
Why was she roaming around you?
See this pitiable soul's suffering,
Yearning for love and comfort.
A baby needs its mother's warmth;
How could a good mother forget her child?
She fails to understand
The blabbering of the little one.
How could she become a mother?
She scorched the soft and gentle palm of the child,
The baby's cry is now a melody to her mother.
The saliva of this infant now seems like kerosene
To the hatred of her mother.
The infant crawls to reach her mother on all fours;
The mother runs away from her.
She feels as if some roadside stray is chasing her.
Her breast now turns as hard as rock;
No stone family can produce milk.
Nails of hers scratch the chest of her tot,
Polishing her nails using the blood of the innocent.
Holding her nose with blood-filled hands,
The breath of the child is forced through the mouth.
The woman now closes the mouth of the baby
With her feet upon the thin lips;
She kicked that little body into the bin.
Full of blood, pulse reduced,
The beat of the baby's heart grew faint.
But the mourning beat of the heart
Still hopes to get back into her arms!
poem2_theme: Core Themes: Spiritual Resilience Internal Suffering Divine Protection Victory over Darkness Core Emotions: Agony Defiance Strength Devotion Fearlessness
poem2_content:
Tears ruined my time,
Disease ruined my day,
Stress ruined my seconds.
The devil is sucking my blood
Through his disgusting mouth.
The lifespan of my scars extended;
Day by day, he consumes my skinny flesh.
Slowly, he enjoys his feast,
Spinning my nerves like a toy top.
That top is whirling in my eyeball;
It’s hollowed and filled with bloody tears.
A thorn wounds deeply, digging into my body.
Wounds fester by the hour;
The demon revels in my downfall.
He celebrates a party;
The theme is "her illness."
Do I care? No!
Enjoy well, drink your fill,
Party hard, eat your fill!
But one important note:
Use your cunning eyes to watch me again.
Watch keenly as I hold my hand
With Yahweh’s right hand.
You can never separate me from my God!
My dearest Father, my loyal love!
The Almighty is always beside me.
No one can separate me for a minute from my destiny.
Jesus said, "Go away Satan!"
This little girl from the kingdom of God will say,
"Get lost Satan!"

poem1_content:
Loving you wasn’t a sudden rush. Instead, it felt like a slow, quiet unfolding. It grew through simple conversations and moments that felt so natural, I never thought to question them.

You became my steady ground. A place where I felt safe enough to lean. I stayed in the comfort for a long time, assuming that because you felt like home to me, I felt like home to you, too. I was so settled that I didn’t notice when the love started flowing only from my side. I didn’t see the shift, because by then, loving you no longer felt like a choice, it simply felt like where I belonged.

 

As time went on, I couldn’t ignore how uneven we had become. I was giving everything I had, yet it felt like my efforts disappeared into the distance before they ever reached you. I stayed right there, attentive and ready, even when your silence grew louder than any promise you’d made.

I convinced myself that loving someone meant giving without expecting anything in return. I called it “compromise”, but in truth, I was only making excuses for feeling alone. I didn’t realize that by trying to be understanding, I was actually normalizing being ignored. I was so focused on holding us together that I forgot I deserved to be held, too.

 

I never forced you to love me back. I just stayed, hoping that one day you’d feel the same way I did. Loving you became a habit I couldn’t break, something I carried quietly without needing a thank you. I stayed too long, not because I believed you’d change., but because I didn’t know how to stop.

Eventually, I had to accept that my presence was something you could take or leave. I didn’t argue or try to fix it. I let the truth settle in, heavy and undeniable, realizing that my love had nowhere left to go.

He was walking along the rocks that were scattered across the shore, some covered in moss and some bathed by the salt water that hits from time to time.

He always wondered why it gave him comfort, but he always knew it deep inside him. It depicted his mindset and heart state, the deafening silence with the breeze piercing the ears, making sounds which he didn't want.

He ponders upon the memories, arranging them into boxes as if he has occlumency ability. It would be nice to pack up the unwanted memories and stack them at the back of our mind.

Sudden coldness in his legs brought him out of his own occlumency session. He looked at the running waves and sighed, it would be good if he could move on like them. Free of any emotional boundary and questions.

He spent his time looking at the copper laden sky with freckles of stars peeking out already from their hiding position. The sea mirrored the sky making it more beautiful, and his mind whispered, ‘Things can be beautiful even if it’s just a replica’.

He pondered upon the thought for some more time and decided to return to his reality which will be like a whiplash to the soul after spending time in his place.

He picked his airpods and started his playlist, he made a quick jog towards his car. He occasionally looked at the few people who were loitering around to bask in the sunset glow. While he was busy admiring the looks on the few people who were left around, he felt like someone was staring at him.

He turned around to find a pair of eyes, looking at him with tears brimming. He didn’t need this, not today, not any day in future. He wasn’t ready yet, he knew that and he was fine with that.

But life had other plans and thought to bring the one person who is crushing his heart as it is already. He blinked his eyes as if that would help him to remove the image that he is seeing right now. But reality doesn’t work like that, does it? He sighed and thought this is it. He was also surprised to see that he is fine now and not like he expected.

He expected himself to throw tantrums, throw a fit or worse crumble under those eyes. But he seemed to be doing just fine, there was a phantom pain in his heart but that was something he could handle. He decided to look at her and he looked square in her eyes. She seemed to be in a daze too, he waited for her to come out of her daze. After which seemed to be a lifetime for him, he saw her getting out of her daze.

He cleared his throat, “Hi Meera”, he voiced out more smoothly than he expected. He gave himself a mental pat and steered clear to have a conversation which is going to hurt like hell. “Hi”, she sobered herself up from her dilemma. He nodded with a small smile that he could manage, it was neither friendly nor it contained animosity, a little hurt and a little annoyance.

He just nodded his head again with a wistful look indicating the nearest bench. She looked a little bit rattled but decided to comply with his gesture. After settling down, both resorted to silence and both didn’t want to start a conversation which would leave a sour expression on their heart.

Though he was done with her silences and detachment. He either wanted closure or he wanted her gone, he knew he can manage himself without the closure too. The uncomfortable silence lingered like an unwanted third wheel, neither of them ready to open a conversation first. Arjun seemed to make a decision and he wanted to finish the conversation as soon as possible.

Nobody will blame him for choosing the easy way out too, that is, just walking away from this conversation. But he knew he had to talk. “Did I do something to make you run away from me ?”, he asked with a tinge of hurt and she heard it loud and clear.

She couldn’t bring out an answer when she knows it’s all on her. He was the yin to her yang, but she couldn’t place him before her passion. He was always supportive of her, and she failed to acknowledge that.

She was quiet and that gave him the answer, he decided to ask and say more of what’s haunting him for the past five years. “You know your name used to make me blush and writhe in joy. But do you know what it makes me feel now? Your name hurts Meera”, he said and it felt okay a little bit more.

Arjun felt at ease saying his heart out, he thought it would be in agony and his heart would be bleeding talking to her, but he found peace and he felt like doing the right thing after a long time.

“I knew you cared about me, but I also knew you didn’t care enough.  And honestly, I don’t know which is worse. Even though I felt a lot of longing and pain in the early days, I missed you. You don’t know how fucking pathetic that made me feel”, Arjun said and it felt like a whiplash to her.

She knew she had to hear him, after all she was the one who chose her passion and left the man who promised her, he would stand by her at all times. Looking back she knew he would have stayed, he would have cheered on her best days, and held her on bad days.

Choosing one’s passion isn’t wrong, throwing away someone who is equally invested in your passion is just cruel. Just because you think you can’t handle both passion and the person, you don’t get the right to gut them and leave them bleeding. She knew, she killed a part of him that day and she never forgot it.

“You were my yes, and I was only your maybe.”, he said with a painful laugh and he continued, “Everybody said to move on and you weren’t worth it, but I said no, Do you know why?” he looked at her with tears coating his eyelids, he felt better with each word he said but he was breaking each minute too. Nobody said it would be this much painful.

The phantom pain which always lingered in his heart took form and devastated him. But he felt an unmistakable push to let it all out, he had a huge urge to say it all loud and he knew he was going to do that.

“I broke my rules for you, I don't think you knew, but I was prepared. Yeah, I knew that you would leave, but it hurt.” 

“I know you're sorry, but I need you to understand the gravity of your mistake. You fucked up big time. You took a man who was ready to turn oceans and seas for you and slit his heart into pieces. And do you know the other thing which always runs in my mind?” he asked her with streaks of tears running down his cheeks , the pain which he was experiencing was running down in the forms of his tears and he wasn’t ashamed of that .

Society may condemn that men must not cry, but screw them all. Men do cry, because they are humans and they have emotions too. It isn’t any less and it doesn’t make them weak. In fact, it takes gall to cry and let out the tears.

She looked at the man who loved her endlessly, and it struck her that this is the first time in a long time that she has used the past tense. She thought he will always love her in some form and she was shook that even she acknowledged that he stopped loving her.

She looked at him with red eyes that depicted how she felt in her heart. He let out a painful chuckle looking at her and she knew he understood her but he didn't owe her anymore. 

After looking at her, he knew there was no use in asking her. “As I asked, the one thing that was running in my mind was, Did I even mean something to you?” he asked, hurting him and her even more. But he wasn’t finished. He had years worth of misery crawling in him, those things twist us and tear us like we never expected.

“It was real for me, even when it hurt me. I don’t know what it was for you. But for me, it was real. It meant more than it should have, and I paid the price for it.”

She was shattered to know how much her cowardice cost him, how much it hurt him. She loved him, heck she loves him. And to see him break down, it made her want to run far away again. “You don’t get it…”, she whispered with voice cracking and heart racing a thousand miles per minute. He let out another strangled chuckle.

“I… I don't get it huh, I would’ve thrown everything away for you. Just for you. You ran away breaking promises, just not the promises alone. You broke me, fuck, you killed me by running away ”, he said with his voice cracking much more and he felt like he was burning with his sorrows.

“I thought I almost had you, didn’t I?”, he asked to compose himself a bit. She turned towards him like lightning at his question. “I don’t know Arjun”, she said brokenly and that answer broke the last straw that held them together. “I wanted it to be you, my forever, god damnit, I really did.”, he whispered to himself but she heard him as loud as the day.

With that he heaved a heavy sigh and felt like he did it. He actually did it, for the past five years , all he thought was this. He wanted this day to come, but he was afraid he’d crumble and fail him but hey he did it.

He was elated but drained out of his mind. He felt like someone sucked out his soul. He looked at the sky and noticed it had changed from its copper laden sheet to a dark blue and black hue spreading across the sky with the stars twinkling much brighter.

He looked at her and he saw her crying silently looking at the stars, once upon a time these tears gutted him. Now he was sad for the woman in front of him but not heartbroken anymore.

He turned back and looked at the sea which always gave him peace in some way. “It’s terrifying, isn’t it?” he whispered but he knew she was hearing. “That even love isn’t always enough.” He fell into a comfortable silence with the sea, but she felt like she was thrown into a deep sea bottomless and suffocating.

She wanted to say many things but she felt like nothing was enough. “Thank you for loving me even when I tasted like heartbreak”, she said looking at the stars, but he knew she was talking to him.

He let out a smile and it was genuine, it felt easy, it wasn’t fake anymore. “And I really wish and hope you will find a love, where you don’t have to question it. I know I made you question yourself. I was young and I didn’t know that I could handle you and my passion. I know now, that if I would have let you then, you would’ve been there for me till now. But I was a coward, I hurt you and I wish I didn't." She let it out slowly and she meant it.

“What if I never forget you?” she asked with a hea...
wefbiwbefwewef
poem2_content: wef

poem1_content:
The collar button stifled my neck so I thought of unbuttoning it . It was cold outside. Inside, as we sat around the table, after a hectic day of lectures, power points, chart and discussion, it was not very warm here too.I fiddled with the handle of the mug containing coffee, lingering the process of finishing it.Others were talking. I have a bad habit. I would listen and listen and once in a while the words would turn to a humming music with no meaning and I would be in a space where I am with my own thoughts. An enquiry at a louder decibel made my mind come back to my present state.
“Have you planned for the sleepover here?” 
A satiric query was directed at me by Vineet with a sly smile. 
Yes, the board had stressed at maximum output and that meant doing overtime.
“I guess he would like it, being all alone with his work in cabin. What else he would do even with his spare time?” Nikita remarked as she sipped in her mug.A dig may be, at my state of life.
“I guess ___ we all have to bring our pillows and blankets, Ha.” I said dragging a smile, picking up the mug for a unwilling sip.
A silence stayed for a short time before Avinash rambled something about pushing the work, that extra work should be treated like volleyball, giving it in other’s court, some can’t make the pass. I knew it was about me and my team whose offer to the other department for sharing the responsibility of arrangement of the program had been declined due to date conflict.
What I had done shortly after that was something I had, as per my assumption , attained mastery at. I was now on the stairs, having slowly got up fidgeting my pockets for a cigarette and had made my way upwards on the terrace.

It was cold outside….till…

“Come.” She beckoned me to the edge of the terrace where she stood holding the railing, smiling. I kind of jogged, and joined her.
'-You don’t smoke? Do you?'
-'Nah, just..just a prop. To smuggle out of a place.'
-'Don’t start. It’s not good.'
-'Nah, chill, I have other addiction.’ I looked straight into her eyes leaning a bit. She smiled.
I leaned back , 'Why didn't you come and save me from that boring roundtable?”
-'You call that saving? ___some rescue operation?'
-'Yeah…like some firefighter entering a building on fire.Rescuing me engulfed in smoke,heat,on verge of dying.'
-'You feel that way?'
-'Hmm.. Without you..'
-'There are worse situations people are living…Seen on TV how people are hiding in bunkers to avoid being killed by drone in Iraq ?'
-'Yeah, horrific. You know I saw that day on news, a little child remained trapped for around 10 days. Her parents fled somehow and the house was soon bombed.They thought she died but was found under debris.It is not about the physical pain , she had some cookies and other stuffs lying around which she ate, it was about the loneliness,the helplessness, the sheer suffocation of the silence.'

-'I think that’s the worst experience any one can have. I am not talking about just that news but see, you have so many wandering thoughts you would have liked to share but there is no one. The loneliness. It fades you out.You just churn those words in your brain, buying time from the twenty four hours to perform some muted soliloquy. Just to survive.'
-'Talking to you gives me some respite.'
- 'Talk, I am here.'
- 'Yes, you have to be here.May be a little longer ____ Actually… It is difficult to put them into words. See how funny it is, if you want to talk about things I actually don’t care about , the shares of our company or something and I can go in a flow yapping about them , but the things that I yearn so much to say that takes a lifetime to be put into proper words, language seems insufficient.'
- 'I understand. I am here.'
She had that look in her eyes that convinced me about her every promise.

Silence.

I followed her calm , quiet eyes and it looked at the glittering city lights down the terrace. It seemed we were standing at a different plain. The city looked beautiful at night.The cold breeze somehow added to the solitude that we shared.
-'I love coming here. Watching the city from here.You feel like a distant observer. You stand here and you feel like you have control over time, as if time has stopped for you. But once you are a part of that city, you are in a rush, everything is over so quickly, so swift. I am scared of seeing everything getting done so quickly, getting over, and it’s time for you to die.'
“You are afraid of death?” I asked.
-'_________ Not death, not being dead, not the end.I am and I guess we all are afraid of not being exhilaratingly alive before death. To not to have created enough memories, or something unsaid, undone. The fear is of that death for which you aren’t prepared.'
Silence.
 Our eyes met.The little distance that we had was slowly filled up by now with our bodies.I could feel her warm breath that was so needed in that cold, dark, misty night.The next words were in whispers which only we heard.
-'I …'
-'Hmm' ?
-'I… want to be exhilaratingly alive.And I can’t be without you. Make me feel that way. Give me that one moment of life. I need it see…desperately.'

Before the night ended, I had created and saved some best moments. We had danced, as they say dance like dervish, We had laughed and swirled in circles till the moon and stars seemed to have come falling down upon us.We had sung loudly, I forgot the lyrics, but something so funny that we were out of breath. We had felt alive that night. She had , before leaving promised to meet me again.I had kept on smiling for a long time after we had parted.

 _________________________________________
“Sir, you knew everything but you never informed us !”  Vineet looked worried as he sat across the manager Mr. Alok. “Yes, but he is recovering from the trauma. That is his way. We need to give him the time. And you can’t complain, he is doing his work, carrying on his regular life just like a normal person, yes he has become a bit reserved, but that won’t appear as a big issue for our company.”  Mr. Alok said turning the pages of a file.
“ It was so scary, to tell you the truth. To see him dancing and laughing and having the best time of his life with no one. All alone. But he was talking and singing as if a living person was mimicking the same action as him.”
“Yes, the girl had died untimely and that was a shock.Slowly he created this imaginary world where he found respite in her. That’s the brief his psychologist had told me.”
Both sat in silence for a while.
After a while shaking his head, still in disbelief Vineet was about to leave the cabin. “Madness, pure madness.” He muttered under his breath.
“You know…”Mr. Alok spoke slowly as he looked through the open window as Vineet turned back holding the door knob.
“That girl, the psychologist told me something that day that she had heard or read somewhere and I try to quote, Unless it’s mad, passionate, extraordinary love it’s a waste of your time.There are too many mediocre thing arounds us that we keep on doing but my dear,love, should not be one of them. He can, unlike us, boast that he had done  and  may be still is doing something extraordinary, don’t you think?”

A gentleness that only eyes could see,
A warmth that is only felt.

Heart that basks in love,
Mind that loathes in thoughts.

Littlest sparks that sets up fast,
Littlest talks that yearn the most.

Words never spoken,
Yet was always understood.

Eyes so divine,
Face so serene,
Slips a little much deep.

Euphoria unsettling.
All that was left were desires—
Desires unspoken.

poem1_title: JUST FOR THE SAKE OF LOVE
poem1_content:
Sparsh Bhateja, sometimes, stand and wonders, why?
If love was real, should it have died?
If love resides, a sacred fire,
How can its flame to ash expire?
The promised bond, if fractured now,
Was it love's essence anyhow?
If love can truly die,
Then what we felt was just a lie.


Love me not for my wisdom, or cleverest line,
Love me just for love, because you’re mine.
Love me simply, as the rain,
Loves the earth, again, again.
Love me with the fact, as nothing to gain,
No promise of fortune, no escape from the pain.

Let reasons fade, let logic cease,
You just find love, and gentle peace.
Love me not for what I do,
Or for the dreams I bring to you.
Love me simply, nothing more,
Love me for the love I pour.
Nothing more, nothing less, just a heart open wide,
Where love can reside, without reason or pride.

Love for just the sake of love, I pray,
And nothing else, come what may.
I don’t ask you to love everyday,
Love me tomorrow, and just today.

poem1_theme: This poem is about the fear that follows you once you fall irrevocably in love. It stems not from lack of trust or faith in your bond, rather from something very trivial, yet much scarier. It's when you overthink everything, question yourself, your faults and shortcomings. Knowing this person chose you, knowing you're not perfect, but loving them so much and wanting nothing less than perfection for them, despite the fact that they love you equally as much as you do.
poem1_content:
And would you care for all the knives, 
All the daggers and the snares? 
Would you care for the moon I wish to see, 
And the stars, to wish upon I'd never dare?

Would you dare to stay when I say, 
You are my only muse? 
And would you consider it love, still,
When I say that I would worship your very existence, 
And that I'd call it religion if your love would be my only sin? 

Would it still be a manic delusion, 
To want you and hide you in the stars forever? 
Or can we just call it love,
For a little while longer?

poem1_title: The coldness which makes you bleed
poem1_content:
Do you know how it feels?
When someone has to sacrifice their everything,
In order to protect your dreams?
To Protect you from a side which is heavy and dark?
and from the battles they are fighting to protect your spark?
To Protect you from the weight which is not yours to carry?
and the unnecessary things for which you don't need to say sorry?
Do you know?
How it feels like a slow poison,
When the only person I feel safe with,
Decides to keep a distance,
In the pursuit of creating a safe space!
Without understanding that,
Protecting someone doesn't mean fixing yourself,
Rather, just being present for someone, in such times, does all the help!
Do you know how heartbreaking it is?
When I fail to convey that,
I have to protect you with all my faith,
Because you chose not to honour your vow of keeping me safe! 
And when i say,
I want you to be my protector,
Does it not explain, that you truly matter?
Will you ever have an idea of the lifelong impression your actions left?
The idea of feeling unsafe, since your silent protection was not enough to leave me feeling continuously bled?
The idea of feeling unwanted, even though i am the only one you have ever wanted?
The idea of feeling cold when i only needed you to make me feel warm and safeguarded?

poem1_theme: Love, relationships, heartbreak, or a blend of these.
poem1_content:
Tushar met Naimisha in college, during a boring lecture. She pretended to enjoy the lecture just to sit beside him. She evnetually proposed him one rainy evening, trembling but certain, saying she had never felt this way before. After that, love arrived in floods. She texted him the moment she woke up and again before sleep. She skipped classes to wait outside his lab, tagged him in posts about soulmates, and told friends—and strangers—that she would marry him someday. He felt chosen.
They married young. For the first few months, life felt steady—shared dinners, lazy Sundays, soft laughter, plans for the future. Then the cracks spoke. He found pictures of boys he didn’t recognize in her phone gallery, WhatsApp messages depicting the same love bombing to other guys similar to what he used to get from her in college. When he confronted her, she cried, said it was a mistake, promised it would never happen again. There was drama, apologies, broken trust patched with hope. 
He forgave her, though his heart stayed heavy with suspicion. He tried to stay and believe. 
But the truth has a way of repeating itself. Time passed. Then it happened again.
Tushar found more messages, another name. This time she didn’t cry. She told Tushar that he was just another guy for whom she had fallen for in college. He only got lucky because her parents came to know about their relationship in college and so she had to marry him. That’s all. If he wanted to stay, he could. If not, he should leave. “I’m like this only,” she said, flat and final.
Something broke inside him.
He just left and filed for divorce, choosing loneliness over a love that had once been too loud—and ended unbearably empty.

Oh soul, of endless deep desires, 
How long have you been waiting for the peace?

You keep walking with the little torch light ,
Like a guard watching through the night.

You search for peace in streets of wrong,
 In a world where crime and pain belong, 

Where hearts have sealed and love is rare, 
Where humans walk with no true care.

O gentle soul, now change your way, 
This little world won't let you pray.
Unlock your heart, let guidance rise, A light travelling from the skies.

Embrace the truth, let faith increase, 
And you shall find eternal peace.
poem2_content:
One who was lost can never be forgotten.
But the remembrance of the one can be painful often.
Searching for vanished can never be helpful. 
Not less than a delusion to be truthful.
Waiting in a que of lost and found,
Returning empty-handed regret can be profound.
Left with the last path of praying for their return,
Is undoable, but something miraculous may take a turn.
Leaving the world apart,
isn't the solution to this start.
One lost, another found,
Resulting alternative as an end.

poem1_theme: An unyielding love that endures separation, with nature standing as the silent witness to its pain and persistence.
poem1_content:
ಹಕ್ಕಿಗಳ ಚಿಲಿಪಿಲಿ ಗಾಯನ ವೈಭವ ಲಹರಿ 
ಸೂರ್ಯನು ಆಗಮಿಸಿದ ಬೆಟ್ಟಗಳ ಮರೆಯಲ್ಲಿ...!

ಹೇಗೆ ವರ್ಣಿಸಲಿ ಅವಳನ್ನ ನೋಡಿದ ಆ ಮಧುರ ಕ್ಷಣ
ಕಳೆದು ಹೋಗಿರುವೆ ಆ ನಿನ್ನ ಸವಿ ನೆನಪಿನಲ್ಲಿ ನಾ
ಸಮಯವೇ ನಿಂತಂತಾಯಿತು ನಿನ್ನ ನೋಡಲು ನಾ...!

ಹೇಗೆ ವರ್ಣಿಸಲಿ ನಿನ್ನ ರೂಪವ ಸೌಂದರ್ಯದೇವತೆ 
ನಿನ್ನ ನಡಿಗೆಯು ಆಡಂಬರದ ಹಂದರದಂತೆ
ನಿನ್ನ ಕೇಶರಾಶಿಯ ಜಾಲದಲ್ಲಿ ನನ್ನ ಸಿಲುಕಿಸಿದಾತೆ
ನಿನ್ನ ನಗುವಿನ ಸವಿ ಶೃತಿಯಲ್ಲಿ ನನ್ನ ಕಳೆದುಹಾಕಿದಾತೆ
ನಿನ್ನ ಕೋಗಿಲೆ ಕಂಠದ ಧ್ವನಿಯಲ್ಲಿ ನನ್ನ ಮೋಹಮಾಡಿದಳಾತೆ 
ನಿನ್ನ ಕಣ್ಣಿನ ಪ್ರಪಂಚದಲ್ಲಿ ನನ್ನನ್ನು ಬಂಧಿಸಿದಳಾತೆ
ನಕ್ಷತ್ರ ಮತ್ತು ಪ್ರಕೃತಿಯ ಸುಂದರ ಹೊದಿಕೆ ನಿನ್ನ ಮೇಲೆ...!

ಆಕಾಶದಿ‌ ಮೋಡಗಳ ಮರೆಯಿಂದ ಹನಿಗಳ ಸಂಗೀತ
ತಂಗಾಳಿಗೆ ನಾಚಿ ನೀರಾಗಿ ವೃಕ್ಷಗಳು ಕುಣಿಯುತ್ತ
ಬರಮಾಡಿದೆ ಪ್ರಕೃತಿಯು ನನ್ನ ದೇವತೆಯನ್ನ
ಆದರೂ ಅವಳು ಮಾತನಾಡದೆ ಬಿಟ್ಟು ಹೋದಳು ನನ್ನ
ದಶಕಗಳಾದರು ಕಾಯುತ್ತೀನಿ ಎನ್ನುವ ಹಠ ನನ್ನ...!

poem1_theme: A reflection on love as a continuous choice shaped by effort, vulnerability, distance, miscommunication, and quiet adjustment. The poem explores how love feels to someone experiencing deep care for the first time, balancing fear and reassurance while choosing connection again and again.
poem1_content:
Love is choosing you again and again,
even on quiet days and even in pain.
Love is choosing you again and again,
trying, adjusting, over and again.

It’s the effort no one really sees...
holding words back to keep the peace.
For a heart that never felt this care,
it feels unreal… like, is this fair?

Sometimes it feels like you’re my whole world,
not to own just a safe place to curl.
I overthink, then let things slide,
just so no storms grow inside.

There’s this fear you never say out loud...
what if they leave? it hangs like a cloud.
Texts misunderstood, feelings delayed,
distance between every word we say.

No calls, just messages through,
but still… every effort says,
“I’m choosing you.”

Love is choosing you...
even when it’s messy and never getting tired of choosing.

Haaye mere dost…
Hamne kabhi tere baare mein likha tha 
ki tum kitni pyaari lagti ho,
kitni pyaari dikhti ho,
aur kitni pyaari ho…
Aur aaj bhi likh rahi hoon,

Ki tab tum itni pyaari lagti thi,
kitni pyaari dikhti thi,
kitni pyaari thi.

Roz mera phone bajta tha 
subah, shaam, Har din, Har waqt
tera hi message ka intezaar  tha.

Aisa lagta tha ki
hum dost na ho,
Ye kabhi ho hi nahi sakta.
Par aaj to tum nazar bhi nahi aati ho

Ab mera phone
tera message se nahi bajta.
Phir bhi main intezaar karti gayi

Hafte guzarte gaye,
mahine beet gaye,
aur ab toh 3 saal ho chuke hain, Yaar!

Mujhe lagta hai ki
shayad tere naye dost ban gaye honge…
Aur haan, khush hoon main
ki tu kahin toh has rahi hogi unke saath. Par mujse nahi

Abhi  bhi likha karti hoon tere baare mein.
Bohot accha likhti hoon.
Kabhi rokar , kabhi udaas hokar likhti hoon.
Bas kabhi khush hokar nahi
Yaad aati hai teri 
Aaj kuch jyada agayi toh likh diya tere bareme

poem1_theme: This poem carries a theme of emotional self-protection shaped by past pain.  At its core, the dominant emotions are sorrow, longing, and guarded vulnerability. The speaker feels the weight of growing older and accumulating hurt, especially in love. There’s a quiet ache for reciprocal love—wanting to be loved with the same depth and sacrifice they give—but also a deep fear of being wounded again.  The closing lines shift from longing to self-chosen solitude. Being alone isn’t portrayed as bitterness; it’s a defensive sanctuary. The “fortress of my heart” symbolizes emotional boundaries built after disappointment—strength, caution, and survival.
poem1_content:
The older I grow,
The deeper my sorrow flows.
Can anyone love me the way I give, the price I pay?

Not all are alarming,
Nor are they truly calming.
Yet I sit in quiet wonder,
Seeking someone to still this thunder.

In the battles of love and time, I guard my heart, drawing the line.
A mistake once made
Doesn't mean I'll let it fade.

For this is why I choose my own zone, Preferring always to remain alone.
No one may enter, no one may brainstorm, In the fortress of my heart, safe and warm.
poem2_theme: This poem is driven by betrayal and disillusionment, with strong undercurrents of grief, hurt, and hard-earned clarity.  The core emotion is the pain of being falsely blamed by someone trusted. The speaker loved and protected this person, seeing them as a safe emotional space, only to be sacrificed for the other person’s self-preservation. There’s a quiet devastation in realizing that loyalty was one-sided.  As the poem progresses, the emotion shifts from raw hurt to awakening and emotional resolve. The line “I was a loss she’d never gain” marks a moment of self-recognition—the speaker understands their own worth, even though it comes through suffering.
poem2_content:
She placed the blame on wounds untrue, For things I never thought to do.
To save her face, her fragile name, 
She let me burn in silent shame.

I held her close, my safest place, 
My quiet truth, my steady grace.
But when the world refused her plea, 
She aimed the hurt directly me.

That's when I learned, through breaking pain, 
I was a loss she'd never gain.
The word "friend" fell, empty, used,
A promise torn, a bond abused.

poem1_content:
In fields of thought, where stars ignite,
A love unfolds, a pure delight.
With gentle touch and whispered word,
A symphony of hearts is heard.

Through sunlit days and moonlit nights,
Love's flame it dances, shining bright.
A bond of souls, forever near,
Dispelling shadows, calming fear.

So let us cherish, hand in hand,
This precious gift, across the land.
For in love's grace, we find our way,
A brighter dawn, a brand new day.

poem1_theme: The theme of "Silent Suffocation" is grief and the silent, breathless pain of losing a lover. It explores how memories linger despite attempts to bury them, how silence itself feels suffocating, and how love remains even after death.
poem1_content:
Silent Suffocation

I tried to bury your memories  
In the deepest corners of the universe.  
I tried to hold my breath,  
Contain myself among the living,  
While silence pressed against my lungs.  

I locked my heart, teaching it  
To surrender, to move on.  
Yet even then, the air of forgetting  
Lasted only for hours.  

Back in my room,  
Your memories clouded my mind,  
And cuddled up in my eyes,  
Falling gently across my face  
Like the weight of unspoken words.  

You may have forgotten me,  
Lost among your stars,  
Or perhaps guiding me through them.  
Yet no matter what,  
You remain—  
a breath I cannot release,  
a precious part of my life.
poem2_theme: The theme of Judged is misunderstanding and unseen struggles — how people judge outwardly without knowing the hidden battles of anxiety, exhaustion, and pain.    The emotion is a mix of defiance and vulnerability — it carries anger at being misjudged, sorrow from silent suffering, and strength in reclaiming your truth.
poem2_content:
Judged

They whispered complaints about my productivity,  
While I wrestled with sleepless galaxies  
And paralysis shadows in the eclipsed night sky.  

They questioned the strength of my confidence,  
While I trembled beneath storms of anxiety,  
And panic attacks that thundered like meteors.  

They branded me lazy,  
While I labored until my ankle swelled—  
Red, burning like a star collapsing.  

They never traced my constellation of pain,  
Yet still… they judged me.

poem1_theme: Emotional confusion regarding the dark circles
poem1_content:
The depth of grief 
Maybe 
Circle or wounds 

Stories to tell 
Or maybe 
Dead cells and unheeding ruin 

Ohh! To be alive while being dead
The crochet of wrinkles and eyes absolutely red 

Fluid of calmness , relief for sure 
Or 
Fluid of regret , pain and sore 

The end might decide is this this or that 
Till then it's just an undenied patch

तुम्हारे हर लफ्ज़ को पहली ओस की तरह सजा रखा है 
तुम्हारे आने से पहले ही खत खतों को रोज नज़र कर सोती हुं 
कुछ इस तरह इन खतों के साथ तुम्हारा इंतज़ार रहता है 
इन सिले लबों पर वो बात नहीं आती 
यूं ही नहीं मेरे हर शब ऐसी ही गुज़र जाती हैं 
तुम्हारे इंतजार मे, तुम्हारे ख्याल में 
जाने वो भोर की शाम कब आयेगी 
जब ये जज़्बात इन लबों पर आयेंगे 
जब शब - ए - तसव्वुर सच हो जायेगी 
जब काव्या अपनी मोहब्बत को अपना बोल पाएगी 
इन सर्द फिजाओं में ये सब तुम तक पहुंच जायेंगे 
और तुम भी उसके अंदाज का इंतजार करोगे 
तुम भी उससे मोहब्बत का इज़हार करोगे।

You were the person
I thought I would never lose,
And here I am again,
Trying to console myself,
Convincing my heart
That you were just another lesson.
The memories still wander through my mind
Like an old movie,
All black and white,
But so thoughtful,
I sit in front of it,
Watching.
My heart stutters—
Whenever I see you look at me,
Whenever I see you smile at me.
So beautiful.
So lovely.
You were all the love I needed,
You were all the person I needed,
You were everything to me,
Like a baby to her mother.
Yet you ask me why I let go.
But the umbilical cord
That bonds a baby to her mother,
The cord, the lifeline,
Has to be cut
For the baby to come out and live.
And I?
I had to cut our connection
So that you could live happily.
Not out of hate,
But out of love.
Love like a mother loves her baby,
Gentle,
And fierce.
Love, love, love.
